meta.source: Foundry PF2e bestiary packs + curated seed
meta.notes: Augmented from core Foundry monster packs (max 5000).

creatures.name: Adept
creatures.description: Adepts have unlocked only the most minor of occult mysteries.

creatures.name: Animated Broom
creatures.description: Animated brooms perform menial tasks of cleaning and upkeep, but they can step in to defend a room from intrusion if needed.

creatures.name: Apothecary
creatures.description: The apothecary skillfully combines materials into unguents and medicines using crushed herbs, curative minerals, and potent extracts. The world is a dangerous place. Thankfully, there are those who devote their lives to easing the pain and suffering of others.

creatures.name: Apprentice
creatures.description: Ambitious apprentices can be found in all cities.

creatures.name: Barrister
creatures.description: Barristers may serve as criminal prosecutors or legal advocates, defending the rights of those accused of crimes or named as defendants in civil cases.

creatures.name: Beggar
creatures.description: Wherever there is poverty, there are beggars.

creatures.name: Bloodseeker
creatures.description: Scourges of swamps and damp, abandoned places, bloodseekers are ravenous blood drinkers.

creatures.name: Clockwork Spy
creatures.description: Engineers, technologists, and mechanically gifted wizards employ clockwork spies-tiny, spiderlike constructs capable of recording and playing back audio-to surreptitiously surveil their enemies or steal secrets from competitors.

creatures.name: Common Eurypterid
creatures.description: The smallest of the giant eurypterids can grow to about the size of a large dog.

creatures.name: Commoner
creatures.description: Many commoners live hard lives of toil as they work to keep their families fed and housed in relative comfort. Society is built upon the backs of laborers.

creatures.name: Compsognathus
creatures.description: The compsognathus is a small bipedal dinosaur that moves in swift, darting motions.

creatures.name: Court Historian
creatures.description: Amid the political machinations of the court stand the court historians, observing and recording events for future generations.

creatures.name: Crawling Hand
creatures.description: A crawling hand formed from the appendage of a Medium creature is quick and agile, skittering in the shadows until it can strike its prey.

creatures.name: Eagle
creatures.description: These large birds of prey swoop down from incredible heights to snatch fish and small mammals in their powerful talons. Eagles nest atop high trees or steep cliffs that provide a commanding view of the surrounding area. Few avian creatures can match the beauty and grace of the eagle.

creatures.name: Flash Beetle
creatures.description: These 3-foot-long insects boast a pair of glowing organs on the back of the abdomen that give off bright light and that continue to glow for days, even after the creature's death.

creatures.name: Giant Centipede
creatures.description: Most giant centipedes (known as sewer centipedes when found in cities) nest in small groups but hunt alone when they seek out food.

creatures.name: Giant Rat
creatures.description: Giant rats are enormous versions of the common vermin.

creatures.name: Gnome Philomath
creatures.description: The innate gnomish inclination to revel in new experiences leads gnome philomaths to delve as deeply as they can into the multitudes of knowledge contained within a library.

creatures.name: Goblin Warrior
creatures.description: The frontline fighters of goblin tribes prefer to fight in large groups—especially when they can outnumber their foes at least three to one.

creatures.name: Grimple
creatures.description: Even more than most gremlins, grimples resent the trappings of civilization: inn common rooms with their rowdy singalongs, livery yards with their whinnying horses, church steeples with their clanging bells.

creatures.name: Guard Dog
creatures.description: The typical guard dog is loyal and beloved by many communities.

creatures.name: Gutter Ooze
creatures.description: These small masses of protoplasm are considered the lesser "cousins" to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Sewer Ooze]{sewer oozes}, emerging from the drainage culverts beneath a city after a rainfall to feed on bits of organic matter that collect in streetside gutters.

creatures.name: Halfling Street Watcher
creatures.description: Halflings' keen sight makes them excellent street watchers.

creatures.name: Haniver
creatures.description: Hanivers are the most benign type of gremlin—capricious fey who skim the waves on rubbery fin-wings.

creatures.name: Harrow Reader
creatures.description: A harrow deck is a set of 54 cards with symbolic illustrations that serves as a sacred divining tool.

creatures.name: Judge
creatures.description: Properly exercised, the duties of a judge include strict adherence to the law regardless of station, with minimal sentimentality.

creatures.name: Kobold Warrior
creatures.description: The typical kobold trains in tunnel fighting, learning to use simple weapons that are effective in narrow spaces.

creatures.name: Librarian
creatures.description: Keepers of knowledge, librarians document discoveries, events, and laws.

creatures.name: Merchant
creatures.description: Small settlements can typically support one or two generalist merchants, and larger cities house multiple specialists—experts in one type of product.

creatures.name: Mitflit
creatures.description: Mitflits are self-loathing and pitiful cowards, easily bullied into servitude by other creatures or even slightly more powerful mitflit leaders.

creatures.name: Monkey
creatures.description: When these small, curious primates come in contact with large humanoid populations, they quickly adapt, learning to steal food or small objects before retreating to the nearest tree branch with their prize.

creatures.name: Nyktera
creatures.description: Nykteras, or bat sprites, are gentle sprites known for their affectionate nature and incredible hospitality.

creatures.name: Obsessive Researcher
creatures.description: Academia rewards specialization, and so the universities and athenaeums of the Inner Sea are full to the brim of sunlight-deprived scholars who are the world experts in such obscure topics such as the migratory habits of bogwids or the folklore of pre-Choral Brevoy.

creatures.name: Phantasmal Minion
creatures.description: This creature can be summoned with the spell @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Phantasmal Minion].

creatures.name: Physician
creatures.description: The art of medicine is a blend of the intellectual and the practical, concerned with how diseases work and how to prevent them.

creatures.name: Pilgrim of Irori
creatures.description: Pilgrims traverse the lands spurred on by some holy reason, be it to visit a sacred place or follow a prophetic vision. Religions inspire devout individuals to uphold their tenets.

creatures.name: Pufferfish
creatures.description: The pufferfish defends itself by filling its flexible body with air or water to extend a number of spikes that otherwise lie flat against its skin.

creatures.name: Red Fox
creatures.description: The shy and perceptive red fox has uncanny adaptability and is just as comfortable in urban areas as in the wilds.

creatures.name: Root Leshy Groundskeeper
creatures.description: Hardy root leshies have the strength and stamina to dig furrows for planting seeds over long hours.

creatures.name: Servant
creatures.description: A servant might be a maid or butler, keeping a home in working order, or a server in an establishment like an inn, taking orders and serving customers. Society is built upon the backs of laborers.

creatures.name: Severed Head
creatures.description: The most common beheaded appears simply as a rotting head, barely preserved by the magic that created it. Beheaded are the reanimated heads of decapitation victims. These mindless undead fly through the air or roll around to attack their prey.

creatures.name: Skeleton Guard
creatures.description: The most common skeletal minions are mere guardians. Animated skeletons are among the most common types of undead.

creatures.name: Skunk
creatures.description: These omnivores are most active at twilight, feeding on everything from berries to grubs and insects.

creatures.name: Snapping Turtle
creatures.description: Snapping turtles are freshwater reptiles with a long flexible neck, a sharp piercing beak, and a confrontational temperament that doesn't prevent some spellcasters from choosing these creatures as familiars. They weigh about 20 pounds and have shells around 18 inches in length.

creatures.name: Soulrider (Celestial)
creatures.description: Although soulriders can grow quite large, most of them are narrow creatures only a few feet long.

creatures.name: Soulrider (Fiend)
creatures.description: Although soulriders can grow quite large, most of them are narrow creatures only a few feet long.

creatures.name: Soulrider (Monitor)
creatures.description: Although soulriders can grow quite large, most of them are narrow creatures only a few feet long.

creatures.name: Sprigjack
creatures.description: Weaker twigjacks are often found in cultivated forests and even gardens.

creatures.name: Sprite
creatures.description: Common sprites, sometimes called firefly sprites, are primeval guardians that latch onto a person, place, or object and defend it for their own inscrutable reasons.

creatures.name: Tax Collector
creatures.description: Ledgers and marks, positive and negative flows, and levies and allotments are a tax collector's daily bread.

creatures.name: Teacher
creatures.description: The passing of knowledge and traditions from generation to generation is a time-honored occupation.

creatures.name: Three-Toed Sloth
creatures.description: The diminutive three-toed sloth is a solitary, tree-dwelling creature.

creatures.name: Tooth Fairy
creatures.description: Lone fairies usually need several minutes of elbow grease and a sleeping or restrained subject to extract a tooth.

creatures.name: Trained Raven
creatures.description: These omnivorous birds are cunning and opportunistic scavengers.

creatures.name: Trilobite
creatures.description: Creature imported from Foundry PF2e data.

creatures.name: Urchin
creatures.description: Although their escapades might be notorious, few urchins are thrill-seekers.

creatures.name: Viper
creatures.description: Each member of this family of venomous snakes has long, hinged fangs that inject potent venom into their prey.

creatures.name: Weasel
creatures.description: Despite their small stature, weasels are skilled hunters.

creatures.name: Yellow Musk Thrall
creatures.description: A creature transformed by a yellow musk creeper's tendrils boring into its brain becomes a yellow musk thrall if it's Small or Medium, or a yellow musk brute if Large (a Small creature becomes a Small thrall rather than a Medium one).

creatures.name: Zombie Shambler
creatures.description: A zombie shambler is a slow-moving horror dangerous in larger groups. A zombie's only desire is to consume the living. Unthinking and ever-shambling harbingers of death, zombies stop only when they're destroyed.

creatures.name: Air Wisp
creatures.description: Air wisps are floating spheres of cloud and storm, perpetually humming a light, whispery tone.

creatures.name: Aiuvarin Translator
creatures.description: Many societies recognize aiuvarins' skills as adept translators. Elves' long lives give them centuries to delve into studies, artistry, or exploration.
languages: two other common or uncommon languages

creatures.name: Azarketi Crab Catcher
creatures.description: The average azarketi citizen in Absalom makes a living fishing or catching crabs.

creatures.name: Badger
creatures.description: The typical badger has dark, brownish-gray fur highlighted with white markings, particularly on the head, giving it a striped mask of fur around its eyes. A threatened badger can swiftly become a ferocious combatant that typically fights until slain.

creatures.name: Blue-Ringed Octopus
creatures.description: While relatively small, the blue-ringed octopus is particularly venomous for its size.

creatures.name: Bone Scavenger
creatures.description: Kholos have a strong connection to their ancestors, often using their bones as art and weapons to further honor them.

creatures.name: Bottlenose Dolphin
creatures.description: The bottlenose dolphin is the most common and widespread species of dolphin.

creatures.name: Conspiracist
creatures.description: Conspiracists misinform and falsify facts to further their own causes. Though they pose little physical threat, conspiracists can have more powerful allies, such as a deluded mob that respond to the conspiracist's signal. Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: Dockhand
creatures.description: Working to load and unload cargo from ships, dockhands are considered unruly, but many stay focused and work hard until the job is done. Society is built upon the backs of laborers.

creatures.name: Dream Spider
creatures.description: A dream spider's webs have an iridescent hue and are infused with the same hallucinogenic compound as the creature's toxin.

creatures.name: Drover
creatures.description: Drovers specialize in moving herds of livestock over large distances. Society is built upon the backs of laborers.

creatures.name: Dwarf Smith
creatures.description: Many dwarves become smiths as their attention to detail, lifestyles that keep them close to useful materials such as iron, and a pride in their work all come together to become a brilliant skill set for working with armaments.

creatures.name: Earth Wisp
creatures.description: Earth wisps are rolling spheres of rock, mud, and leaves, perpetually humming with low, rumbling energy.

creatures.name: Envoy
creatures.description: Envoys are guests of a given court, representing the interests of another court or organization.

creatures.name: Farmer
creatures.description: Workers of the fields, vineyards, and orchards of the world, farmers are known for their rugged endurance and skill with plants and animals. Society is built upon the backs of laborers.

creatures.name: Fire Wisp
creatures.description: Fire wisps are sizzling spheres of flame and ash, perpetually crackling and shimmering with heat.

creatures.name: Fisher
creatures.description: More than just hobbyists, fishers catch fish and other seafood with the intention of selling most of or all of their catch. Society is built upon the backs of laborers.

creatures.name: Giant Maggot
creatures.description: Giant flies lay their eggs on the bodies of larger monsters or livestock.

creatures.name: Grindylow
creatures.description: The top half of a grindylow vaguely resembles a goblin, but from the waist down, their bodies split into a tangle of suckered, wriggling tentacles.

creatures.name: Homunculus
creatures.description: A homunculus is a tiny servitor construct created by a crafter to serve as a spy, scout, messenger, or assistant.

creatures.name: Hryngar Sharpshooter
creatures.description: Hryngar sharpshooters serve both as ranged support for caravans and as snipers posted on guard towers overlooking quarries and other areas where workers toil away the hours.

creatures.name: Ioton
creatures.description: Periodically, stray thoughts colliding on the Astral Plane become entangled, forming a knot of rudimentary intellect.

creatures.name: Kangaroo
creatures.description: Kangaroos are marsupials distinguished by long faces, large ears, powerful back legs and tails, and a distinctive hopping gait.

creatures.name: Leaf Leshy
creatures.description: Leaf leshies are diminutive protectors of forests clad in armor and hats made of fruit, flowers, or leaves.

creatures.name: Mayor
creatures.description: The mayor is the political leader of a settlement.
languages: up to 2 additional languages spoken in their settlement

creatures.name: Melixie
creatures.description: Melixies are hyperactive insect sprites with legendary sweet tooths, capable of drinking down honey, nectar, sugar, and other sweets in alarming quantities.

creatures.name: Miner
creatures.description: Miners explore deep underground in search of minerals and rare ores, taking numerous precautions to keep themselves safe. Society is built upon the backs of laborers.

creatures.name: Orc Scrapper
creatures.description: Acting as the most common orc combat unit, orc scrappers are ferocious fighters who rely on unrelenting strength, rather than combat experience, to prove their mettle and attempt to rise in the hold's standing.

creatures.name: Ort
creatures.description: These shapeless masses of quivering flesh are the least of devilkind.

creatures.name: Penitent of Calistria
creatures.description: Atonement is seen as an ultimate act of supplication for those who have wronged their faith. Repentance takes several forms—acts of service, a pilgrimage, flagellation, or divine quests. Religions inspire devout individuals to uphold their tenets.

creatures.name: Political Upstart
creatures.description: Fiery and heady, a political upstart actively pushes against the status quo. These lone wolves have an aura of mystery, bravado, and swagger.

creatures.name: Poppet Attendant
creatures.description: Poppet attendants are among the most common form of poppets. Most attendants can be found in creative trades, especially among tailors and cobblers. Poppets are simple constructs made to assist their creators with basic tasks.

creatures.name: Prime Minister
creatures.description: The prime minister is the leader of a nation's bureaucracy.
languages: up to 3 additional languages spoken in their nation

creatures.name: Pugwampi
creatures.description: Mean, dog-faced, and craven, pugwampis take disproportionate enjoyment from the accidents and missteps of other creatures—something that happens often due to the supernatural aura of ill fortune these gremlins project.

creatures.name: Puppet
creatures.description: On the surface, puppeteers are simply providers of entertainment to the masses.

creatures.name: Riding Pony
creatures.description: Horses serve as mounts and beasts of burden in many societies.

creatures.name: Ringhorn Ram
creatures.description: Ringhorn rams, named for the massive, curving spirals protruding from their heads, travel the plains.

creatures.name: Sea Snake
creatures.description: These lithe snakes like to frequent the shallow waters of tropical seas.

creatures.name: Soulbound Homunculus
creatures.description: Most homunculi use a dose of their creator's blood as their spark of life, but it's possible to use a technique similar to that used in the crafting of a soulbound doll to give a homunculus a personality and the semblance of life.

creatures.name: Spear Frog
creatures.description: The spear frog is named for its toxin, which is traditionally used to envenom thrown projectiles like spears and daggers. Frogs that are poisonous or grow to monstrous size can be a menace to adventurers.

creatures.name: Spider Swarm
creatures.description: An abundance of food, the sudden hatching of a clutch of eggs, or magical influence can cause smaller spiders to gather in terrifying, deadly masses. Few everyday vermin inspire as much dread as the infamous spider.

creatures.name: Stingray
creatures.description: Found in salt or fresh water, stingrays are normally passive creatures, but when threatened or cornered, they lash out with their tails to lance foes with their toxic stingers.

creatures.name: Toady
creatures.description: These minions perform the thankless tasks that keep their master's vile machine running. Whether out of loyalty or fear, a toady serves their boss faithfully. Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.
languages: one additional language spoken by their boss

creatures.name: Torchbearer
creatures.description: Torchbearers carry light sources for seasoned explorers. Explorers are often well-equipped and well-trained for any type of hazard and are eager to lead others into the wild.

creatures.name: Vampire Squid
creatures.description: Vampire squid are neither undead nor blood drinkers. Their name is instead inspired by their red eyes and the dark, cloak-like webbing between their arms. Living in the lightless depths of the ocean, the vampire squid can eject a cloud of bioluminescent mucus that nauseates predators.

creatures.name: Vine Leshy
creatures.description: Irrepressible vine leshys love to explore and collect tales to share.
languages: speak with plants (plants with vines only)

creatures.name: Water Wisp
creatures.description: Water wisps are swimming spheres of foam and water, perpetually humming with a wash of soft sounds.

creatures.name: Wolf Skeleton
creatures.description: Without the burden of flesh, skeletal wolves charge across the battlefield with terrifying speed.

creatures.name: Abbot of Abadar
creatures.description: Abbots are often the heads of religious institutions less focused on spellcasting, such as orphanages, religious schools, or charities. Religions inspire devout individuals to uphold their tenets.

creatures.name: Acolyte of Iomedae
creatures.description: Underling clerics are still learning the tenets of their faith and answering to a superior priest. Their days are spent in devotion and learning, sequestered in temples. Religions inspire devout individuals to uphold their tenets.

creatures.name: Air Mephit
creatures.description: Air mephits are capricious and flighty relative to their kin; they are as likely to fly blindly into battle as they are to whine in terror at a loud noise.

creatures.name: Air Scamp
creatures.description: A pale blue head and paper-thin wings peek out from the cloud that clings to the air scamp.

creatures.name: Akata
creatures.description: An akata is a hairless, blue-skinned quadruped with fearsome jaws, glowing eyes, a pair of thin tails, and a mane of writhing tentacles.

creatures.name: Amoeba Swarm
creatures.description: An amoeba swarm consists of thousands of individual single-celled organisms held together by acrid-smelling slime. As ravenous as they are mindless, amoeba swarms use no tactics.

creatures.name: Animated Silverware Swarm
creatures.description: Nobles are known to pay great amounts to animate their silverware, both for ease in cleaning and to serve as novelties during dinner parties. Many animated objects have useful functions but become dangers when uncontrolled.

creatures.name: Arbiter
creatures.description: These spherical aeons are scouts and diplomats.

creatures.name: Augur
creatures.description: These spherical knots of sinewy muscle, serrated blades, and bloody metal are the most common velstracs on the Netherworld.

creatures.name: Bodyguard
creatures.description: Hired to protect someone famous or powerful, bodyguards use intimidation, quick wits, and martial skill to keep their charges safe.

creatures.name: Boggard Scout
creatures.description: Often tasked with patrolling the borders of their lands, boggard scouts learn to speak another language (typically Common) to deal with trespassers.

creatures.name: Brownie
creatures.description: Brownies make their homes in the trunks of hollow trees, small earthy burrows, and even under porches or within the crawl spaces of farmhouses.

creatures.name: Cacodaemon
creatures.description: These twisted embodiments of violence and spite are spawned from eddies of angry and warped souls amid Abaddon's mists.

creatures.name: Caligni Dancer
creatures.description: Caligni dancers serve as intermediaries between caligni factions, carrying messages and negotiating deals between the notoriously independent groups.

creatures.name: Camel
creatures.description: For generations untold, nomads and traders have relied on sure-footed camels to cross the harsh deserts and trackless wastes around the world.

creatures.name: Carbuncle
creatures.description: Never have legend and misinformation met upon a more inauspicious brow than that of the lowly carbuncle.

creatures.name: Cassisian
creatures.description: The weakest of angels, cassisians usually serve as lackey messengers for more powerful angels or as spiritual guides for mortals.

creatures.name: Catfolk Pouncer
creatures.description: Catfolk pouncers travel the world in search of new experiences.

creatures.name: Cave Scorpion
creatures.description: Cave scorpions prefer to reside in underground lairs.

creatures.name: Combat Engineer
creatures.description: Be it a bridge to get a battalion across a river, watchtowers to pierce the fog of war, or fortifications to secure territory, armies have always needed those who can build.

creatures.name: Coral Capuchin
creatures.description: A coral capuchin resembles a hairless pink monkey with large eyes and batlike wings.

creatures.name: Cultist
creatures.description: Hidden secrets and occult powers have an irresistible lure for many. Since the majority of these NPCs are spellcasters, consider using alternative spell lists to adjust their themes.

creatures.name: Cunning Fox
creatures.description: Spirit guides of cunning are often seen as the weakest of the spirit guides, but they're also the most numerous and proactive, guiding families to safe paths or leaving food and water for warriors.

creatures.name: Cythnigot
creatures.description: The cythnigot is a foul fungal parasite that grows and thrives in the corpses of small creatures.

creatures.name: Dancer
creatures.description: Dance can be used to tell stories, share emotions, provide entertainment, and display a performer's athletic ability. Performances come in a wide variety of forms, from musical methods like singing and instruments to physical dancing and juggling to simple orating and conversing.

creatures.name: Deep One
creatures.description: An average mature deep one weighs 300 pounds and measures 7 feet in length, though they appear shorter on land due to their wide stance and natural hunch.

creatures.name: Deep One Hybrid
creatures.description: Whether from their devout faith to Dagon or their own biological lineage, some humans display physical indicators of their connection to deep ones.

creatures.name: Doru
creatures.description: These deceivers whisper fetid lies, sowing dark and dread inspiration into foolish mortal ears.

creatures.name: Drow Fighter
creatures.description: Drow fighters train to master techniques that inflict deep, bleeding wounds and use poisoned crossbow bolts to exhaust enemies.

creatures.name: Druid Initiate
creatures.description: A neophyte druid learns basic techniques and spellcasting soon after being initiated into their druidic order.

creatures.name: Dust Mephit
creatures.description: Whining and self-important creatures, dust mephits are known for their tendency to pronounce doom and gloom at every opportunity, lamenting every insignificant and minor difficulty as though it were an insurmountable obstacle and making themselves out to be heroes for struggling valiantly against t…

creatures.name: Dwarf Warrior
creatures.description: Between their finely crafted equipment and natural bravery, a dwarf warrior can hold their ground against mighty foes.

creatures.name: Earth Mephit
creatures.description: Earth mephits are humorless and trudge about their tasks with little enthusiasm.

creatures.name: Earth Scamp
creatures.description: An earth scamp's rocky exterior could camouflage it well among loose rocks if not for the scamp's large eyes and bat-like wings.

creatures.name: Electric Eel
creatures.description: Usually found in freshwater rivers and lakes, an electric eel is not particularly aggressive, but its ability to stun predators and prey alike can be dangerous to larger creatures searching for their next meal.

creatures.name: Elf Ranger
creatures.description: Many elves learn the bow before even picking up their first blade. Because of this, many elves act as rangers at some point in their long lives. Elves are mysterious and intelligent, and graceful and cunning in battle.

creatures.name: Esipil
creatures.description: Among the least of the sahkils, esipils delight in spreading fears and uncertainty among people who live with animals that could turn on them.

creatures.name: Festrog
creatures.description: Spawned from corpses of those who died of illness or starvation and were twisted by void energy, festrogs display a viciousness that rivals other undead.

creatures.name: Fetchling Scout
creatures.description: Fetchling scouts patrol the outskirts of their communities, looking for any possible threats.

creatures.name: Fire Mephit
creatures.description: Fire mephits are conniving and quick to anger.

creatures.name: Fire Scamp
creatures.description: Although arguably quite friendly, fire scamps are considered far more dangerous than their kin.

creatures.name: Flumph
creatures.description: Even among the numerous types of tentacled aberrations populating the lands of Golarion, flumphs are an anomaly.

creatures.name: Forager
creatures.description: Foragers know the areas they live and work in like no other.

creatures.name: Fuath
creatures.description: Despite their small size, fuaths are shipwreckers incarnate.

creatures.name: Gathlain Wanderer
creatures.description: Gathlains have made a name for themselves as inquisitive and mischievous, as well as flighty and distractable.

creatures.name: Ghoul
creatures.description: Ghouls are ravenous undead who haunt graveyards and eat corpses. Few creatures are more ubiquitous to sinister locations such as lonely graveyards and ruined crypts than the flesh-eating undead known as ghouls.

creatures.name: Ghoul Stalker
creatures.description: Ghouls stalkers are ravenous undead who haunt graveyards and eat corpses. Few creatures are more ubiquitous to sinister locations such as lonely graveyards and ruined crypts than the flesh-eating undead known as ghouls.

creatures.name: Giant Amoeba
creatures.description: These blobs of nearly transparent protoplasm are identical in form and behavior to the microscopic creatures from which they have evolved, except their outlandish size makes them all the more dangerous.

creatures.name: Giant Cockroach
creatures.description: Cockroaches are communal creatures, rarely setting out on their own unless they are searching for food. If an adventurer encounters a single giant cockroach while exploring, they had best take precautions against others, as there is likely an entire colony nearby.

creatures.name: Giant Fly
creatures.description: This massive fly's twitching limbs seem to never stop moving as it constantly cleans itself and scoops anything edible into its mouthparts.

creatures.name: Giant Frog
creatures.description: Giant frogs can grow up to 6 feet long and weigh over 200 pounds, with rows of razor-sharp teeth lining their gaping mouths. Frogs that are poisonous or grow to monstrous size can be a menace to adventurers.

creatures.name: Giant Gecko
creatures.description: These roughly human-sized reptiles have amazing feet capable of clinging tenaciously to any surface.

creatures.name: Giant Skunk
creatures.description: Giant skunks thrive in places where their larger size and more potent musk allow them to fend off predators their smaller cousins can't.

creatures.name: Giant Solifugid
creatures.description: The smallest of giant solifugids are still large enough to harry desert traders and their mounts.

creatures.name: Giant Tick
creatures.description: This grotesque tick is the size of a dog and scurries with surprising speed.

creatures.name: Gnome Bard
creatures.description: Being a bard allows a gnome a wonderful excuse to constantly travel and see new places while simultaneously creating wonderful things.

creatures.name: Goblin Chef
creatures.description: Goblins eat almost anything. Though they'll survive on raw or little-cooked meat, some prefer searing or frying their food first—or better yet, pickling it! A goblin chef can make anything edible (at least to a goblin). Goblins can be found across Golarion, sometimes threatening taller humanoids (w…

creatures.name: Goblin Commando
creatures.description: The theoretical leaders of goblin raids are called goblin commandos.

creatures.name: Goblin Dog
creatures.description: Goblins' eponymous pets aren't true canines at all but rather large, blunt-nosed rodents with thin bodies and long legs.

creatures.name: Goblin Pyro
creatures.description: Some goblins take their people's admiration of fire fully into the realm of deadly obsession.

creatures.name: Goblin Snake
creatures.description: Goblin snakes sometimes train snakes as pets or companions. A goblin snake is a foul-smelling serpentine creature covered in greasy black scales and sporting a fanged, lipless head resembling that of a goblin.

creatures.name: Goblin War Chanter
creatures.description: While all goblins enjoy singing, goblin war chanters pride themselves on mastering the art of vocal performance.

creatures.name: Gourd Leshy
creatures.description: Gourd leshies are guardians of fields, gardens, and farms.

creatures.name: Grave Robber
creatures.description: Many cultures have a tradition of burying their dead with a selection of the deceased's most precious possessions. Such valuables can be easy pickings for those with no respect for—or fear of—the dead. In the underbelly of society, the lawless reign supreme.

creatures.name: Gravedigger
creatures.description: An often-overlooked group of laborers, gravediggers have a reputation for being as quiet and grim as their workplace.

creatures.name: Grig
creatures.description: Grigs are kindly musicians of the fey, often getting themselves into trouble due to their penchant for confronting evil well beyond their ability to vanquish.

creatures.name: Grioth Scout
creatures.description: The first grioths encountered on a new world are scouts.

creatures.name: Grippli Scout
creatures.description: Gripplis make their homes in the treetops of tropical jungles and forests.

creatures.name: Guard
creatures.description: Guards are rank-and-file members of a town watch or city guard, trained to look for trouble, take down criminals, and follow orders.

creatures.name: Gunsmith
creatures.description: Even though every gunslinger learns the basics of maintaining and repairing firearms as a part of their training, few would question the expertise of a master gunsmith regarding the weapons by which they live and die.

creatures.name: Halfling Troublemaker
creatures.description: A halfling's bravado can sometimes lead them into trouble.

creatures.name: Hippocampus
creatures.description: Hippocampi serve as steeds for aquatic humanoids or roam wild in the sea.

creatures.name: Hobgoblin Soldier
creatures.description: Soldiers make up the bulk of hobgoblin society, whether that society is a village or a military unit.

creatures.name: House Drake
creatures.description: Beautifully colored in purples and electric blues, the little dragonets called house drakes are genuinely brilliant in more ways than one.

creatures.name: Hryngar Bombardier
creatures.description: Hryngar chemists have a knack for creating alchemical grenades.

creatures.name: Hunting Spider
creatures.description: Hunting spiders are the most common type of giant spider, though not the largest. Few everyday vermin inspire as much dread as the infamous spider.

creatures.name: Hyena
creatures.description: Hyenas are cunning, powerfully built carnivores that bear a heavy resemblance to dogs and other canines, though they are not themselves canines.

creatures.name: Ice Mephit
creatures.description: Though capable of the same depths of cruelty as their fire mephit kin, ice mephits carry theirs out in a more calculating way.

creatures.name: Imp
creatures.description: Imps are fiendish infiltrators and corrupters who, despite their diminutive stature, are more than capable of subtly influencing a weak-willed individual into performing increasingly evil acts over time.

creatures.name: Innkeeper
creatures.description: The sight of an inn is a welcome one to any weary traveler.

creatures.name: Jinkin
creatures.description: Jinkins are sadistic tinkers who steal and sabotage items and take great pride in their power to curse precious objects.

creatures.name: Kholo Pragmatist
creatures.description: Due to their aggressive lifestyle, kholo warriors rarely live to an old age.

creatures.name: Kobold Scout
creatures.description: Most kobolds encountered outside of a well-defended warren or lair are kobold scouts, those trained for stalking and hunting.

creatures.name: Lantern Archon
creatures.description: Friendly, curious, and eternally optimistic, lantern archons embody the virtue of hope.

creatures.name: Lizardfolk Defender
creatures.description: A lizardfolk defender serves as a protector of the young, guardian of the settlement, and when no other options are available, a soldier in times of war.

creatures.name: Lizardfolk Scout
creatures.description: Iruxi scouts are the lizardfolk most often encountered by adventurers, for these unsentimental explorers and hunters spend most of their lives on the move, constantly patrolling the territories held by their enclave.

creatures.name: Local Herbalist
creatures.description: Local herbalists use their understanding of the natural world to heal and restore balance. Most join a secret lodge that teaches these ancient arts. The world is a dangerous place. Thankfully, there are those who devote their lives to easing the pain and suffering of others.

creatures.name: Lyrakien
creatures.description: Lyrakiens are musical messengers and embodiments of free travel.

creatures.name: Manta Ray
creatures.description: While manta rays might appear daunting, they are generally docile.

creatures.name: Mechanic
creatures.description: No one knows how to get the most out of their equipment better than a mechanic.

creatures.name: Merfolk Warrior
creatures.description: Merfolk warriors form the bulk of the militias of their vast underwater realms and meet potential aggressors head-on with uncompromising force.

creatures.name: Messenger
creatures.description: When a message, mail, or package needs to be delivered, messengers make deliveries—typically from large towns and cities or to other towns and cities. Society is built upon the backs of laborers.

creatures.name: Naari Pyrochemist
creatures.description: Naaris' blood blazes with elemental fire. On the Plane of Fire, most naaris are second-class citizens who serve under the iron heel of the ifrits and their Dominion of Flame, but those born outside the ifrit hierarchy or who choose to flee it live lives of passion in search of fame, glory, and powe…

creatures.name: Naiad
creatures.description: Naiads protect streams, ponds, springs, and other natural bodies of fresh water.

creatures.name: Nixie
creatures.description: These aquatic fey often guard ponds, rivers, lakes, and springs, protecting their bucolic homes from the advances of predators and careless settlers alike.

creatures.name: Nosoi
creatures.description: A nosoi resembles a whippoorwill, sparrow, or other small bird wearing a heavy leather plague doctor's mask.

creatures.name: Ooze Mephit
creatures.description: Slick in every sense of the word, ooze mephits are unapologetic sycophants, shamelessly flattering anyone they may be able to con a favor out of, although their lack of intelligence and subtlety means that only the most vain or trusting targets fail to see through their honeyed words.

creatures.name: Orc Agriculturist
creatures.description: In the severe lands orcs occupy, there are no lush fields blooming with crops.

creatures.name: Orc Veteran
creatures.description: Orc veterans have survived several bloody and chaotic conflicts, coming out the other side with scars and experience that make them even more dangerous opponents.

creatures.name: Oread Guard
creatures.description: Elemental earth laces through the bones of oreads, who appear similar to stone statues of their mortal ancestry, with delicate crystals in place of hair, fur, or scales.

creatures.name: Ostovite
creatures.description: Skittering scavenger vermin common throughout the Abyss, ostovites roam battlefields to harvest flesh and bones.

creatures.name: Plague Zombie
creatures.description: Plague zombies are infested with horrible contagions. A zombie's only desire is to consume the living. Unthinking and ever-shambling harbingers of death, zombies stop only when they're destroyed.

creatures.name: Prisoner
creatures.description: Most who end up in a jail, dungeon, or prison are just biding their time until their imprisonment ends, trying to find ways to make it through interminable days of boredom and deprivation.

creatures.name: Python
creatures.description: This nonvenomous family of snakes is rarely a threat to anything but small mammals and birds, hunting by coiling around prey and crushing victims with their powerful muscles.

creatures.name: Quasit
creatures.description: Unlike other demons, quasits are formed when a mortal spellcaster sheds a portion of their own sinful soul to create a familiar or companion.

creatures.name: Raktavarna
creatures.description: Raktavarnas are simple rakshasa incarnations of betrayal and assassination, often existing to test the mettle of those who were treacherous or poisonous in a previous life.

creatures.name: Rat Swarm
creatures.description: A swarm of rats can cause total chaos within a household or business.

creatures.name: Reef Octopus
creatures.description: The common reef octopus is a risky but valuable catch for coastal fishers.

creatures.name: Reefclaw
creatures.description: Reefclaws are aquatic monsters that resemble huge shrimp or lobsters.

creatures.name: Riding Dog
creatures.description: Riding dogs types include larger dogs, such as mastiffs, wolfhounds, and huskies, and are bred often by halflings and gnomes to serve as mounts.

creatures.name: Riding Horse
creatures.description: Horses serve as mounts and beasts of burden in many societies.

creatures.name: Rigger
creatures.description: A knack for knots and no fear of heights are the prime qualifications of these high-flying rope wranglers and lookouts. Adventurers may need passage on a swift vessel, or they might face danger from raiders at sea or in coastal settlements.

creatures.name: Samsaran Anchorite
creatures.description: A unique connection to the cycle of life and death defines the mortal lives of samsarans.

creatures.name: Sewer Ooze
creatures.description: These amorphous masses of sewage and other detritus make their way through filthy culverts beneath cities large and small. Slimes, molds, and other oozes can be found in dank dungeons and shadowed forests. While not necessarily evil, some grow to enormous sizes and have insatiable appetites.

creatures.name: Shade (Abaddon)
creatures.description: The hunted appear as Medium-sized versions of themselves from their prior lives, and they exist to be pursued and consumed by daemons.

creatures.name: Shade (Astral Plane)
creatures.description: These appear as astrally projected versions of their mortal forms, yet they lack a silver cord.
languages: any one spoken in life (such as common)

creatures.name: Shade (Axis)
creatures.description: The remade appear as parchment-skinned humanoids covered in lines of close black script.

creatures.name: Shade (Boneyard)
creatures.description: The dead appear as animated skeletons of the type of creature they were when they were alive.

creatures.name: Shade (Creation's Forge)
creatures.description: The enlightened appear as diaphanous, radiant versions of their mortal selves.

creatures.name: Shade (Dead Vault)
creatures.description: The caged are sent to Rovagug's prison plane, and appear as chained versions of their mortal bodies, save that their faces are hideous insectile visages.

creatures.name: Shade (Dreamlands)
creatures.description: The dreamers appear as their ideal appearance, age, and gender from their mortal bodies.

creatures.name: Shade (Elysium)
creatures.description: The chosen appear as idealized versions of their mortal selves with glowing eyes.

creatures.name: Shade (Ethereal Plane)
creatures.description: The terrorized appear as sickly, haunted-looking versions of their mortal selves.
languages: any one spoken in life (such as common)

creatures.name: Shade (Heaven)
creatures.description: The elect have golden halos and ghostly wings, but they otherwise appear as their mortal forms.

creatures.name: Shade (Hell)
creatures.description: The damned look like terribly scarred or mutilated versions of their mortal selves.

creatures.name: Shade (Maelstrom)
creatures.description: The shapeless appear as half-melted reflections of their former mortal selves.

creatures.name: Shade (Netherworld)
creatures.description: The mutilated appear as they did in their mortal lives, yet are covered in wounds or partially adorned or wrapped in chains.

creatures.name: Shade (Nirvana)
creatures.description: The cleansed take on animal forms that match their personalities in life.

creatures.name: Shade (Outer Rifts)
creatures.description: The larvae appear as maggot-like grubs with the face the shade had in life.

creatures.name: Shade (Plane of Air)
creatures.description: Air pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves composed of air.

creatures.name: Shade (Plane of Earth)
creatures.description: Earth pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves built of earth or stone.

creatures.name: Shade (Plane of Fire)
creatures.description: Fire pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves shaped from flame.

creatures.name: Shade (Plane of Metal)
creatures.description: Metal pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves made of various, often sharpened, metals.

creatures.name: Shade (Plane of Water)
creatures.description: Water pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves formed of flowing water.

creatures.name: Shade (Plane of Wood)
creatures.description: Wood pneuma appear as versions of their mortal selves carved from wood.

creatures.name: Shade (Universe)
creatures.description: The remnants are among the rarest of shades; appearing as plain, bland versions of their mortal selves.
languages: any one spoken in life (such as common)

creatures.name: Shikigami
creatures.description: Shikigami are made of paper and can easily transform between a flat cutout form and a three-dimensional doll of molded paper.

creatures.name: Shokasura
creatures.description: Shokasuras are tragic, wretched beings, broken by their despair at the cruel truths of reality.

creatures.name: Silvanshee
creatures.description: Silvanshees are a stealthy and extremely inquisitive contingent of agathions who gather information about the mortal world for other agathions.

creatures.name: Skulk
creatures.description: The cowardly and lazy skulks live on the fringes of organized societies and steal from others without a second thought.

creatures.name: Spawning Soulrider (Celestial)
creatures.description: When soulriders absorb sufficient planar energy, they become gravid with countless tiny eggs.

creatures.name: Spawning Soulrider (Fiend)
creatures.description: When soulriders absorb sufficient planar energy, they become gravid with countless tiny eggs.

creatures.name: Spawning Soulrider (Monitor)
creatures.description: When soulriders absorb sufficient planar energy, they become gravid with countless tiny eggs.

creatures.name: Squirrel Swarm
creatures.description: Descending with a shriek, a swarm of squirrels is a menace to foes, crawling in and out of pockets and clothing, biting and scratching as it goes.

creatures.name: Steam Mephit
creatures.description: These brash and arrogant creatures are among the more energetic mephits.

creatures.name: Stheno Harpist
creatures.description: With living snakes for hair, sthenos, who are descended from medusas, are a curious and independent ancestry.

creatures.name: Suli Dune Dancer
creatures.description: Because their genie forebears travel all across the Universe, sulis (scions of mortals and jann, who are composed of all six elements) are by and large the most common geniekin on that plane.

creatures.name: Sunflower Leshy
creatures.description: Sunflower leshys are the ambassadors and social leaders of their kind.

creatures.name: Sylph Sneak
creatures.description: Born with elemental gales coursing through their breath, sylphs are wispy planar scions whose bodies seem caught in a perpetual, gentle breeze.

creatures.name: Tanuki Village Hero
creatures.description: A tanuki village hero is a firefighter, monster slayer, peacekeeper, and altruist.

creatures.name: Tripkee Scout
creatures.description: Tripkee scouts typically keep watch on the outskirts of tripkee settlements.

creatures.name: Tunnel Viper
creatures.description: There is no better way for a ysoki to distinguish themself than to defend the warren, with many proving themselves by venturing out and challenging monsters they come across.

creatures.name: Umbral Gnome Scout
creatures.description: Umbral gnome scouts patrol the tunnels that lead into their settlements.

creatures.name: Undine Hydromancer
creatures.description: Undines are infused with elemental water-the churning power of the briny deep flows through them.

creatures.name: Vampire Bat Swarm
creatures.description: Although the typical vampire bat has a wingspan of 7 inches and doesn't pose a significant threat to larger prey alone (and indeed, these blood-drinkers can feed without their sleeping victims ever noticing), some unusually aggressive species of these bats hunt in deadly swarms.

creatures.name: Vanara Disciple
creatures.description: Many young vanaras travel, both to see the world and to search for a path that might lead to enlightenment.

creatures.name: Velociraptor
creatures.description: A smaller cousin of the deinonychus, the velociraptor is a swift, cunning pack hunter.

creatures.name: Vexgit
creatures.description: These gremlins are covered head-to-toe by a crustacean-like exoskeleton that clacks and rattles whenever a vexgit moves.

creatures.name: Void Zombie
creatures.description: A void zombie arises when a humanoid dies from an akata's void death affliction.

creatures.name: Voidworm
creatures.description: Other proteans don't consider the flying, iridescent beings known as voidworms to be part of a protean caste at all, but instead merely a shameful side effect of the Maelstrom's constantly churning energy.

creatures.name: War Pony
creatures.description: Horses serve as mounts and beasts of burden in many societies.

creatures.name: Water Mephit
creatures.description: Water mephits are consummate jokers who are always happy to trade a favor for a good laugh.

creatures.name: Water Scamp
creatures.description: Water scamps are marked apart from other scamps by sleek fur that traps a layer of water next to their skin.

creatures.name: Wayang Whisperblade
creatures.description: Some wayangs make use of their connections to shadows to outmaneuver their foes in combat.

creatures.name: Wolf
creatures.description: Wolves live and hunt in packs, which, contrary to popular belief, are not led by the strongest in the group, but typically consist of a mated pair, their pups, and juvenile offspring from previous mating seasons.

creatures.name: Wyrwood Sneak
creatures.description: Wyrwood sneaks specialize in moving unseen behind enemy lines, performing reconnaissance and quietly eliminating threats.

creatures.name: Xulgath Warrior
creatures.description: Xulgath warriors strike with fury and eager cruelty, always ready for the next fight.

creatures.name: Yzobu
creatures.description: Yzobus are herd beasts common across northern plains and mountains, resembling a cross between a steer, a sturdy horse, and a yak.

creatures.name: Zoaem
creatures.description: These endlessly curious archons are formed from spinning wheels of golden light given corporeal shape.

creatures.name: Acrobat
creatures.description: Acrobats perform feats of agility, balance, and strength. Performances come in a wide variety of forms, from musical methods like singing and instruments to physical dancing and juggling to simple orating and conversing.

creatures.name: Aiuvarin Elementalist
creatures.description: Commonly referred to as half-elves, aiuvarins often have trouble fitting into society. This can lead to many aiuvarins diverting their full attention towards their own personal pursuits, such as studying spellcasting. Elves are mysterious and intelligent, and graceful and cunning in battle.

creatures.name: Android Infiltrator
creatures.description: Androids possess synthetic bodies inhabited by living souls.

creatures.name: Angheuvore Flesh-Gnawer
creatures.description: Angheuvores, or mortic elves, have an insatiable hunger for raw humanoid flesh-though they're reluctant to feed on other elves.

creatures.name: Animated Armor
creatures.description: Suits of animated armor see use both as guardians and as training partners in high-end martial academies able to afford the extravagance.

creatures.name: Archer Sentry
creatures.description: Archer sentries slightly outrank rank-and-file guards, taking positions on walls, garrisons, and other important locations where they can stay out of the fray and pick off criminals or assailants.

creatures.name: Arms Dealer
creatures.description: A seedy arms dealer has access to all kinds of deadly weapons and can quickly provide them to clients in need… for the right price, of course. These lone wolves have an aura of mystery, bravado, and swagger.

creatures.name: Astronomer
creatures.description: Different cultures have created stories about the hows and whys of the universe, if things exist beyond the stars, and if the gods manipulate the heavenly bodies.

creatures.name: Azarketi Explorer
creatures.description: This rakish azarketi explores the wrecks and ruins of the Inner Sea's coasts.

creatures.name: Bandit
creatures.description: Bandits waylay travelers and plunder their valuables before disappearing back to their wilderness hideouts. Many bandits seek only to steal and release their victims alive, though a few prefer to leave no witnesses. In the underbelly of society, the lawless reign supreme.

creatures.name: Black Bear
creatures.description: Contrary to the name, black bears can be cinnamon or even blond in coloration, depending on which hue is most beneficial for surviving in their local environment.

creatures.name: Blasphemer of Zon-Kuthon
creatures.description: Blasphemers spread messages contrary to the tenets of their faith, often out of the belief that the gods are specifically targeting them to spread this message.

creatures.name: Bleachling Survivor
creatures.description: It is almost unheard of for gnomes to survive the horrendous sickness known as the Bleaching, but those few who do are permanently altered.

creatures.name: Blindheim
creatures.description: Inhabitants of damp, subterranean caves, blindheims resemble stubby humanoids with oversized, frog-like heads and bulbous eyes.

creatures.name: Blink Dog
creatures.description: At first glance, blink dogs seem to be little more than long-eared, bearded canines with tawny coats.

creatures.name: Boar
creatures.description: Boars are omnivorous mammals, hunted heavily because their meat is considered a delicacy.

creatures.name: Bog Strider
creatures.description: The insectile bog striders call themselves seshes, but they take no offense at those who use the more common vernacular to refer to them.

creatures.name: Boggard Warrior
creatures.description: Boggard warriors exalt single combat and prefer to fight alone so that no one can contest their kills.

creatures.name: Bugbear Prowler
creatures.description: Bugbear prowlers specialize in the art of lurking in the shadows.

creatures.name: Cactus Leshy
creatures.description: These little plants are known for their nettlesome attitude.

creatures.name: Caligni Skulker
creatures.description: Most often found on the surface are caligni skulkers, stealing staple goods and luxuries alike.

creatures.name: Cave Fisher
creatures.description: Cave fishers lurk in dark corners near the ceilings of caves and underground structures, lying in wait for prey to approach.

creatures.name: Choker
creatures.description: With long, cartilaginous limbs and gray skin, chokers easily hide in stony alcoves, rocky fissures, and darkened staircases to ambush their prey.

creatures.name: Cobbleswarm
creatures.description: Busy road builders might unknowingly use cobble mites in construction. Cobble mites resemble squarish stones a few inches across with mouths like split geodes. Though indolent and harmless alone, they're dangerous in large groups.

creatures.name: Cockroach Swarm
creatures.description: While cockroaches tend to gather in cramped spaces, a disturbed colony is sometimes prone to swarming, where hundreds or even thousands of the insects scurry out of their hiding places in a raft of shiny brown and black carapaces on thousands of skittering legs.

creatures.name: Construction Worker
creatures.description: A brilliant architect can contemplate wondrous structural marvels, but someone must pick up a hammer and make these dreams real. Construction workers are the backbone of any city's infrastructure. Society is built upon the backs of laborers.

creatures.name: Courtesan
creatures.description: Courtesans are high-class entertainers and professional companions, paid in favors and privileges for the honor of their company.

creatures.name: Coven Aspirant
creatures.description: Only the foolish would bring themselves to the attention of hags, but some aspirants strive to join a hag coven out of a desire for power or companionship.

creatures.name: Crocodile
creatures.description: Crocodiles can be found basking on riverbanks, lurking in swamps, or floating in lakes.

creatures.name: Deinonychus
creatures.description: Deinonychuses are wily hunters that attack in groups of up to a dozen individuals, ripping apart prey with sharp talons and powerful jaws.

creatures.name: Dero Stalker
creatures.description: Dero stalkers explore the surface world by night, seeking out victims to abduct.

creatures.name: Dhampir Wizard
creatures.description: This dhampir is a svetocher, the child of a moroi vampire.

creatures.name: Domovoi
creatures.description: The elderly domovoi are patrons of the home and the dearest of all house spirits to the people they live with.
senses: tremorsense (imprecise) within their entire bound home

creatures.name: Draugr
creatures.description: The risen corpses of sailors who died at sea, known as draugrs, reek of rot and decay from the briny deep.

creatures.name: Driver
creatures.description: With daring and charm, a driver is a professional operator of mechanical vehicles.

creatures.name: Dromaar Mountaineer
creatures.description: Dromaar mountaineers are hardened half-orc scouts who often lead raiding or scouting parties on dangerous expeditions.

creatures.name: Drow Rogue
creatures.description: Loners at heart, drow rogues trust no one-least of all fellow slayers.

creatures.name: Drunkard
creatures.description: Every tavern has one-that person who drinks a little too much and starts a fight.

creatures.name: Duende
creatures.description: Duendes fancy themselves as lords of the land-which is true, in a literal sense, these spirits embody the very soil upon the ground.

creatures.name: Ember Fox
creatures.description: Ember foxes resemble their canid namesakes, save for the flames that make their fur and the tips of their long whiskers flicker and glow.

creatures.name: Envyspawn
creatures.description: Sinspawn were created by one of seven ancient wizards known collectively as runelords—each of whom embraced and embodied one of seven sins.

creatures.name: Fading Fox
creatures.description: With fur that shifts between hues of red, dull brown, and silver as it moves, the fading fox vexes any pursuer.

creatures.name: Fey Dragonet
creatures.description: Although they are much smaller than their larger dragon cousins, fey dragonets have many of the same physiological attributes, including long necks, toothy maws, sinuous tails, and sharp claws.

creatures.name: Flaming Skull
creatures.description: More dangerous than simple severed heads, these skulls are wreathed in unearthly flames. Beheaded are the reanimated heads of decapitation victims. These mindless undead fly through the air or roll around to attack their prey.

creatures.name: Forlorn Artist
creatures.description: Forlorn elves, who spend their lives among shorter-lived peoples, often grow morose from constant loss. Some channel this melancholy into their art. Elves' long lives give them centuries to delve into studies, artistry, or exploration.

creatures.name: Fungus Leshy
creatures.description: Fungus leshies guard caves, bogs, and damp, dark places.

creatures.name: Ghast
creatures.description: Ghasts-feral, foul-smelling, and constantly hungry-are the more powerful kin of ghouls. They are relentless in the pursuit of their prey. Few creatures are more ubiquitous to sinister locations such as lonely graveyards and ruined crypts than the flesh-eating undead known as ghouls.

creatures.name: Ghoul Soldier
creatures.description: Retaining their martial skill, these powerful ghouls are not afraid to meet their foe in the open, feeding on the flesh of their fallen opponents to learn their combat abilities.

creatures.name: Giant Ant
creatures.description: Giant ants are much like their smaller kin in their industrious habits, though growing to the size of ponies makes them much deadlier. Ants are industrious insects that aid the natural processes of decay and renewal.

creatures.name: Giant Badger
creatures.description: The violent, territorial giant badger is a relentless predator.

creatures.name: Giant Bat
creatures.description: While big bats are certainly not uncommon in dark caves and abandoned ruins and may instill fear in squeamish spelunkers, the so-called giant bat is a true monster, weighing well over 100 pounds and having a wingspan of nearly 15 feet.

creatures.name: Giant Crab
creatures.description: Crabs are scavenging crustaceans known for their hard shells and iconic sideways gait.

creatures.name: Giant Flying Squirrel
creatures.description: Though other squirrels can fall from a great height safely, flying squirrels can soar through the air.

creatures.name: Giant Leech
creatures.description: Capable of growing to lengths of nearly 5 feet, giant leeches have circular maws filled with hook-like teeth.

creatures.name: Giant Monitor Lizard
creatures.description: Large and seemingly ponderous, a monitor lizard is a deceptively swift and ruthless predator.

creatures.name: Giant Opossum
creatures.description: Larger species of opossum can eat nearly anything human-sized or smaller. Few creatures have survived as long and in as many environments as the opossum.

creatures.name: Giant Porcupine
creatures.description: The porcupine, sometimes called a quill pig, is a shy, gray-and-brown herbivore covered in barbed quills.

creatures.name: Giant Toad
creatures.description: Most giant toads live in dry environments, particularly deserts, and eagerly eat almost any creature smaller than themselves.

creatures.name: Giant Viper
creatures.description: The giant viper's fangs are a frightening sight, with injection tubes as long as daggers.

creatures.name: Gluttonyspawn
creatures.description: Sinspawn were created by one of seven ancient wizards known collectively as runelords—each of whom embraced and embodied one of seven sins.

creatures.name: Gnome Daredevil
creatures.description: These nimble warriors capitalize on their slight stature and dexterous movements to evade and overtake their foes on the battlefield.

creatures.name: Greedspawn
creatures.description: Sinspawn were created by one of seven ancient wizards known collectively as runelords—each of whom embraced and embodied one of seven sins.

creatures.name: Halfling Head Chef
creatures.description: Renowned for their culinary expertise, halfling head chefs navigate the complex world of high cuisine with flavorful creations and fiery tempers. Halflings thrive on simple pleasures—having a pint at the pub or warming their feet by the hearth.
senses: +15 to smell and taste); keen eyes

creatures.name: Herexen
creatures.description: When a cleric rebels against their deity and dies in the grip of blasphemous rage, the heresies they committed in life may fuel their transformation into an undead herexen.

creatures.name: Hippogriff
creatures.description: With the proud bearing of a great raptor and the magnificence of a powerful horse, hippogriffs are thought to be an accidental fusion of creatures or perhaps the creation of a flesh-warping wizard with a keen aesthetic sense.

creatures.name: Hryngar Taskmaster
creatures.description: Hryngar priests of the taskmaster god Droskar often assume leadership roles within their communities, advancing the goals of their deity through coercion and displays of force.

creatures.name: Icicle Snake
creatures.description: Translucent and capable of hanging suspended and near motionless, icicle snakes sense the heat of living creatures as a threat and attempt to use their camouflage and chilling bite against foes.

creatures.name: Incutilis
creatures.description: Incutilises are intelligent, nautilus-like sea creatures that attack their victims' brains to take over their bodies, creating puppets to use for labor, combat, or their own malevolent amusement.

creatures.name: Infantry Soldier
creatures.description: Though low on the military hierarchy, infantry are still highly disciplined warriors, challenging for any ordinary person to face in combat. A military serves to defend and fight on behalf of nations and can be trained and deployed in various ways.

creatures.name: Ittan-Momen
creatures.description: Born from large pieces of cloth, ittan-momen flutter in the air, catching the last rays of the setting sun.

creatures.name: Jotunborn Sage
creatures.description: Jotunborn are large humanoids with indirect connections to ancient giants like titans.

creatures.name: Juggler
creatures.description: Jugglers are physical performers who master the art of manipulating props.

creatures.name: Kappa
creatures.description: Mischievous by nature, kappas delight in playing pranks on unsuspecting travelers.

creatures.name: Kholo Hunter
creatures.description: Kholo are tall, hyena-headed humanoids who dwell in savannas, warm grasslands, and arid hills.

creatures.name: Kitsune Trickster
creatures.description: With minor magic and shapeshifting capabilities, these tricksters love to play mostly harmless pranks for their own enjoyment.

creatures.name: Kobold Cavern Mage
creatures.description: Kobold cavern mages are born more than trained, hatching from eggs that absorbed particularly large amounts of primal earth energy.

creatures.name: Kobold Dragon Mage (Black)
creatures.description: Kobold dragon mages use magic to carry out their secret schemes.

creatures.name: Kobold Dragon Mage (Blue)
creatures.description: Kobold dragon mages use magic to carry out their secret schemes.

creatures.name: Kobold Dragon Mage (Green)
creatures.description: Kobold dragon mages use magic to carry out their secret schemes.

creatures.name: Kobold Dragon Mage (Red)
creatures.description: Kobold dragon mages use magic to carry out their secret schemes.

creatures.name: Kobold Dragon Mage (White)
creatures.description: Kobold dragon mages use magic to carry out their secret schemes.

creatures.name: Kobold Trapper
creatures.description: Kobolds are skillful artisans, always inventing new traps and snares to defend their territory and ambush enemies.

creatures.name: Ledalusca
creatures.description: Ledaluscas are reflective water elementals that can bring forth images previously reflected on their surfaces and wholly freeze themselves in the shapes of what they reflected.

creatures.name: Leopard
creatures.description: Leopards are among the smallest of the big cats, yet they are still dangerous creatures to tangle with.

creatures.name: Leprechaun
creatures.description: Leprechauns are mostly jovial tricksters who prefer mischief over conflict.

creatures.name: Living Boulder
creatures.description: Living boulders roll and glide through the Plane of Earth, gathering gemstones and metal shards until their surfaces resemble a ship's hull covered in barnacles.

creatures.name: Lizardfolk Stargazer
creatures.description: The tradition of astrology and looking to the stars for both navigation and prognostication is well-established in iruxi society, and their wise and observant stargazers are among the people's most respected members.

creatures.name: Loan Shark
creatures.description: Loan sharks lend money to those in need but charge high interest. If there's ever an issue with repayment, they'll send their gang to ensure clients pay in full. In the underbelly of society, the lawless reign supreme.

creatures.name: Lustspawn
creatures.description: Sinspawn were created by one of seven ancient wizards known collectively as runelords—each of whom embraced and embodied one of seven sins.

creatures.name: Merfolk Wavecaller
creatures.description: Merfolk wavecallers use their primal magic to defend their people.

creatures.name: Monkey Swarm
creatures.description: A single monkey can cause mischief. A troop of monkeys, emboldened by their numbers, can cause utter chaos, attacking anyone and looting anything in sight before scattering away with whatever they can carry. Monkeys vary greatly in size, color, and behavior. Some are distinguished by bright orange …

creatures.name: Morlock
creatures.description: Originating from humans long lost from the world of light, morlocks are brutal monsters that dwell in the tangled tunnels of the upper reaches of the Darklands.

creatures.name: Morlock Tinkerer
creatures.description: Most morlocks have little talent for crafting, but many have an uncanny knack for tinkering.

creatures.name: Mudwretch
creatures.description: In its resting form, a mudwretch looks like a large puddle of thick, dark mud, heaped at the center in a slightly drier patch of loam.

creatures.name: Munavri Spellblade
creatures.description: Although the subterranean Darklands are known for the cruel and domineering civilizations led by fiend-worshipping peoples who dwell within those sinister caverns, not every such subterranean society is ruled that way.

creatures.name: Munsahir
creatures.description: Munsahirs are an elemental people living on the Plane of Fire, often crudely described by mortals as brassy dwarves.

creatures.name: Nagaji Soldier
creatures.description: Nagaji are lithe but muscular bipeds with humanoid figures and serpentine heads.

creatures.name: Natural Scientist
creatures.description: If there's a mystery of nature that requires firsthand evidence to solve, natural scientists collect that data.

creatures.name: Navigator
creatures.description: A navigator uses celestial bodies and shipping lanes to determine routes. Adventurers may need passage on a swift vessel, or they might face danger from raiders at sea or in coastal settlements.

creatures.name: Nuglub
creatures.description: The "towering" hunchbacked nuglubs are among the largest gremlins, though they're not quite three feet tall.

creatures.name: Orc Commander
creatures.description: When orcs raid, the strongest is chosen as the leader, backed up by their siblings and other immediate family.

creatures.name: Pipefox
creatures.description: Pipefoxes are small, fluffy snakes with the head of a fox.

creatures.name: Pirate
creatures.description: These scourges are a threat to anyone who spends time away from land. Adventurers may need passage on a swift vessel, or they might face danger from raiders at sea or in coastal settlements.

creatures.name: Poacher
creatures.description: Rules against hunting may insulate the private reserves of nobles or guard the viability of animal populations in shared forests during specific seasons.

creatures.name: Poppet Mage
creatures.description: With extra time, care, and patience, poppets can be woven with magic, making them talented little spellcasters. Poppets are simple constructs made to assist their creators with basic tasks.

creatures.name: Pridespawn
creatures.description: Sinspawn were created by one of seven ancient wizards known collectively as runelords—each of whom embraced and embodied one of seven sins.

creatures.name: Prophet
creatures.description: The gods occasionally send messages in dreams to individuals who wander the lands. Some prophets have not received true divine missives but have misinterpreted normal dreams. Religions inspire devout individuals to uphold their tenets.

creatures.name: Pteranodon
creatures.description: Pteranodons are quick and agile reptiles with 20-foot wingspans that enable them to hover on wind currents for hours.

creatures.name: Pusk
creatures.description: Among the lowest and least powerful of all demons, the wretched pusks are just as vicious and cruel as their more powerful brethren.

creatures.name: Rat Snake Swarm
creatures.description: A solitary snake might be no cause for alarm, but a hissing mass of frenzied snakes can make even seasoned adventurers shudder.

creatures.name: Rokurokubi
creatures.description: Rokurokubi come into being when mortals are cursed for some misdeed, though often the one who bears the curse is not the one who committed the offense, but instead their child or spouse.

creatures.name: Rosethorn Ram
creatures.description: These hardy mountain dwellers surpass their lowlander cousins in stubbornness and agility.

creatures.name: Ruffian
creatures.description: Ruffians often work as bodyguards and enforcers for powerful criminals, using their strength to bully others into submission. In the underbelly of society, the lawless reign supreme.

creatures.name: Saboteur
creatures.description: Saboteurs excel at infiltration, using it to perform destructive acts. Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: Sasquatch
creatures.description: The elusive sasquatches dwell in the deepest, most remote and secluded tracts of wilderness.

creatures.name: Sea Devil Scout
creatures.description: Scouts, among the lowliest of sea devils, ply the inky waters of the ocean in search of aquatic prey, tread ashore to gauge landborne threats, or charge into battle with the advance forces of a greater sea devil war party.

creatures.name: Sedacthy Scout
creatures.description: Scouts, usually young sedacthies, ply the ocean in search of animal servants or tread ashore to hunt.

creatures.name: Shadow Drake
creatures.description: Shadow drakes are typically among the smallest and least powerful of their species.

creatures.name: Shocker Lizard
creatures.description: These brightly colored reptiles share the rough size and pack mentality of guard dogs, roaming in small groups.

creatures.name: Skeletal Champion
creatures.description: These skeletons retain the cunning they possessed in life. Animated skeletons are among the most common types of undead.

creatures.name: Skeletal Horse
creatures.description: Skeletal horses are sometimes used as mounts by other undead or monsters. Animated skeletons are among the most common types of undead.

creatures.name: Skum
creatures.description: The most numerous of the alghollthus are the ulat-kinis, a servitor race created from human stock to serve as rank-and-file soldiers in alghollthu armies.

creatures.name: Slime Mold
creatures.description: A slime mold appears as a mound of earth and detritus covered in a thick layer of fungus that exudes a faint stink of decay.

creatures.name: Slothspawn
creatures.description: Sinspawn were created by one of seven ancient wizards known collectively as runelords—each of whom embraced and embodied one of seven sins.

creatures.name: Slurk
creatures.description: The slurk is a sticky, tusked frog-beast found in underground lairs and caves.

creatures.name: Soulbound Doll (Brave)
creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.
languages: One Spoken by its Creator (typically Common)

creatures.name: Soulbound Doll (Calm)
creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.
languages: One Spoken by its Creator (typically Common)

creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.
languages: One Spoken by its Creator (typically Common)

creatures.name: Soulbound Doll (True Neutral)
creatures.description: Soulbound dolls are eerie mannequins or playthings that have been imbued with a small piece of a deceased mortal's soul.
languages: one spoken by it's creator (typically common)

creatures.name: Spark Moth
creatures.description: Spark bats congregate around volatile weather in the Plane of Air. Some elementals embody aspects of air, such as smoke, lightning, and fog.

creatures.name: Squirming Swill
creatures.description: Grotesque pieces of cast-off animal carcasses slowly stew into an overcooked mass at the bottom of a witch or hag's cauldron.

creatures.name: Stone Lion Cub
creatures.description: Stone lion cubs are curious, playful, and occasionally accidental mischief makers.

creatures.name: Street Musician
creatures.description: Many musicians make their living off stage by playing at markets, fairs, or crossroads.

creatures.name: Strix Kinmate
creatures.description: Kinmates carry a strong bond for one another and excel at teamwork. Strix, called itarii in their own language, are avian humanoids with sprawling, dark-feathered wings and large talons. They possess angular features and piercing eyes that are fixed facing forward.

creatures.name: Surgeon
creatures.description: The surgeon specializes in the physical alteration of the body to prevent the spread of disease, removing necrotic and decaying flesh to help the whole to survive.

creatures.name: Tatzlwyrm
creatures.description: Tatzlwyrms resemble human-sized snakes with two arms and a dragon's head.

creatures.name: Tengu Sneak
creatures.description: Tengu are an adaptable people originally from the continent of Tian Xia, but whose travels have taken them across all of Golarion.

creatures.name: Terror Bird
creatures.description: Common terror birds are notable hunters. On their own, they use their great speed to catch prey unaware. In a flock, they can swarm and take down larger beasts like aurochs with their overwhelming numbers. Terror birds aren't one species, but rather a family of deadly, flightless avian predators. A…

creatures.name: Tripkee Camoufleur
creatures.description: Camoufleurs, masters of natural camouflage, not only disguise their village scouts and warriors before they embark, but also create and maintain the village-wide concealment needed to keep their community hidden and safe.

creatures.name: Triton
creatures.description: While sometimes understandably mistaken for merfolk-both species live underwater and have a blend of features from both humans and fish-tritons have two legs instead of a tail.

creatures.name: Umbral Gnome Warrior
creatures.description: Umbral gnome warriors are quick to charge into battle but focus on defending their kin and their homes over more aggressive tactics when a choice is available.

creatures.name: Vermin Catcher
