creatures.description: Paid exterminators keep streets and sewers clear of pests like rats, snakes, weasels, and insects—even giant rats. Society is built upon the backs of laborers.

creatures.name: War Horse
creatures.description: Horses serve as mounts and beasts of burden in many societies.

creatures.name: Warg
creatures.description: Wargs hunt in packs like wolves, but their penchant for taunting victims makes them easy to distinguish from their cousins.

creatures.name: Werebat
creatures.description: Werebats form organized colonies of opportunistic hunters.

creatures.name: Wereboar
creatures.description: Wereboars tend to be aggressive and stubborn, and often live with their own kind in small, remote communities.

creatures.name: Werecrocodile
creatures.description: The curse of the werecrocodile instills powerful hunger, urges violent displays of dominance, and amplifies greed.

creatures.name: Wererat
creatures.description: Wererats tend to be selfishly opportunistic, avaricious, and paranoid as a result of their curse.

creatures.name: Wolverine
creatures.description: Wolverines resemble small bears, to which they are related. Stocky and muscular with long claws, wolverines are about 3 feet in length and weigh approximately 40 pounds. They have a thick fur coat that protects them from the cold temperatures of the forests and frozen tundras where they reside.

creatures.name: Wrathspawn
creatures.description: Sinspawn were created by one of seven ancient wizards known collectively as runelords—each of whom embraced and embodied one of seven sins.

creatures.name: Xulgath Skulker
creatures.description: Xulgath warrens are patrolled—some might say "haunted"—by the community's skulkers.

creatures.name: Yellow Musk Brute
creatures.description: A creature transformed by a yellow musk creeper's tendrils boring into its brain becomes a yellow musk thrall if it's Small or Medium, or a yellow musk brute if Large (a Small creature becomes a Small thrall rather than a Medium one).

creatures.name: Yellow Musk Creeper
creatures.description: Made up of thick, green vines with numerous yellow flowers, the yellow musk creeper grows at places where the ground is enriched with spilled blood and corpses, like battlefields and graveyards.

creatures.name: Zombie Brute
creatures.description: Necromantic augmentations have granted this zombie increased size and power. A zombie's only desire is to consume the living. Unthinking and ever-shambling harbingers of death, zombies stop only when they're destroyed.

creatures.name: Zyss Serpentfolk
creatures.description: Even the least among zyss serpentfolk consider themselves greater than any mammal.

creatures.name: Aapoph Serpentfolk
creatures.description: Aapophs possess greater strength and stronger venom than their zyss kin, but they lack zyss's innate magic.

creatures.name: Akizendri
creatures.description: Akizendris gnaw at sources of knowledge and lore, gleefully corrupting and altering them to vex scholars across the planes with contradictions and untruths.

creatures.name: Animated Statue
creatures.description: Animated statues are often used to guard crypts, small shrines, or areas in government buildings where they can be positioned amid normal statues to hide their true nature until an intruder arouses their ire.

creatures.name: Ankhrav
creatures.description: These horse-sized, burrowing monsters generally avoid heavily settled areas like cities, but ankhravs' predilection for livestock and humanoid flesh ensures that the creatures do not remain in the deep wilderness for long.

creatures.name: Artillerist
creatures.description: The maintenance and operation of a siege weapon is the highest form of art for the artillerist.
senses: (+10 if the artillerist is crewing a siege weapon)

creatures.name: Assassin Vine
creatures.description: The assassin vine is a carnivorous plant with a voracious appetite.

creatures.name: Athamaru Hunter
creatures.description: Deep in the sea, schools of athamarus—piscine humanoids armed with spears and specialized crossbows—stalk sharks, sea serpents, and giant squid from the backs of their giant moray eel mounts.

creatures.name: Boggard Swampseer
creatures.description: The boggard swampseer has been gifted with magic through their worship of the demon lord Gogunta, and they use their power to rule a boggard village, keeping the rest of the village in line and planning raids on nearby communities.

creatures.name: Bosun
creatures.description: A ship's boatswain, or bosun, leads the deckhands who maintain the ship. Adventurers may need passage on a swift vessel, or they might face danger from raiders at sea or in coastal settlements.

creatures.name: Brine Shark
creatures.description: Brine sharks are deadly elementals that roam the endless oceans of the Plane of Water.

creatures.name: Bugbear Tormentor
creatures.description: The bugbear tormentor seeks to torture their prey as much through psychological intimidation as through physical harm.

creatures.name: Bunyip
creatures.description: Bunyips are dangerous aquatic predators that resemble a cross between a shark and a seal.

creatures.name: Buso Farmer
creatures.description: Busos are tree-dwelling folk with a simmering desire to consume the flesh of others.

creatures.name: Caligni Slayer
creatures.description: Caligni slayers possess greater occult talents than others of their kind.

creatures.name: Centaur
creatures.description: Centaurs are legendary hunters and trackers who resemble heavily muscled humans with the bodies of powerful horses from the waist down.

creatures.name: Centaur Herbalist
creatures.description: Many centaurs are skilled in the study of plants, especially those in the areas in which they roam.

creatures.name: Centipede Swarm
creatures.description: Swarms of centipedes are dangerous indeed, ravenous carpets of skittering hunger capable of devouring a traveler whole in a matter of minutes.

creatures.name: Changeling Exile
creatures.description: This changeling exile is the child of a cuckoo hag.

creatures.name: Charlatan
creatures.description: Charlatans use pretense and misdirection to swindle money and other valuables from the credulous and confused. In the underbelly of society, the lawless reign supreme.

creatures.name: Chronicler
creatures.description: Exploration means very little if no account of the details exist.

creatures.name: Chupacabra
creatures.description: These notorious predators have an undeniable thirst for blood.

creatures.name: Cinder Rat
creatures.description: These oversized rodents are made of smoldering charcoal and elemental fire, and noxious fumes continually bellow from their flaming flesh.

creatures.name: Cockatrice
creatures.description: Ugly and aggressive, the dread cockatrice stalks garbage pits and hillside dumps in search of prey that it can turn to stone with its petrifying beak and subsequently consume piece by broken piece.

creatures.name: Compsognathus Swarm
creatures.description: Though a curious lone compsognathus is occasionally known to sneak into an adventurer's camp, they're often scouting from a nearby pack potentially containing dozens of the tiny bipedal dinosaurs.

creatures.name: Conscript Squad
creatures.description: Not all soldiers are on the battlefield by choice.

creatures.name: D'ziriak
creatures.description: These strange creatures are native to the Netherworld, where their colorful nature is in opposition to that realm's overwhelmingly monochromatic palette.

creatures.name: Dero Strangler
creatures.description: Dero stranglers specialize in capturing living victims, and they are often called upon to aid in abductions.

creatures.name: Dire Wolf
creatures.description: Much larger and more foul-tempered than their common cousins, dire wolves haunt primeval lands that accommodate their massive size and proportionately large hunting grounds and appetites.

creatures.name: Diver
creatures.description: Divers can often make large amounts of money by diving for treasures and selling them at a markup to tourists and antiquities dealers. Adventurers may need passage on a swift vessel, or they might face danger from raiders at sea or in coastal settlements.

creatures.name: Divoynik
creatures.description: A close-knit settlement is rocked to its core when a beloved pillar of the community is accused of a heinous crime.

creatures.name: Doppelganger
creatures.description: In their natural forms, doppelgangers are humanoid creatures with flesh of indeterminate color and features that lack fine details, as if unfinished.

creatures.name: Dragonblood Occultist
creatures.description: The dragonblood occultist draws power from their omen dragon benefactor.

creatures.name: Draxie
creatures.description: The mischievous dragon sprites called draxies have dueled their pixie cousins for the title of ultimate prankster for centuries.

creatures.name: Drow Priestess
creatures.description: Many drow priestesses venerate demon lords and other foul divinities.

creatures.name: Dryad
creatures.description: Dryads are fey guardians of the trees and creatures who dwell in wooded areas.

creatures.name: Dvorovoi
creatures.description: Traditionally, the dvorovoi is seen as the domovoi's younger sibling and has charge of a peasant's yard and all the animals and tools within.
senses: tremorsense (imprecise) within their entire bound yard

creatures.name: Esobok
creatures.description: Esoboks are brute hunters and pugnacious sentinels who serve as the guard dogs of the Boneyard.

creatures.name: Fen Mosquito Swarm
creatures.description: When deadly fen mosquitoes gather in large numbers, they form into lethal swarms capable of draining blood at a truly alarming rate.

creatures.name: Fiend Caller
creatures.description: Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: Gambling Companion
creatures.description: A reputation for protecting against bad luck, combined with skill and knowledge, establish these tengu as ideal gambling companions.

creatures.name: Ganzi Martial Artist
creatures.description: Children of primeval chaos, ganzis intertwine the churning pandemonium of the Maelstrom with the more mundane tumult of mortal life.

creatures.name: Gelatinous Cube
creatures.description: Found underground or in dungeons, these quivering cubes of slime continuously scour their domain for food.

creatures.name: Ghoran Manipulator
creatures.description: Ghorans are sentient plants who grow in the shape of humanoids.

creatures.name: Giant Chameleon
creatures.description: Giant chameleons are legendary for their ability to change their skin color in response to their surroundings. Their eyes are capable of peering in different directions independently, making them almost as difficult to sneak up on as they are to notice in the first place.

creatures.name: Giant Dragonfly Nymph
creatures.description: The aquatic, wingless offspring of dragonflies are called dragonfly nymphs.

creatures.name: Giant Eagle
creatures.description: No mere animals, giant eagles have a keen intellect and a strong sense of honor.

creatures.name: Giant Mantis
creatures.description: These massive cousins of praying mantises stand taller than an average human. These predators possess lightning-quick forelegs and a bone-breaking bite.

creatures.name: Giant Scorpion
creatures.description: These massive, terrifying arachnids are typically 8 feet long from head to the base of the tail.

creatures.name: Giant Seahorse
creatures.description: Giant seahorses are bear-sized, docile versions of their tiny, more common cousins.

creatures.name: Giant Vulture
creatures.description: Recognizable at a distance by their black feathers and bald necks, vultures drift along air currents looking for carrion to serve as their next meal.

creatures.name: Giant Wasp
creatures.description: Giant wasps are much more dangerous but less common than their smaller kin, and—to the relief of those who encounter them—they tend to be solitary.

creatures.name: Giant Whiptail Centipede
creatures.description: Whiptail centipedes are sleek and swift tunnel predators.

creatures.name: Gorilla
creatures.description: Gorillas can be territorial creatures, particularly if provoked by hunters or the presence of more dangerous monsters.

creatures.name: Grioth Cultist
creatures.description: Grioth cultists represent the lowest tier of their remorseless cult-worshippers of the Elder Mythos tasked with leading scouting missions into new worlds.

creatures.name: Grizzly Bear
creatures.description: This large and powerful omnivore inhabits forested hills.

creatures.name: Grothlut
creatures.description: Sluglike abominations, grothluts are grotesque dregs of the fleshwarping process.

creatures.name: Harbormaster
creatures.description: A port must sustain itself on different types of flows: the seaward and coastal flows, marked by the rising and falling of the tides, and the constant flow of trade.

creatures.name: Hell Hound
creatures.description: A hell hound's appearance dismisses any doubts as to their infernal origins—flesh the color of burning pitch, teeth as sharp as any fiend's pitchfork, and a shroud of ever-burning hellfire are all trademark features.

creatures.name: Hobgoblin Spellbreaker
creatures.description: Largely due to an ancestral grudge against all things elven, many hobgoblins have an inherent distrust for magic, particularly the "elf magic" of the arcane tradition.

creatures.name: Hound Topiary
creatures.description: A majestic hound can be found guarding the entrance to several noble gardens across Avistan, often as a warning sign to raiding goblin parties or a devotional plea for protection from Cayden Cailean or Dispater, depending on the region.

creatures.name: Hyaenodon
creatures.description: Hyaenodons are ferocious primeval creatures that resemble hyenas.

creatures.name: Inspector
creatures.description: Inspectors cultivate a wide selection of skills to investigate arson, murder, and other serious crimes, usually in major urban centers.
senses: (+15 to Sense Motive, Seek, or Search)

creatures.name: Jailer
creatures.description: A jailer's primary responsibility is to keep prisoners from escaping.

creatures.name: Kholo Bonekeeper
creatures.description: Some kholos serve as conduits to the divine and are responsible for placating spirits and gods alike.

creatures.name: Kobold Egg Guardian
creatures.description: Kobold egg guardians are some of the best warriors in a tribe, tasked with protecting the next generation.

creatures.name: Kovintus Geomancer
creatures.description: Kovintus are reclusive humanoids with an inborn talent for geomancy that ties them intrinsically to nature.

creatures.name: Kuribu
creatures.description: Kuribus serve as the guardians of temples and other holy sites.

creatures.name: Lacridaemon
creatures.description: Souls who die from neglect or exposure due to becoming lost, trapped in small spaces, or otherwise being far from help can transform into lacridaemons.

creatures.name: Leaping Thief
creatures.description: Some catfolk take advantage of their natural agility to steal from those too slow to catch them.

creatures.name: Lion
creatures.description: Lions are cooperative hunters, ambushing dangerous prey in groups of lionesses that work in tandem to trap and kill their prey.

creatures.name: Living Graffiti (Blood)
creatures.description: A living graffiti is a painting or drawing that has come to life by magic.

creatures.name: Living Graffiti (Chalk)
creatures.description: A living graffiti is a painting or drawing that has come to life by magic.

creatures.name: Living Graffiti (Ink)
creatures.description: A living graffiti is a painting or drawing that has come to life by magic.

creatures.name: Living Graffiti (Oil)
creatures.description: A living graffiti is a painting or drawing that has come to life by magic.

creatures.name: Mage for Hire
creatures.description: Some mercenaries sell magical talents to earn a living.

creatures.name: Martial Student
creatures.description: Every warrior must begin somewhere. Martial artists strive to master the art of hand-to-hand fighting.

creatures.name: Megalictis
creatures.description: These giant weasels are driven by the same hunger that plagues their smaller cousins, but because of their much larger size, they can quickly devastate the wildlife in an environment.

creatures.name: Mime
creatures.description: Mimes are performers who use movement, gestures, and expressions without any speech to act out a scene or situation for onlookers. Performances come in a wide variety of forms, from musical methods like singing and instruments to physical dancing and juggling to simple orating and conversing.

creatures.name: Moose
creatures.description: Moose are a large, solitary type of deer. They're majestic, territorial, and extremely grumpy-traits that make them dangerous to unwary travelers and would-be predators alike. Males are instantly recognizable, standing 7 feet tall at the shoulder, with large heads and thick necks to support their i…

creatures.name: Musketeer
creatures.description: Flashy and confident, the musketeer isn't above using dirty tricks to gain the upper hand in a fight. Despite their bravado, musketeers are fiercely loyal to their allies. These lone wolves have an aura of mystery, bravado, and swagger.

creatures.name: Narwhal
creatures.description: Narwhals are mid-sized whales known for the long tusk that grows from their nose.

creatures.name: Necrophidius
creatures.description: Although the necrophidius is a mindless construct with no particular ability to comprehend the complexities of good or evil, these sinister-looking constructs are often crafted by evil or macabre creators seeking to imbue their guardians with a frightening aesthetic.

creatures.name: Noble
creatures.description: Envied by many and detested by some, nobles exude confidence and gentility.

creatures.name: Ogre Warrior
creatures.description: The simplest of ogres are slabs of muscle with hateful eyes, misshapen visages, and malformed bodies.

creatures.name: Pachycephalosaurus
creatures.description: Pachycephalosauruses are normally peaceful, herbivorous dinosaurs, but they grow much more violent during mating season, when they fight one another to win over mates and warn away interlopers.

creatures.name: Pegasus
creatures.description: The pegasus is a winged horse prized for its capacity to serve as an aerial mount.

creatures.name: Piranha Swarm
creatures.description: More teeth and fury than body, few fish command as much fear and anxiety as the piranha.

creatures.name: Pitborn Adept
creatures.description: One of the most common types of nephilim is the pitborn, who bear a demonic corruption infesting their mortal bloodline.

creatures.name: Platecarpus
creatures.description: The mid-sized platecarpuses are the most common type of mosasaur.

creatures.name: Propagandist
creatures.description: The misleadings, half-facts, and effortless spin propagandists cast over events create proof of whatever their bosses need them to prove. Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: Quickling
creatures.description: Few creatures can match a quickling's speed.

creatures.name: Raven Swarm
creatures.description: A flock of ravens is known as an unkindness.

creatures.name: River Drake
creatures.description: Although the glistening scales and sleek, fin-like wings on these drakes give them an appearance reminiscent of river fish, they are actually distant relatives of the dragons that rule the oceans.

creatures.name: Runaway Blueblood
creatures.description: Dissatisfied with their privileged upbringing, the runaway blueblood has left the life of luxury behind to forge a new path for themself. These lone wolves have an aura of mystery, bravado, and swagger.

creatures.name: Rust Monster
creatures.description: Found in lost dungeons, deep caves, and abandoned mines, rust monsters are a bane to any adventurers who rely on armor, weapons, or other metal items.

creatures.name: Scalescribe
creatures.description: Often seen at the sides of spellcasters, scalescribes are tiny, snakelike creatures with numerous hands that usually hold a multitude of fountain pens in various ink colors.

creatures.name: Sea Hag
creatures.description: Sea hags specialize in transformation magic, preying on those who are desperate to change some aspect of their physical appearance.

creatures.name: Seaweed Leshy
creatures.description: Calmer than most of their other botanical brethren, seaweed leshys are a tranquil, discerning breed of leshy.

creatures.name: Shulsaga
creatures.description: Shepherds of the Silver Sea, shulsagas patrol the Astral Plane to protect the nascent demiplanes that form there, warding off those who would usurp the demiplanes or despoil them with unwelcome philosophies.

creatures.name: Sibyl
creatures.description: Sibyls are oracular diviners who often fall deep into trances with their oracular curses to spur a frenzied mental state.

creatures.name: Skeletal Giant
creatures.description: The reanimated bones of giants make excellent necromantic thralls. Animated skeletons are among the most common types of undead.

creatures.name: Smith
creatures.description: Most smaller communities have at least one smithy where locals and travelers can have horses shod or equipment repaired.

creatures.name: Snapping Flytrap
creatures.description: Snapping flytraps typically have two sets of tooth-edged leaves, each measuring 3 feet wide, at the end of 10-foot-long stalks. Flytraps eagerly feed on humanoids, monstrous insects, and larger prey.

creatures.name: Sod Hound
creatures.description: Sod hounds are mossy extraplanar canines formed of packed dirt and pebbles.

creatures.name: Spriggan Bully
creatures.description: The typical spriggan is a bully who prefers to take live prisoners in fights, but only to ensure that they have a large stock of living victims to torment, tease, and abuse.

creatures.name: Street Skelm
creatures.description: Street skelms abuse the deference given to elders and the well-to-do in public spaces, appearing as such so they can verbally or physically punish those who dare defy their cruel sensibilities.

creatures.name: String Slime
creatures.description: Found underground or in dungeons, these quivering, slug-like ropes of slime continuously scour their domain for food.

creatures.name: Swarm Voice
creatures.description: The swarm voice is the secular leader of a ysoki warren or one family in a larger warren.

creatures.name: Tattoo Guardian
creatures.description: Tattoo guardians are vigilant protectors drawn onto the skin of the individuals they are meant to protect.

creatures.name: Tonic Merchant
creatures.description: This alchemist sells healing potables, but might offer their services without a fee to those truly in need. The world is a dangerous place. Thankfully, there are those who devote their lives to easing the pain and suffering of others.

creatures.name: Tooth Fairy Swarm
creatures.description: A mob of tooth fairies working together can conduct forced dentistry in seconds.

creatures.name: Toymaker
creatures.description: The whimsy of a toymaker is only matched by their ruthlessness when cornered.

creatures.name: Tracker
creatures.description: An untrained eye might spot a sign or two of a wild creature's passing, but only a skilled tracker can identify several such signs and discern their relationship to each other, connecting one to the next until they form a trail of prints, scat, fur, feathers, and blood that leads to the quarry's la…

creatures.name: Trailgaunt
creatures.description: Legends warn of trailgaunts-the twitching, rotting corpses of seasoned travelers who became lost on their journeys and died of starvation, thirst, or exposure to the elements.

creatures.name: Traveling Actor
creatures.description: The life of a traveling actor is, contrary to belief, not one of glamor but effort.

creatures.name: Trilobite Swarm
creatures.description: Trilobites typically live in close proximity to others of their kind.

creatures.name: Trollhound
creatures.description: Trollhounds are squat, slavering beasts akin to trolls in canine form.

creatures.name: Troubadour
creatures.description: Troubadours keep alive the traditional songs of their culture and write original works to commemorate major events. Performances come in a wide variety of forms, from musical methods like singing and instruments to physical dancing and juggling to simple orating and conversing.

creatures.name: Tumbleweed Leshy Courier
creatures.description: A tumbleweed leshy's ability to move quickly across long distances with ease allows them to act as messengers and go-betweens.
languages: speak with plants (tumbleweeds and scrubland brush only)

creatures.name: Twigjack
creatures.description: "True" twigjacks, as they consider themselves, are found in wild forests alongside the fey they idolize.

creatures.name: Unicorn
creatures.description: Symbols of grace and purity, unicorns resemble proud and noble horses.

creatures.name: Urdefhan Warrior
creatures.description: From the moment they're born, urdefhans are prepared for war.

creatures.name: Vampiric Mist
creatures.description: Though they're often mistaken for vampires in gaseous form or an unusual type of air elemental, vampiric mists are in fact strange aberrant life-forms.

creatures.name: Vermlek
creatures.description: Vermleks, also called worm demons, manifest from the souls of mortals who desecrated the dead, such as grave robbers or necromancers.

creatures.name: Violet Fungus
creatures.description: At a glance, a violet fungus might seem to be little more than an unsightly and sickeningly purple mushroom of unusual size.

creatures.name: Vishkanya Infiltrator
creatures.description: These vishkanyas can meld with a crowd and slip into and out of places otherwise off-limits.

creatures.name: Watch Officer
creatures.description: Often leading a small team of lower-ranking guards, watch officers patrol their assigned areas to maintain order and enforce laws.

creatures.name: Web Lurker
creatures.description: Web lurkers, known also as ettercaps, are ugly monsters that not only dwell within the lairs of spiders and swarms, but actively cultivate and shepherd such vermin.

creatures.name: Werewolf
creatures.description: The curse of the werewolf—known as lycanthropy to many—instills in its carriers the hungry bloodlust and predatory instincts of the wolf.

creatures.name: Wight
creatures.description: Wights are intelligent undead spawned through inescapable cycles of spite.

creatures.name: Winged Chupacabra
creatures.description: Some chupacabras are mutants with large reptilian wings and have been known to carry off goats or even children.

creatures.name: Wolliped
creatures.description: Wollipeds are large, docile herd animals found in the snowy foothills of large mountains.

creatures.name: Xulgath Leader
creatures.description: Xulgath leaders are usually the strongest, most violent members of a community—although in some cases, particularly in larger groups, xulgath clutches are led by powerful demon cultists or other monsters who seek to use the xulgaths as servants or sacrifices.

creatures.name: Yeth Hound
creatures.description: Wicked canine creatures who live for the thrill of the hunt, yeth hounds often serve evil masters as guardians and trackers.

creatures.name: Zebub
creatures.description: Zebubs serve as Hell's messengers and spies.

creatures.name: Zephyr Hawk
creatures.description: Zephyr hawks drift among the currents of the Plane of Air in great flocks.

creatures.name: Abrikandilu
creatures.description: Wrecker demons, also known as abrikandilus, despise beautiful things and do everything in their power to destroy both people and objects regarded as such.

creatures.name: Aghash
creatures.description: Walking embodiments of curses, misfortune, and the evil eye, aghashes wander the deserts of the Material Plane, searching out the arrogant, charming, and persuasive to humiliate and undermine.

creatures.name: Alce
creatures.description: Wingless griffons, known as alces, result from a rare mutation.

creatures.name: Amphisbaena
creatures.description: An amphisbaena is an exceedingly aggressive, venomous viper of remarkable size that bears two heads-one on each end of its body.

creatures.name: Aphorite Sharpshooter
creatures.description: The denizens of the Eternal City of Axis first forged aphorites to serve as emissaries, agents, and facilitators, with one foot in the sublime, perfect order, and one in the messy, murky, confusing muck of the Universe.

creatures.name: Arboreal Warden
creatures.description: Arboreal wardens are the rangers of arboreal society.

creatures.name: Attic Whisperer
creatures.description: Beware the haunting sobs of the attic whisperer, for they carry the pained wrath of an abandoned child who perished due to the neglect or absence of their caretakers.

creatures.name: Barghest
creatures.description: Barghests are canine beasts that take great joy in the hunt, often lurking near humanoid settlements to find prey that can provide a suitable challenge.

creatures.name: Beast Tamer
creatures.description: Beast tamers bring the wild to civilization, rearing and training creatures to follow their commands and perform flashy tricks that entertain audiences.

creatures.name: Bison
creatures.description: Bison are large bovines with short faces and two horns; they weigh an upwards1 of 2,000 pounds and stand up to 6 feet at the withers.

creatures.name: Bounty Hunter
creatures.description: Bounty hunters are constantly on the move, whether within city walls or the wilderness, trailing their fugitive quarries for capture...

creatures.name: Brood Leech Swarm
creatures.description: Most smaller species of leeches do not tend to swarm, but brood leeches are prone to gathering in seething, undulant mats of squirming gluttony.

creatures.name: Burglar
creatures.description: These criminals specialize in breaking and entering, gaining access to secure buildings and bypassing security measures undetected. In the underbelly of society, the lawless reign supreme.

creatures.name: Cairn Wight
creatures.description: Jealous guardians of tombs, barrows, and sepulchers, cairn wights usually spawn from necromantic rituals.

creatures.name: Calathgar
creatures.description: Calathgars are mysterious predatory plants that grow in the depths of the densest frozen forests, realms where icicles rarely melt from snow-cloaked trees.

creatures.name: Caligni Hunter
creatures.description: Although caligni hunters are often pressed into leading their skulker kin, most hunters prefer to wander the Darklands or raid the surface free from those responsibilities.

creatures.name: Carrion Golem
creatures.description: Carrion golems are foul-smelling and fly-swarmed amalgams of putrefied parts stitched together from many different creatures.

creatures.name: Coil Spy
creatures.description: Some serpentfolk undergo intense ritual training and practice to improve their innate ability to disguise themselves.

creatures.name: Corn Leshy Throng
creatures.description: A thick forest or flourishing farm sometimes sees an explosion of primal magic leading to the creation of a multitude of leshies.

creatures.name: Corrupted Relic
creatures.description: Creatures that once held particular items dear can be bound to them after death, their souls inhabiting the item from within.

creatures.name: Daeodon
creatures.description: Where the typical boar is merely ill-tempered and generally unfriendly, the towering daeodon is legitimately hateful and ruthlessly violent.

creatures.name: Deluded Mob
creatures.description: Pulled astray by lies, bribes, and propaganda, these desperate people are convinced to fight on their behalf of utter villains. Conspiracists, propagandists, masterminds, despots, and more take advantage of these mobs. Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: Demolitionist
creatures.description: While most engineers take immense pride in their work constructing something that may survive many lifetimes, the demolitionist takes pride in destroying such pompous things in the most spectacular way possible.

creatures.name: Duskwalker Ghost Hunter
creatures.description: Duskwalkers are infused with the same energies as psychopomps.

creatures.name: Dwarf Stonecaster
creatures.description: All dwarves share a connection to the earth, but stonecasters have focused and polished that connection.

creatures.name: Earthen Destrier
creatures.description: This wave of dirt takes the crude likeness of the melded forequarters of a charging warhorse and a rocky knight wielding a rudimentary lance of gray stone. Certain earth elementals manifest as specific types of material, be they boulders, sand, or crystals.

creatures.name: Enigmatic Conspirant
creatures.description: Powerful organizations work out of public view, shaping lives while facing few consequences.

creatures.name: Equestrian Constable
creatures.description: Equestrian constables patrol for criminals on horseback in wealthy areas or serve as reeves to enforce court orders.

creatures.name: Exuberant Apprentice
creatures.description: Bright-eyed and bushy-tailed, these young mages seamlessly combine boundless curiosity, vigorous enthusiasm, and a complete lack of survival instincts.

creatures.name: False Priest
creatures.description: Belief is perhaps the strongest force in the universe.

creatures.name: Filth Fire
creatures.description: These creatures look like roiling clouds of black smoke churning above a mound of burning refuse.

creatures.name: Flytrap Leshy
creatures.description: While often seen as unfriendly by non-leshys, flytrap leshys get along well with other leshys.

creatures.name: Gancanagh
creatures.description: Gancanaghs are lovers, revelers, and dashing duelists of Elysium.

creatures.name: Gargoyle
creatures.description: Gargoyles are monstrous hunters made of elemental stone.

creatures.name: Ghost Commoner
creatures.description: The ghost commoner is an ordinary person who believes they died unjustly. When some mortals die through tragic circumstances or without closure, their souls can linger and haunt a locale significant to them in life.

creatures.name: Giant Dragonfly
creatures.description: These buzzing insects are the size of a small horse.

creatures.name: Giant Pangolin
creatures.description: Despite their prodigious size, giant pangolins are solitary, reclusive creatures who forage by night for insects and vermin.

creatures.name: Giant Stag Beetle
creatures.description: The giant stag beetle is larger than a horse, and the sight of one flying (if slowly and somewhat clumsily) on great buzzing wings is unnerving.

creatures.name: Giant Wolverine
creatures.description: Giant wolverines grow to the approximate size of horses and claim even more expansive territories than their more diminutive wolverine cousins. While a smaller wolverine will defend its prey from armed humanoids, a giant wolverine is unafraid to stalk and kill such creatures.

creatures.name: Gigantopithecus
creatures.description: These fierce kin of orangutans are three times heavier than a gorilla. While many apes exhibit peaceful or reclusive behavior, gorillas can be territorial, and the megaprimatus is especially aggressive and dangerous.

creatures.name: Goblin Scavenger
creatures.description: Many goblins scavenge materials, shiny objects, and anything else they can get their hands on, but a goblin scavenger is the best at finding such useful items.

creatures.name: Gobmob Snake
creatures.description: These bizarre mutant goblin snakes possess no fewer than three heads. A goblin snake is a foul-smelling serpentine creature covered in greasy black scales and sporting a fanged, lipless head resembling that of a goblin.

creatures.name: Gray Ooze
creatures.description: These dangerous oozes are the bane of any who travel through swamps, marshes, or damp caves.

creatures.name: Great White Shark
creatures.description: An apex predator among the coastal surface waters where it hunts, the great white shark is one of the largest shark species.

creatures.name: Green Hag
creatures.description: Green hags hate beauty and purity, so they use disguises and treachery to lure and murder innocents, corrupt the pure of heart, and shatter the minds and morals of their victims.

creatures.name: Griffon
creatures.description: Griffons are regal beasts revered as symbols of freedom and strength in many cultures.

creatures.name: Guide
creatures.description: Guides bring travelers, tourists, and adventurers into the wondrous natural world, using their expertise to avoid deadly monsters and gruesome hazards. Explorers are often well-equipped and well-trained for any type of hazard and are eager to lead others into the wild.

creatures.name: Hadrosaurid
creatures.description: Hadrosaurids are a broad grouping of herbivorous dinosaurs that share characteristic flat snouts filled with rows of grinding teeth well suited for feeding on vegetation.

creatures.name: Hellbound Attorney
creatures.description: The Hellbound attorney sought the assistance of a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.Phistophilus]-one of Hell's contract devils-to bolster her legalistic ability on the Universe.

creatures.name: Hermit Crab Swarm
creatures.description: When hermit crabs find a shell that is too large, they lie in wait for others to arrive and move into the shell, abandoning a possible replacement.

creatures.name: Hobgoblin Archer
creatures.description: Bands of hobgoblin soldiers typically have at least one archer among their ranks.

creatures.name: Horned Archon
creatures.description: Horned archons are secretive and tireless sentinels and scouts, patrolling the Outer Sphere's untamed wilds for evil to eliminate and keeping small communities safe from demons, devils, and worse.

creatures.name: Hound Archon
creatures.description: Among the rank-and-file soldiers of Heaven's armies, few individuals are more dependable and truer to the cause than the noble hound archons, embodiments of the virtue of diligence.

creatures.name: Huldra
creatures.description: At first glance, a huldra seems a simple creature-a human in plain robes or peasant dress, sporting long, unbound hair.

creatures.name: Ice Troll
creatures.description: Ice trolls are nightmarish frozen monsters of rime-stained claws and hoarfrost teeth who haunt glaciers, mountains, and tundras.

creatures.name: Jann
creatures.description: All six elements build each jann and fill them with a constant desire to travel, typically jaunting about the Universe.

creatures.name: Janni
creatures.description: Jann are genies composed of all four elements, meaning they are not quite home on any of the Elemental Planes.
languages: one elemental language (aquan auran ignan or terran), one planar language (abyssal celestial or infernal), telepathy 100 feet

creatures.name: Jinx Eater
creatures.description: Whether kidnapped, conscripted, or having entered voluntary service, a tengu in the role of jinx eater on a ship's crew is tasked with keeping the crew free of misfortune.

creatures.name: Kapoacinth
creatures.description: Kapoacinths dwell not amid cliffs or rooftops, but beneath the sea, using their wings to swim.

creatures.name: Kasa-Obake
creatures.description: The jerky stomping outside in the middle of the night belongs to the kasa-obake.

creatures.name: Kelpie
creatures.description: Kelpies are malevolent, amphibious fey shapechangers who lure mortals to a watery grave.

creatures.name: Kholo Sergeant
creatures.description: When kholos band together to form mercenary bands or raiding parties, the strongest or most respected among them is often designated the hunt leader or sergeant.

creatures.name: Kobold Earth Diver
creatures.description: Kobold earth divers study the geology of the areas near their communities.

creatures.name: Korred
creatures.description: Korreds are reclusive fey in the form of small, hirsute individuals with long, animated manes of hair.

creatures.name: Kushtaka
creatures.description: Kushtaka, or "land otters" as they are sometimes known, are devious shapeshifters.

creatures.name: Larval Ofalth
creatures.description: Ofalths reproduce asexually. When they first hatch from their leathery eggs, larval ofalths resemble a tendril of flesh supported by spindly legs, but they soon cocoon themselves in trash, to serve as both armor and camouflage. Found in castle dung heaps, city dumps, and sewers, ofalths are living …

creatures.name: Living Thunderclap
creatures.description: A living thunderclap is a small storm cloud (sometimes in the vague shape of a humanoid) that cracks and booms with thunder. Some elementals embody aspects of air, such as smoke, lightning, and fog.

creatures.name: Lovelorn
creatures.description: A particularly macabre form of undead, these spiderlike creatures resemble still-beating hearts with rib bones for legs and tendrils of gore dangling beneath.

creatures.name: Mandragora
creatures.description: A mandragora looks like a freshly pulled tuber that has grown into the malformed shape of a child with a grotesque face and hideously bloated body.

creatures.name: Mastermind
creatures.description: Masterminds weave long-ranged plots to see their nefarious goals come to fruition, deftly manipulating those around them, turning enemies into friends and then pitting them against one another. Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: Mimic
creatures.description: Thought to be the result of a failed experiment meant to animate furniture or possibly a sinister alghollthu creation, mimics are clever monsters that can take the form of common manufactured objects.

creatures.name: Minotaur Hunter
creatures.description: A minotaur is a large humanoid with bovine features such as horns, hooves, and a layer of hair that covers their entire body.

creatures.name: Mist Stalker
creatures.description: The tentacled mist stalker shrouds itself in a cloak of mist through which its single, never-blinking eye can see with clarity, allowing it an advantage when stalking prey. Water elementals that become infused with cold or mist have increased mobility in regions outside of bodies of water.

creatures.name: Myceloid
creatures.description: The ambulatory fungus creatures called myceloids are notorious for spreading deadly purple pox, controlling creatures' minds, and devouring humanoid flesh.
languages: Telepathy 100 feet (myceloids and those afflicted by purple pox only)

creatures.name: Nightgaunt
creatures.description: Nightgaunts lurk in dreams, lying in wait to spirit away the unwary dreamer.

creatures.name: Nucol
creatures.description: Representing the fear of parasites and affliction, nucols appear as violent, pestilence-ridden boars.

creatures.name: Ogre Glutton
creatures.description: Ogre gluttons take the act of feeding to a horrific extreme, capable of extending their already cavernous mouths wide enough to gulp down a halfling.

creatures.name: Orc Gamekeeper
creatures.description: Gamekeepers live on the outskirts of the hold, usually remaining solitary and tending to animals they've captured.

creatures.name: Otyugh
creatures.description: Lords of sewers, ditches, and landfllls, otyughs are filthy monstrosities that stomp about on three massive legs in search of tasty garbage and refuse.

creatures.name: Ovinnik
creatures.description: The ovinnik is the most ferocious of house spirits, and the only one that will kill if sufficiently angered.
senses: tremorsense (imprecise) within their entire bound granary or storeroom

creatures.name: Owlbear
creatures.description: With the body of a powerful brown bear and the keen senses of an owl, the owlbear is a dangerous territorial predator, fearlessly attacking any creature that strays into its domain.

creatures.name: Pack Leader
creatures.description: Primal spellcasters with a particular affinity for animals often aid their community by training and healing their domesticated animals.

creatures.name: Palace Guard
creatures.description: Often the younger offspring of minor nobility or those from long lines of trusted staff, palace guards are in charge of defending the royal family and their inner stronghold.

creatures.name: Peryton
creatures.description: The peryton is an amalgam of horror, merging the features of a stag, wolf, and hawk.

creatures.name: Phantom Knight
creatures.description: Cavaliers and knights who died for their cause make for particularly strongwilled phantoms.

creatures.name: Pixie
creatures.description: Insatiably curious, overly excitable, and just a bit puckish, pixies are wanderers and tricksters who use their pixie dust to create all sorts of whimsical situations, as well as to defend themselves.

creatures.name: Poracha
creatures.description: Porachas are feline beasts native to the mystical Forest of Spirits in Tian Xia.

creatures.name: Qarna
creatures.description: Qarnas are secretive and tireless sentinels and scouts, patrolling the Outer Sphere's untamed wilds for evil to eliminate and keeping small communities safe from demons, devils, and worse.

creatures.name: Ratfolk Grenadier
creatures.description: Grenadiers use alchemical techniques and stealth to defend their communities.

creatures.name: Rhinoceros
creatures.description: Rhinoceroses are short-tempered, territorial, and easily startled, and these traits combined with their innate ferocity means their natural instinct when disturbed is to attack.

creatures.name: Satyr
creatures.description: To a satyr, life is a party and everyone is invited.

creatures.name: Scarecrow
creatures.description: A ramshackle collection of materials in a human shape, the scarecrow construct is indistinguishable from a normal scarecrow until it slowly creaks to life.

creatures.name: Scorpion Swarm
creatures.description: These massive, terrifying arachnids are typically 8 feet long from head to the base of the tail.

creatures.name: Sea Devil Brute
creatures.description: Physically imposing sea devils are equipped with some of the best weaponry the community can find, though most are happy to rend their foes using tooth and nail.

creatures.name: Sedacthy Marauder
creatures.description: Physically imposing sedacthies prove their status by controlling dangerous aquatic creatures like great white sharks and giant moray eels.

creatures.name: Shabti Redeemer
creatures.description: Cobbled together from broken bits of mortal souls, shabti are facsimiles of wealthy or powerful mortals seeking to escape punishment for their sins upon death.

creatures.name: Shadow
creatures.description: The mysterious undead known as shadows lurk in dark places and feed on those who stray too far from the light.

creatures.name: Shadow Spawn
creatures.description: The mysterious undead known as shadows lurk in dark places and feed on those who stray too far from the light.

creatures.name: Shae
creatures.description: Shae are wispy, tenebrous creatures native to the Plane of Shadow.

creatures.name: Shambler Troop
creatures.description: This shuffling mass of decaying flesh moves with dull but singular focus. Necromancers most often create these mindless undead as obedient, expendable servitors. Left to its own devices, a zombie seeks only to consume the living, stopping only when its rotting body can no longer hold together.

creatures.name: Shriezyx
creatures.description: Long ago, Thassilonian wizards created shriezyx in twisted experiments to act as guards and test subjects.

creatures.name: Standard Bearer
creatures.description: Within a troop, the standard bearer is a beacon of morale, cohesion, and camaraderie.

creatures.name: Stone Lion
creatures.description: Stone lions are a common sight outside of banks, temples, palaces, and even private residences.

creatures.name: Sweet Hag
creatures.description: Sweet hags use bright colors, pleasant visages, and sugary treats to draw in children and the young at heart.

creatures.name: Terror Shrike
creatures.description: Creature imported from Foundry PF2e data.

creatures.name: Tidewater Guard
creatures.description: Iruxi tidewater guards are capable fighters skilled at amphibious attacks and overpowering vessels along any shore.

creatures.name: Tiger
creatures.description: Tigers are solitary and territorial hunters, using their striped hides to blend into the forests and jungles they call home and preferring to attack with surprise.

creatures.name: Trained Bat
creatures.description: Creature imported from Foundry PF2e data.

creatures.name: Tripkee Rain-Caller
creatures.description: Influential within their communities, these wise druids hold power over water and weather.

creatures.name: Ugothol
creatures.description: Among the subtler of the alghollthu creations were the ugothols—also known as faceless stalkers.

creatures.name: Vampire Servitor
creatures.description: Vampires use their servitor pawns for infiltration and reconnaissance. Vampires are undead creatures that feed on the blood of the living.

creatures.name: Viper Swarm
creatures.description: The frightening mass of shining scales, gleaming eyes, and fangs dripping with venom that constitute a viper swarm has brought an end to many an unlucky adventurer.

creatures.name: Wasp Swarm
creatures.description: Wasp nests are made of chewed wood fibers foraged from the surrounding flora, which the wasps make into a material similar to paper.

creatures.name: Werebear
creatures.description: Unwavering conviction fills a werebear during their transformations.

creatures.name: Weretiger
creatures.description: These ferocious werecreatures stalk prey with the cunning and skill of a true apex predator.

creatures.name: Zealot of Asmodeus
creatures.description: Zealots ferret out plots against their religion and seek justice for their church's followers.

creatures.name: Aasimar Redeemer
creatures.description: Mortals whose ancestry has been influenced by celestials are known as aasimars, and angelkin, who have blood of angels coursing through their veins, are among the most common type of them.

creatures.name: Advisor
creatures.description: Trusted counselors of the court, advisors whisper words of guidance into the ears of those in power.

creatures.name: Army Ant Swarm
creatures.description: An army ant swarm is a terrifying carpet of stinging insects that devours all in its path. Ants are industrious insects that aid the natural processes of decay and renewal.

creatures.name: Avuncular Professor
creatures.description: These world-wise scholars know that there's more to life than just research.
languages: up to 4 additional languages, including at least one obscure or long-dead language that the avuncular professor likes to quote

creatures.name: Azuretzi
creatures.description: Azuretzis are sinuous, serpentine creatures with daggersharp teeth covered in brilliant blue scales and mottled purple and pink highlights that shimmer in a pareidolic approximation of leering, laughing faces.

creatures.name: Barbazu
creatures.description: Barbazus, known also as bearded devils or infantry devils, are murderous fiends who satiate their lust for annihilation by serving as the foot soldiers of Hell's armies, often leading hordes of lesser devils such as imps and lemures into battle.

creatures.name: Basidirond
creatures.description: The basidirond, also called a mindfrond, is a fungal creature that crawls about on woody tendrils.

creatures.name: Basilisk
creatures.description: The basilisk is a reptile with a nasty disposition and the ability to turn creatures to stone with its gaze.

creatures.name: Bill-Band
creatures.description: Boisterous collectives of thrill-seeking halflings join in bill-bands to engage in spirited competitions and lively sports.

creatures.name: Bog Mummy
creatures.description: The cultural practice of mummifying the dead is not the only way a body can become preserved, nor is it the only route that gives rise to these disease-spreading undead monstrosities.

creatures.name: Bogwid
creatures.description: The abhorrent combination between a toad and an octopus, a bogwid drags its bloated green body through the swamps in search of a meal for the many larvae it carries on its back.

creatures.name: Bore Worm Swarm
creatures.description: A single finger-length bore worm is unpleasant but mostly innocuous.

creatures.name: Brimorak
creatures.description: These goat-headed demons have glowing red eyes and flaming hooves.

creatures.name: Caligni Vanguard
creatures.description: Caligni children born without eyes-an extremely rare occurrence-are considered sacred to their communities.

creatures.name: Castaway
creatures.description: Be it the result of shipwreck, forcible marooning, or personal choice, surviving alone on an island long enough tends to weed out the weak of body and mind.

creatures.name: Catrina
creatures.description: Not all spirits who enter the Boneyard realize they have died.

creatures.name: Cecaelia Trapper
creatures.description: Cecaelias resemble humans with octopus tentacles instead of legs.

creatures.name: Champion of Rovagug
creatures.description: There are perhaps no mortals more anathematic to peace than champions of Rovagug or other destructive deities. Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: City Guard Squadron
creatures.description: Garrisons of professional guards are given the duties of patrolling the streets, assisting citizenry, and acting as a quick military response in times of crisis.

creatures.name: Cloaker
creatures.description: Weird and paranoid creatures that dwell in the Darklands, cloakers resemble hideous, flying manta rays.

creatures.name: Cyclops
creatures.description: The kingdoms of the cyclopes date to an age before the rise of humanity, when dragons and giants and serpentfolk ruled the world.

creatures.name: Dandasuka
creatures.description: Known as "biters" among the more powerful rakshasa castes, dandasukas serve as spies and assassins for rakshasa clans or powerful spellcasters who have found methods to bind one of these fiends to their will.

creatures.name: Demonbane Warrior
creatures.description: Elves' long lives give them centuries to delve into studies, artistry, or exploration.

creatures.name: Dero Magister
creatures.description: Dero magisters are leaders among dero society.

creatures.name: Despot
creatures.description: Despots live to amass and exploit power over others. Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: Dig-Widget
creatures.description: Thieves covet dig-widgets, specialized constructs built for infiltration.

creatures.name: Divine Warden Of Nethys
creatures.description: This divine warden serves Nethys, the neutral god of magic.

creatures.name: Djinni
creatures.description: Benevolent genies from the Plane of Air, djinn value art, culture, knowledge, and trade.

creatures.name: Dromaar Lorekeeper
creatures.description: Lorekeepers are meticulous keepers of the colorful and often misunderstood history of orcs and dromaar.

creatures.name: Emperor Cobra
creatures.description: These powerful serpents infest bogs and lowlands.

creatures.name: Ether Spider
creatures.description: Ether spiders are members of a vague taxonomy called ethereal wildlife and are deadly predators from the Ethereal Plane.

creatures.name: Eunemvro
creatures.description: Eunemvros wander the Material Plane with the sole purpose of destroying undead.

creatures.name: Fence
creatures.description: Fences make themselves indispensable to the underworld by paying for stolen goods only to resell them later, whether through a seemingly legitimate business or to a closed group of elite buyers. In the underbelly of society, the lawless reign supreme.

creatures.name: Flame Drake
creatures.description: Flame drakes dwell near volcanoes and magma, but it's not unheard of for one to drift into nearby areas like forests or wooded hills.

creatures.name: Forest Troll
creatures.description: Forest trolls are gangly giants who stalk the fringes of civilization.

creatures.name: Giant Crawling Hand
creatures.description: A giant crawling hand is the appendage of a very large creature, such as a giant.

creatures.name: Giant Frilled Lizard
creatures.description: Found in warm and tropical forests and savannas, these immense lizards are a deadly threat to the unsuspecting traveler.

creatures.name: Giant Hermit Crab
creatures.description: Lucky hermit crabs with plentiful resources can live for decades and grow to tremendous sizes, often with magical help.

creatures.name: Giant Moray Eel
creatures.description: Giant moray eels dwell in warm, tropical waters and build lairs in narrow, twisting caves made of coral.

creatures.name: Gibbering Mouther
creatures.description: Amorphous blobs of yammering mouths and oozing, fleshy sludge, gibbering mouthers are among the strangest creatures found either aboveground or below.

creatures.name: Globster
creatures.description: The tide washes ashore all manner of detritus, from harmless seaweed and shells to the rotting corpses of massive aquatic creatures.

creatures.name: Gnokesh
creatures.description: The structure of a gnokesh, a wheel of ancient tomes, refects its role in Heaven.

creatures.name: Goblin Get Gang
creatures.description: There is little more terrifying than a goblin raiding party out to take from its neighbors.

creatures.name: Grimstalker
creatures.description: These violently murderous fey have one purpose: to stealthily hunt down and slay humanoids brazen enough to dare set foot in the wilderness.

creatures.name: Grodair
creatures.description: Grodairs are bizarre fishlike creature native to the First World.

creatures.name: Harpy
creatures.description: Harpies are amalgamations of human and bird, resembling feral humans with wings, talons, and sharp teeth.

creatures.name: Harpy Skeleton
creatures.description: A live harpy's song is compelling and captivating; a harpy skeleton's is anything but, rending the target's mind. Almost any creature that had bones in life and leaves them behind in death can become a shambling, undead skeleton-humanoids, beasts, aberrations, fey, and more.

creatures.name: Hieracosphinx
creatures.description: Lacking the intelligence of their kin, falcon-headed hieracosphinxes are cowardly, cruel-hearted creatures.

creatures.name: Hippopotamus
creatures.description: Typical adult hippos move quickly on land and attack stealthily in the water. Hippopotamuses, or hippos for short, are semiaquatic animals that spend most of their time in rivers and lakes, but can thrive on land.

creatures.name: Ice Golem
creatures.description: Ice golems are either carved from massive blocks of ice, or pieced together from individual components that are then fused together through heat and refreezing.

creatures.name: Ice Worm Swarm
creatures.description: A single finger-length bore worm is unpleasant but mostly innocuous.

creatures.name: Jaathoom
creatures.description: Possessing all the subtlety and elegance of air itself, the jaathooms of the Plane of Air operate within dreams, nightmares, and time.
languages: (Can't Speak Any Language); Cloud of Visions

creatures.name: Kodama
creatures.description: A kodama is a type of kami who inhabits a tree.

creatures.name: Lampad
creatures.description: Lampads protect dark, hidden places underground.

creatures.name: Lava Worm Swarm
creatures.description: A single finger-length bore worm is unpleasant but mostly innocuous.

creatures.name: Lawbringer Warpriest
creatures.description: Mortals whose ancestry has been influenced by archons are called lawbringers.

creatures.name: Leucrotta
creatures.description: The vile and vicious offspring of a demon lord and a monstrous species of hyenas, leucrottas are intelligent and cruel beasts.

creatures.name: Living Landslide
creatures.description: Living landslides resemble humanoids made of earth and gravel.

creatures.name: Living Waterfall
creatures.description: Living waterfalls are humanoid-shaped columns of churning water.

creatures.name: Living Whirlwind
creatures.description: A living whirlwind resembles a dust devil with a vague mouth and eyes formed among the dust and debris swirling within it.

creatures.name: Living Wildfire
creatures.description: Living wildfires appear as humanoids made of living fire, burning everything they come across for the pure joy of it.

creatures.name: Lurker In Light
creatures.description: These malicious and strange fey appear as fairies about 2 feet tall, with insectile wings, large eyes, and bulbous heads.

creatures.name: Mage-Eater Worm Swarm
creatures.description: A single finger-length bore worm is unpleasant but mostly innocuous.

creatures.name: Megatherium
creatures.description: Megatheriums dwell deep within ancient forests and humid jungles.

creatures.name: Mountaineer
creatures.description: Mountaineers usually travel solo, but some guide expeditions into dangerous terrain. Explorers are often well-equipped and well-trained for any type of hazard and are eager to lead others into the wild.

creatures.name: Namorrodor
creatures.description: Namorrodor stalk the night, craving the flesh of the living.

creatures.name: Necral Worm Swarm
creatures.description: A single finger-length bore worm is unpleasant but mostly innocuous.

creatures.name: Necromancer
creatures.description: Defiling the natural order and spitting in the face of convention, the necromancer remains dutifully committed to understanding what forces await beyond the mortal boundaries of life and death.

creatures.name: Ochre Jelly
creatures.description: Ochre jellies are animate masses of protoplasm with a sickly combination of yellow, orange, and brown hues.

creatures.name: Ogre Spider
creatures.description: These terrifying creatures grow as large as elephants.

creatures.name: Orca
creatures.description: While many know orcas as "killer whales," they're actually the largest species of dolphin.

creatures.name: Ostiarius
creatures.description: Ostiariuses, as emissaries of the velstracs, tend to the portals between the Netherworld and the Universe.

creatures.name: Penanggalan
creatures.description: Penanggalans feed on the blood and entrails of the young.

creatures.name: Plague Doctor
creatures.description: These healers, often seen wearing distinctive masks and burning powders to defend against airborne plagues, are as much feared as they are respected.

creatures.name: Polar Bear
creatures.description: Polar bears are known for their pale fur, with black eyes, lips, and toe pads.

creatures.name: Poltergeist
creatures.description: When a creature dies, and for whatever reason its spirit is unable or unwilling to leave the site of its death, that spirit may manifest as a poltergeist: a restless, invisible spirit that is still able to manipulate physical objects.

creatures.name: Razzle Dazzler
creatures.description: The razzle dazzler is a particularly beguiling sort of entertainer who specializes in delighting with illusions and showy displays.

creatures.name: Redcap
creatures.description: Redcaps are sadistic and capricious fey who thrill in bloodletting and murder.

creatures.name: Sabosan
creatures.description: Sabosans are intelligent, bat-like humanoids who live in warm forests and drink the blood of other creatures, particularly people.

creatures.name: Shrine Skelm
creatures.description: The unquestioned authority that a priest wields, with influence over even the most intimate details of a congregation's lives, is the ultimate prize for a shrine skelm.

creatures.name: Skaveling
creatures.description: One must perform a hideous ritual to make a skaveling.

creatures.name: Skinstitch
creatures.description: Skinstitches' hulking frames are made from flayed humanoid skin stuffed with straw and poisonous insects.

creatures.name: Sniper
creatures.description: A keen eye, a steady hand, and a killer instinct combine to form a ruthless, emotionless harbinger of death.

creatures.name: Spiny Eurypterid
creatures.description: Spiny eurypterids are aggressive, tenacious, and utterly unwilling to stop chasing prey, even to their own detriment.

creatures.name: Spirit Priest
creatures.description: While dwarves worship gods like most any other ancestry, many dwarves also look to the spirits of the world for guidance and support.

creatures.name: Stalker Automaton
creatures.description: Stalker automatons house the minds of skilled hunters, scouts, and assassins in bodies patterned after the great hunters of the natural world.
languages: One other language the stalker knew in life usually jistkan, Telepathy 60 feet

creatures.name: Storm Hag
creatures.description: Ruthless and temperamental, storm hags are the personification of a storm's rage.

creatures.name: Subaquatic Marauder
creatures.description: A new breed of pirate, these raiders strike at unsuspecting ships from beneath the cover of the waves.

creatures.name: Tiger Topiary
creatures.description: Tiger topiaries are most often found within the Impossible Lands and Tian Xia, adding a dangerous appearance to an enchanting garden.

creatures.name: Tomb Jelly
creatures.description: Tomb jellies are animate masses of protoplasm with a sickly combination of yellow, gray, and black hues.

creatures.name: Tomb Raider
creatures.description: Great treasure awaits those willing to explore the hazardous depths of ancient tombs and forgotten dungeons.

creatures.name: Tournament Combatant
creatures.description: Tournaments attract martial artists like moths to a flame, with participants crossing vast distances for the chance to test their might. Martial artists strive to master the art of hand-to-hand fighting.

creatures.name: Troll
creatures.description: Common trolls are dim-witted, gangly giants who stalk the fringes of civilization.

creatures.name: Umbral Gnome Rockwarden
creatures.description: Umbral gnome rockwardens follow druidic teachings and commune with the natural elemental influences and denizens of the Darklands.

creatures.name: Unsanctioned Sheriff
creatures.description: Believing the ends justify the means, the unsanctioned sheriff is unafraid to use others for their own gain, through bribes, manipulation, or force. These lone wolves have an aura of mystery, bravado, and swagger.

creatures.name: Urdefhan Tormentor
creatures.description: Urdefhan spellcasters with a knack for the divine are invariably unholy worshippers of daemonkind, typically taking on one of Abaddon's Four Apocalypse Riders as their patron deity.

creatures.name: Velociraptor Pack
creatures.description: Velociraptors are clever pack hunters who generally subsist off small herbivores but are not afraid to take on larger prey as a group.

creatures.name: Venedaemon
creatures.description: Those killed by magic or killed in the pursuit of magic can arise as venedaemons.

creatures.name: Vescavor Swarm
creatures.description: The most common and numerous vescavors are the raving swarms.

creatures.name: Veteran War Horse
creatures.description: A veteran war horse has not only been extensively trained but has also experienced battle several times.

creatures.name: Vloriak
creatures.description: Vloriaks rise from the sinful souls of those whose acts of sabotage resulted in despair and terror, and they delight in the destruction of mortal artifice.

creatures.name: Vordine
creatures.description: The vast city of Dis trains endless legions of vordines to guard the upper layers of Hell and march across the planes at the archdevils' orders.

creatures.name: Watchmage
creatures.description: A watchmage uses a mix of magic and martial training to enforce the law.

creatures.name: Witchwarg
creatures.description: Witchwargs are related to wargs, but are larger, smarter, and far more dangerous.

creatures.name: Wizard Sponge
creatures.description: This strange type of fungus colony takes its name from its initial purpose: the wizard who first developed it sought a means to keep her tower clean from residue both mundane and magical.

creatures.name: Wizard Sponge (Crypt)
creatures.description: This strange type of fungus colony takes its name from its initial purpose: the wizard who first developed it sought a means to keep her tower clean from residue both mundane and magical.

creatures.name: Wizard Sponge (Fey Domain)
creatures.description: The Fey Domain Wizard Sponge develops a strangely capricious growth pattern despite being mindless, such as growing in the pockets of a creature's clothing before devouring it.

creatures.name: Wizard Sponge (Fiendish Temple)
creatures.description: This strange type of fungus colony takes its name from its initial purpose: the wizard who first developed it sought a means to keep her tower clean from residue both mundane and magical.

creatures.name: Wizard Sponge (Toxic Lair)
creatures.description: If this variety is eaten, it deals @Damage[1d6[persistent,poison]].

creatures.name: Wizard Sponge (Underwater)
creatures.description: This strange type of fungus colony takes its name from its initial purpose: the wizard who first developed it sought a means to keep her tower clean from residue both mundane and magical.

creatures.name: Yamah
creatures.description: Yamahs are devoted to protecting others from magic used for evil, particularly the kind that traps souls or controls a creature's free will.

creatures.name: Yeti
creatures.description: Nearly a myth, a yeti is rarely seen—and even when it is, it is often too late.

creatures.name: Aapoph Granitescale
creatures.description: The mutated aapophs dubbed granitescales have bulky frames covered in hard gray plates.

creatures.name: Abandoned Zealot
creatures.description: Once devout followers whose faith proved false, abandoned zealots return from the doorstep of the afterlife after being denied the eternal reward they expected.

creatures.name: Ahuizotl
creatures.description: The ahuizotl is a vicious, semiaquatic predator that resembles a hideous cross between a badger and an otter, with disturbingly web-fngered paws supplemented by a ffth hand at the end of a long, serpentine tail.

creatures.name: Ankylosaurus
creatures.description: Squat, heavily armored quadrupeds, ankylosauruses are stubborn and irascible.

creatures.name: Annis Hag
creatures.description: Annis hags are brutal torturers and murderers, delighting in the musical screams and tasty flesh of young creatures and those who are pure of heart.

creatures.name: Awakened Tree
creatures.description: Arboreal regents and other wielders of powerful primal magic grant temporary sentience to trees in order to protect the forest.

creatures.name: Babau
creatures.description: The babau is an assassin, a murderer, and a sadist-certainly not unusual traits, yet their penchant for stealth and surprise sets them apart from their kin.

creatures.name: Baobhan Sith
creatures.description: Undeath is rare in the fey realm of the First World, for that plane's infusion with the raw power of life makes undead uncomfortable at best.

creatures.name: Bauble Beast
creatures.description: The reclusive and deceptive creatures called bauble beasts create false jewelry as a byproduct of their extremely bizarre magical digestive systems.

creatures.name: Beetle Carapace
creatures.description: Created from the exoskeleton of a giant stag beetle, this mindless husk can cut a foe in half using its powerful mandibles.

creatures.name: Big Boss Goblin
creatures.description: Big boss goblins order members of their band around and deal with any longshanks they come across—sometimes via violence or diplomacy, and sometimes by ordering another goblin to deal with them (in ways that the big boss goblin approves of if they work or claims were awful ideas they never would ha…

creatures.name: Blizzardborn
creatures.description: Freezing and thawing in a near-constant loop, blizzardborn resemble humanoid forms composed of a mixture of partially melted snow and sleet.

creatures.name: Blodeuwedd
creatures.description: The mysterious blodeuwedds dwell in those parts of the world where the boundaries between the Material Plane and the First World have worn thin, or around portals between the two planes.

creatures.name: Bodach
creatures.description: Creature imported from Foundry PF2e data.

creatures.name: Bone Mother
creatures.description: The bone mother—a warren's oracle—cuts an imposing figure.

creatures.name: Bralani
creatures.description: Bralanis embody the freedom of fair competition, embracing opportunities to test their skills against worthy foes and bring mortals together with the thrill of good-natured sports and contests.

creatures.name: Caligni Caller
creatures.description: Although the mysterious demigods known as the Forsaken disappeared eons ago, many calignis continue to follow their ancient traditions of worship.

creatures.name: Captain Of The Guard
creatures.description: The captain of the guard leads a troop of soldiers who serve as security forces for a powerful individual, most often a high-ranking noble or very rich merchant, though this stat block could also represent a lowerranking captain of the guard for the leader of a nation.

creatures.name: Catfolk Name Collector
creatures.description: Name collectors are esteemed touchstones of catfolk culture.

creatures.name: Cave Bear
creatures.description: Larger, stronger, and far more aggressive than its smaller cousins, the cave bear is a behemoth that avoids civilized lands, preferring to dwell in remote places.

creatures.name: Cave Giant
creatures.description: Cave giants are the vicious loners of the giant world.

creatures.name: Cavern Troll
creatures.description: Insatiable scavengers, cavern trolls stalk the eternal gloom of the Darklands, consuming all in their path—even rocks and minerals.

creatures.name: Ceustodaemon
creatures.description: Ceustodaemons are formed from the souls of vile mortals, particularly those who took efforts to hasten their own death, their willingness shaping them into daemonic servants.

creatures.name: Choral
creatures.description: Choral angels are incredible singers who fill the halls of Nirvana with pious chants and sacred hymns.

creatures.name: Chouchin-Obake
creatures.description: A chouchin-obake takes the shape of a paper lantern with a horizontal split near the bottom from which a long tongue protrudes.

creatures.name: Clockwork Soldier
creatures.description: These diligent machines guard their assigned posts tirelessly.

creatures.name: Divine Warden of Pharasma
creatures.description: This divine warden serves Pharasma, the goddess of birth, death, and fate. It protects a temple or shrine to the Lady of Graves.

creatures.name: Drider
creatures.description: The first fleshwarping process mastered by the drow remains both their most successful and most infamous: the drider.

creatures.name: Dwarf Battalion
creatures.description: Dwarven soldiers make formidable units, capable of holding their own against most enemies.

creatures.name: Elananx
creatures.description: These strange, fey felines resemble large, broad bobcats from a distance, but a closer view reveals something amiss.

creatures.name: Ettin
creatures.description: Two heads aren't always better than one. The slovenly, violent giants known as ettins are proof enough of that. The origin of these two-headed brutes is unknown, but few who encounter ettins ponder their beginnings for long. Rather, the first priority for most creatures during such a confrontation …

creatures.name: Evangelist
creatures.description: Evangelists, the velstracs' unofficial ambassadors, roam the farthest reaches of the planes to spread the word of their kind's abhorrent belief in perfection through pain.

creatures.name: Executioner
creatures.description: Executioners carry out sentences from cruel tyrants and legitimate rulers alike.

creatures.name: Fire Jellyfish Swarm
creatures.description: While individually one of these fist-sized jellyfish is merely a nuisance, in great numbers, fire jellyfish can form into dangerous swarms.

creatures.name: Gadgeteer
creatures.description: Few are as prepared to be unprepared as a gadgeteer, who are masters at building seemingly impossible creations out of almost anything.

creatures.name: Gamekeeper
creatures.description: Gamekeepers know every beast that walks, slithers, flies, or swims in their territory and where to find them; try to keep up with the pack if you think you can.

creatures.name: Giant Mosquito
creatures.description: These horrifically enlarged versions of the common mosquito often prey upon megafauna like dinosaurs and other large creatures, but they won't turn down a chance to drink the blood of a smaller target-such as a humanoid.

creatures.name: Giant Tarantula
creatures.description: Tarantulas are ambush predators, but will attack prey in the open. Few everyday vermin inspire as much dread as the infamous spider.

creatures.name: Gnome Conservationist
creatures.description: The natural world boasts unsurpassed beauty and countless variety, and the gnome conservationist is devoted to experiencing and protecting as many examples of this grandeur as they possibly can.

creatures.name: Gourd Leshy Witch
creatures.description: Some spooky leshies learn the ways of occult spellcasting from strange spirits of nature that lurk just out of sight or under cover of darkness.

creatures.name: Graveknight Captain
creatures.description: Graveknight captains once proudly led squads of troops in battle. When a fearsome combatant falls in battle, the warrior's vengeful spirit can sometimes fuse with their armor, creating a graveknight.

creatures.name: Gurgist Mauler
creatures.description: Humans that become mortics are known as gurgists.

creatures.name: Halfling Smuggler
creatures.description: Halfling smugglers are sought out for their ability to deftly navigate the shadowy underworld to move illicit goods and information. Halflings thrive on simple pleasures—having a pint at the pub or warming their feet by the hearth.

creatures.name: Hobgoblin Battalion
creatures.description: A hobgoblin battalion is usually composed of multiple soldiers and archers commanded by a single captain, although every battalion also has its own internal hierarchy that clearly defines the chain of command should the current captain fall in battle.

creatures.name: Hobgoblin General
creatures.description: Hobgoblin generals serve as leaders of entire armies and rulers of hobgoblin settlements.

creatures.name: Hodag
creatures.description: Because they're often fantastically described in the wild tall tales of loggers or miners, hodags are considered by many to exist only in local folklore.

creatures.name: Hungry Ghost
creatures.description: Hungry ghosts arise from those who didn't receive proper burials or whose graves were neglected.

creatures.name: Hydra
creatures.description: Hydras are multiheaded, foul-tempered serpentine beasts with voracious appetites, widely feared for their regeneration abilities.

creatures.name: Iguanodon
creatures.description: Iguanodons are large, herbivorous dinosaurs that inhabit swamps and forests where they browse on the abundant vegetation.

creatures.name: Interrogator
creatures.description: Interrogators use pain and intimidation against prisoners and other helpless victims to force "confessions." Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: Iron Hag
