creatures.description: Iron hags are kidnappers, targeting those too young to even remember to fight back.

creatures.name: Jungle Drake
creatures.description: Believed to be related to horned dragons, jungle drakes are dangerous hunters equipped with a debilitating venom delivered by a large barbed stinger or their noxious phlegm.

creatures.name: Kurobozu
creatures.description: A monk who violated their monastic vows in life might arise after death to become an undead horror known as a kurobozu.

creatures.name: Lamia
creatures.description: Just as they were cursed long ago, lamias can inflict a curse on those they touch, clouding the victim's mind and pushing conscious thought toward animalistic instincts.

creatures.name: Legbreaker
creatures.description: Criminal organizations are always happy to loan out money at exorbitant rates, and their legbreakers are always happy to collect. In the underbelly of society, the lawless reign supreme.

creatures.name: Line Infantry
creatures.description: Infantry are the backbone of most armies. These professional soldiers, marked by matching uniforms, straightforward tactics, and the drive to follow well-practiced orders, make up the bulk of most military forces—but are often considered the most expendable. A military serves to defend and fight on…

creatures.name: Lunar Naga
creatures.description: Lunar nagas are skilled diviners and astrologers whose dark scales and white hair sparkle with motes of light that mimic the night skies to which they are forever drawn.

creatures.name: Maftet Guardian
creatures.description: Maftets are hawk-winged humanoids with leonine lower bodies that dwell in ancient ruins and cities thought lost, typically in desert or mountain regions.

creatures.name: Manticore (Quill Tail)
creatures.description: A manticore is a monstrous amalgamation of lion, dragon, and human with a penchant for feasting on human flesh.

creatures.name: Manticore (Scorpion Tail)
creatures.description: A manticore is a monstrous amalgamation of lion, dragon, and human with a penchant for feasting on human flesh.

creatures.name: Mi-Go
creatures.description: Mi-gos are both scientists and colonists, yet their extraterrestrial nature and malevolent motives color their investigations with cruelty.

creatures.name: Monster Hunter
creatures.description: Some mercenaries eschew the complications of politics and fealty and just get paid to hunt monsters.

creatures.name: Mountain Guardian
creatures.description: Those who have unlocked the secrets of opening a kinetic gate to an elemental plane within themselves wield immense power over that element.

creatures.name: Mummy Guardian
creatures.description: The majority of mummy guardians were created by cruel and selfish masters to serve as guardians to protect their tombs from intruders.
languages: plus any one language they knew while alive

creatures.name: Nightmare
creatures.description: Nightmares are flaming equine harbingers of death.

creatures.name: Ocean Nomad
creatures.description: Adventurers may need passage on a swift vessel, or they might face danger from raiders at sea or in coastal settlements.

creatures.name: Owb
creatures.description: Commonly called owbs, or lesser owbs by more powerful owb prophets, most of these mysterious creatures focus on manipulating caligni culture.
languages: cant speak any languages, Telepathy 100 feet

creatures.name: Phalanx Formation
creatures.description: A proper phalanx formation requires the simultaneous use of spear and shield in order to attack enemies near and far while defending each other.

creatures.name: Priest of Sarenrae
creatures.description: Cloistered priests safeguard their temples and communities. They are the stalwart keepers of their god's tenets, devoted to spreading the word. Their guidance or healing services come at the cost of a donation. Religions inspire devout individuals to uphold their tenets.

creatures.name: Puppeteer
creatures.description: On the surface, puppeteers are simply providers of entertainment to the masses.

creatures.name: Reckless Scientist
creatures.description: It's the reckless scientist's job to break the rules of reality, no matter the cost. Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: Revenant
creatures.description: Revenants are obsessed, undead stalkers who arise from their own murders and are driven by only one thing: revenge against their killers.
languages: any one spoken in life by their murderer (typically Common)

creatures.name: Rhu-Chalik
creatures.description: Also called void wanderers, rhu-chaliks can survive the depths of space indefinitely, passing between worlds over the eons and scouting those planets for the Dominion of the Black.

creatures.name: Rocketeer
creatures.description: It takes a very specific personality to strap on a tank filled with a highly flammable alchemical substance and set it afire to launch oneself into the sky, so it is perhaps unsurprising that most rocketeers are reckless and bombastic individuals who delight in the theatrics inherent in their craft.

creatures.name: Sage
creatures.description: The greatest knowledge comes from experience.

creatures.name: Sand Sentry
creatures.description: This creature of pure sand moves with an eerie grace, shifting back and forth between a detailed likeness of a human and a muted and featureless bipedal form.

creatures.name: Sargassum Heap
creatures.description: A sargassum heap is a mass of semi-intelligent seaweed that floats through the ocean, luring in its victims with hallucinogenic spores.

creatures.name: Scythe Tree
creatures.description: Malevolent, vicious, and cruel, scythe trees pose as normal trees near deep forest paths, where they wait to slaughter passersby.

creatures.name: Sea Devil Baron
creatures.description: Unlike in many cultures, the highest-ranking sea devils are expected not just to wade into battle alongside their brethren, but to actively lead the campaign.

creatures.name: Sea Drake
creatures.description: Long and slender, sea drakes have fins down the length of their backs and webbing between their talons, making them just as adapted for gliding through ocean waves as the skies above.

creatures.name: Sedacthy Speaker
creatures.description: High-ranking sedacthies are expected to both plan campaigns and wade into battle.

creatures.name: Shambler
creatures.description: Shamblers are cunning carnivorous plants that resemble heaps of wet, rotting vegetation.

creatures.name: Ship Captain
creatures.description: The captain is the ultimate authority on a vessel, responsible for the livelihood and well-being of everyone on the ship. Adventurers may need passage on a swift vessel, or they might face danger from raiders at sea or in coastal settlements.

creatures.name: Skrik Nettle
creatures.description: The expansive skies of the First World are home to skrik nettles-delicate, bizarre-looking creatures that resemble large jellyfishes.

creatures.name: Skull Peeler
creatures.description: Skull peelers, despite their ferocious and well-deserved moniker, are considered by many to be downright adorable, especially when viewed from a safe distance.

creatures.name: Smilodon
creatures.description: Smilodons are large saber-toothed cats, apex predators that are significantly more muscular and broader than the other species of big cats.

creatures.name: Smoke Creeper
creatures.description: Smoke creepers shift and modify their appearance as they float through the Plane of Air, but they prefer to take on a vaguely insectile form with wings trailing choking fumes and glowing red eyes. Some elementals embody aspects of air, such as smoke, lightning, and fog.

creatures.name: Spy
creatures.description: Any number of nobles could be spies—a beloved confidante of the queen or even the court jester.

creatures.name: Striding Fire
creatures.description: An embodiment of the speed and chaos of a spreading blaze, a striding fire appears as a lithe and long-limbed humanoid composed of shifting-hued flames churning around a skeleton-like framework.

creatures.name: Sulfur Zombie
creatures.description: Glowing with a dull amber light, these odious creatures spawn from a combination of baleful magic and fire.

creatures.name: Tengu Bladesmith
creatures.description: With the tengu diaspora spreading across much of Golarion, their knowledge and tradition of blade crafting has been passed through the generations to those who show interest and aptitude.

creatures.name: Terra-Cotta Soldier
creatures.description: A single terra-cotta warrior stands 6 feet tall and weighs 600 pounds.

creatures.name: Umasi
creatures.description: Tales tell of strange humanoids who harvest appendages and organs from other creatures to graft onto their own perpetually decaying bodies.

creatures.name: Vampire Count
creatures.description: Vampire counts rule their demesnes and subjects through a mix of fear and cruelty. Vampires are undead creatures that feed on the blood of the living.

creatures.name: Verdurous Ooze
creatures.description: Verdurous oozes are found in temperate forests, warm jungles, or other places where plant life grows in abundance.

creatures.name: Veteran Noble
creatures.description: Veteran nobles have survived battles in the past, both social and physical. These movers and shakers are often the patrons or mentors of bold adventurers. The denizens of a noble court are the most powerful people in a civilization, primed with wealth, station, and authority above the common people.

creatures.name: Vrykolakas Spawn
creatures.description: Vrykolakas unleash their spawn upon the world to spread terror, plague, and suffering and to draw attention away from their masters.

creatures.name: Vulpinal
creatures.description: Vulpinals serve as the cunning, clever musicians and minstrels of the agathions.

creatures.name: Warden
creatures.description: Wardens are the chief guardians of borders and frontiers.

creatures.name: White Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The most feral and least intelligent of all the chromatic dragons, white dragons are brutish, predatory, and chiefly motivated by self-preservation.

creatures.name: White Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: The most feral and least intelligent of all the chromatic dragons, white dragons are brutish, predatory, and chiefly motivated by self-preservation.

creatures.name: Wihsaak
creatures.description: These gaunt and insectile sahkils torment their foes by focusing on a widespread fear of insects and creeping, crawling vermin.

creatures.name: Will-o'-Wisp
creatures.description: Malevolent balls of colored light, will-o'-wisps haunt lonely marshes and forests, where they lure unsuspecting travelers into danger.

creatures.name: Witchwyrd
creatures.description: Witchwyrds conceal most of their faces, leaving only their eyes unmasked.
languages: One or more planar languages, Truespeech

creatures.name: Wood Giant
creatures.description: Wood giants are the denizens and protectors of the deepest, most primeval forests in the world.

creatures.name: Wood Golem
creatures.description: Wood golems are often given vaguely humanoid shapes, almost as if cobbled together from scraps of firewood and discarded burls.

creatures.name: Woolly Rhinoceros
creatures.description: Even bulkier than their non-woolly cousins, these rhinoceroses have a shaggy pelt of long, thick fur and a huge, crescent-shaped horn.

creatures.name: Wraith
creatures.description: Wraiths are malevolent undead who drain life and shun light.

creatures.name: Wyvern
creatures.description: A wyvern is a venomous drake with a well-earned reputation for impatience and aggression.

creatures.name: Xill
creatures.description: Xills visit the Material Plane, where they're often known as ethereal stalkers, primarily to maraud and to kidnap creatures back to their native Ethereal Plane.

creatures.name: Zecui
creatures.description: These chitinous entities can only grow to adulthood within a host creature, usually a humanoid or larger animal.

creatures.name: Zombie Hulk
creatures.description: These towering horrors rise from monstrous, gigantic corpses. A zombie's only desire is to consume the living. Unthinking and ever-shambling harbingers of death, zombies stop only when they're destroyed.

creatures.name: Aesra
creatures.description: Despite their flaming blades and ring of unblinking eyes, aesras are the diplomats of peace among the archons, preferring justice via compromise and mutual benefit rather than justice by the sword.

creatures.name: Alghollthu Master
creatures.description: Aboleths form the core of alghollthu society, and while they are the "common folk" of their own societies, they see themselves as masters of all others.

creatures.name: Allosaurus
creatures.description: The allosaurus is a huge bipedal dinosaur built for hunting larger prey.

creatures.name: Aqudel
creatures.description: Aqudels are obsessed with control. They consider everything around them open to manipulation, and their position in the alghollthu hierarchy leads them to lord over networks of ugothols or small, established societies that they often bully and exploit from the shadows. Aqudels answer only to vidile…

creatures.name: Arboreal Reaper
creatures.description: While some forests have an idyllic, peaceful quality to them, others feel distinctly unfriendly or even sinister-these forests are the favored haunts of arboreal reapers.

creatures.name: Azarketi Tide Tamer
creatures.description: The most ambitious and capable azarketis become tide tamers, learning how to speak with and train aquatic animals.

creatures.name: Bandit Gang
creatures.description: Cutthroat crews of criminals form gangs to take out larger scores and intimidate their victims. Bandit gangs are often accompanied by a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-npc-core.Actor.Gang Leader]. In the underbelly of society, the lawless reign supreme.

creatures.name: Black Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Murderous tyrants of marshes, swamps, bogs, and fens, black dragons terrorize their domains with intense fervor.

creatures.name: Black Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Murderous tyrants of marshes, swamps, bogs, and fens, black dragons terrorize their domains with intense fervor.

creatures.name: Black Pudding
creatures.description: Most often found below ground, these oozes scour caves for objects to dissolve with their corrosive secretions.

creatures.name: Brass Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Brass dragons are whimsical tricksters who delight in humor and play.

creatures.name: Brass Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Brass dragons are whimsical tricksters who delight in humor and play.

creatures.name: Caulborn
creatures.description: Telepathic prophets and historians, caulborn gather in small cabals for protection and camaraderie, using their collective brainpower to unravel cosmic conundrums.

creatures.name: Champion of Shelyn
creatures.description: Champions are bastions of their deities' virtues and are living beacons for their causes.

creatures.name: Chuul
creatures.description: Also known as chu'ulothis, these large, armor-plated, crustacean predators lurk beneath the surface of pools, mires, and ponds, waiting to snatch up prey with their massive claws before paralyzing them with their tentacles.

creatures.name: Coarti
creatures.description: The burning-winged coartis are marked by the onerous contracts they agreed to and bound to Hell by the machinations of a legalistic devil, usually a phistophilus.

creatures.name: Con Rit
creatures.description: The con rit is a hulking aquatic centipede that has traded its hundreds of legs for just as many fins.

creatures.name: Crossroads Guardian
creatures.description: When a village is threatened by a horde of bandits but there's no one to help, when a monastery's divine relic must be guarded but the last monk is on their deathbed, when the world can be saved only through the use of an artifact no one alive remembers, nature responds by creating a crossroads gua…

creatures.name: Culdewen
creatures.description: Perpetually cursed to hunger and failure, a culdewen plies waterways in search of appealing fish to catch-the bigger the better.

creatures.name: Cult Leader
creatures.description: A career of mystical accomplishments combined with a lifetime of subterfuge and intimidation has elevated this occultist to a powerful position.

creatures.name: Dark Naga
creatures.description: Dark nagas are wicked, jealous creatures that crave power and wealth.

creatures.name: Dedicated Druid
creatures.description: Individuals who dedicate their life to the protection and preservation of the natural world often become druids.

creatures.name: Demonologist
creatures.description: Demonologists can pull a creature from the Outer Rifts and bend it to their will... for a time. Hidden secrets and occult powers have an irresistible lure for many. Since the majority of these NPCs are spellcasters, consider using alternative spell lists to adjust their themes.

creatures.name: Departmental Chair
creatures.description: All the departmental chair really wants is a chance to quietly do their research.

creatures.name: Drainberry Bush
creatures.description: Drainberry bushes are floating bushes that originate from the First World, with long, thorny vines and dense clusters of bright-red berries.
languages: Can't speak any language, Telepathy 100 feet

creatures.name: Dullahan
creatures.description: Riding on a horse as black as night, the headless hunter known as the dullahan tracks down and takes the heads of those they deem unfit to continue living.

creatures.name: Dweomercat
creatures.description: Dweomercats are magically gifted, four-eyed felines from the First World, where they prey upon other creatures and feed on their primal energy.

creatures.name: Elasmosaurus
creatures.description: Elasmosauruses are long-necked, primeval reptiles that dwell in deep oceans and seas.

creatures.name: Elephant
creatures.description: Those who live near elephants have learned to be wary of angering the beasts, but even with precautions in place, elephants sometimes still rampage.

creatures.name: Empress Bore Worm
creatures.description: When an area lacks sufficient food, the bore worms in a swarm become increasingly agitated and desperate.

creatures.name: Empress Ice Worm
creatures.description: When an area lacks sufficient food, the bore worms in a swarm become increasingly agitated and desperate.

creatures.name: Empress Lava Worm
creatures.description: When an area lacks sufficient food, the bore worms in a swarm become increasingly agitated and desperate.

creatures.name: Empress Mage-Eater Worm
creatures.description: When an area lacks sufficient food, the bore worms in a swarm become increasingly agitated and desperate.

creatures.name: Empress Necral Worm
creatures.description: When an area lacks sufficient food, the bore worms in a swarm become increasingly agitated and desperate.

creatures.name: Experienced Hound
creatures.description: An experienced hound has been on dozens of hunts. They are often raised from pups to catch the scents of certain animals and deliver their bodies unharmed when taken down by their owners. An experienced hound could accompany a hunter or other NPC who specializes in tracking down prey.

creatures.name: Fleshwarper
creatures.description: Cruel scientists called fleshwarpers create horrors from the flesh of others. Many desire to push science forward, but others need only grotesque glee. Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: Fortune Eater
creatures.description: The fickle finger of fate has quashed hundreds of fledgling adventurers, sometimes in a particularly memorable or ignominious manner.
languages: languages spoken by the adventurers (typically common, dwarven, elven, and goblin)

creatures.name: Frost Drake
creatures.description: Frost drakes pose an immense danger in the frozen reaches they call home, where they roam far and wide to hunt for prey such as caribou, wolves, small bears, tundra-dwelling people, and even lone frost giants.

creatures.name: Gang Leader
creatures.description: Gang leaders direct cutthroats, killers, thieves, and toughs. The gang leader often appears alongside a bandit gang or other criminals. Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: Giant Animated Statue
creatures.description: Giant animated statues' increased size and power make them most useful in large vaults, spacious chambers, or outdoor locations.

creatures.name: Giant Jellyfish
creatures.description: Unlike its smaller cousins, the giant jellyfish is an active predator that chases down its prey through reefs or open water.

creatures.name: Greater Barghest
creatures.description: Once a barghest has eaten enough to grow into a greater barghest, it typically seeks a method to leave the Material Plane and return to the Abyss, there joining other fiends as yet another of that plane's horrors.

creatures.name: Greater Shadow
creatures.description: Shadows that spend long amounts of time on the Netherworld and absorb its magic become greater shadows. The mysterious undead known as shadows lurk in dark places and feed on those who stray too far from the light.

creatures.name: Gunwitch
creatures.description: As wielders of both occult power and firearms, gunwitches pride themselves in using both unconventional weapons and obscure magic.

creatures.name: Halfling Yarnspinner
creatures.description: Yarnspinners weave captivating tales that entertain, educate, and preserve the rich heritage of the halfling people across generations. Halflings thrive on simple pleasures—having a pint at the pub or warming their feet by the hearth.

creatures.name: Hārakasura
creatures.description: Hārakasuras appear as two f gures that are intrinsically intertwined, moving in complete tandem.

creatures.name: Heavy Cavalry
creatures.description: A charging band of knights, mounted upon heavy warhorses and clad in steel plate, is a sight to be feared on the battlefield.

creatures.name: Hellcat
creatures.description: Hellcats are devious predators native to the fiery pits of Hell.
languages: Can't speak any language, Fiendish telepathy 100 feet

creatures.name: Hill Giant
creatures.description: Selfish and crude, these rough-skinned raiders are bullies and ultimately cowards.

creatures.name: Hound of Tindalos
creatures.description: Lean and athirst, the hounds of Tindalos are drawn to those who tamper with the flow of time, travel through time, or use magic or rare alchemical drugs to send their thoughts or perception backward or forward in time.

creatures.name: Hunter
creatures.description: As is reflected in the many depictions of the elk-headed Erastil, god of the hunt, the hunter is very much a creature of the forest, known by the forest and familiar with every aspect of it.

creatures.name: Invidiak
creatures.description: For most demons, possessing a creature requires a ritual, magic item, or condition, but for invidiaks, who lack a body, possession is akin to donning a suit of clothing. Known also as shadow demons, invidiaks are formed from envious mortal souls.

creatures.name: Jabali
creatures.description: The proud and brazen jabalis of the Plane of Earth value physical skill and love bargaining, games of chance, and working with metal and stone.

creatures.name: Kanya
creatures.description: Kanyas are bearers of blessings and fortune, as well as harbingers of wisdom and spiritual growth.

creatures.name: Kholo Outrider
creatures.description: Many kholo packs are semi-nomadic, staying in one place until they're pushed out by others, or the local resources begin to grow sparse.

creatures.name: Kirin
creatures.description: The rare and majestic antlered beast known as a kirin supposedly appear only in times of peace and prosperity or during the birth or death of a great sage.

creatures.name: Knight
creatures.description: Elite fighters from the lowest ranks of nobility, knights are proud champions of their court.

creatures.name: Lantern Archon (Gestalt)
creatures.description: Friendly, curious, and eternally optimistic, lantern archons embody the virtue of hope.

creatures.name: Legion Archon
creatures.description: Despite their flaming blades and heavy armor, legion archons are the diplomats of peace among the archons, preferring justice via compromise and mutual benefit rather than justice by the sword.

creatures.name: Levaloch
creatures.description: Fearsome giants of jagged iron, levalochs serve in the armies of Hell as potent warriors and tenacious hunters-creatures of absolute discipline endlessly obedient to diabolical tyrants.

creatures.name: Living Tar
creatures.description: Most often found belowground, these oozes scour caves for objects to dissolve with their corrosive secretions.

creatures.name: Manticore Paaridar
creatures.description: Monks dissatisfied with the limits of their humanoid forms and yearning for a shortcut can steal the might from other creatures.

creatures.name: Master of Disguise
creatures.description: A master of disguise uses costuming, makeup, and minor illusions to deceive. Some conceal their identity for years, infiltrating organizations under deep cover. In the underbelly of society, the lawless reign supreme.

creatures.name: Medusa
creatures.description: Monstrous humanoids that resemble humans with snakes instead of hair, medusas are best known for their petrifying gazes that—if lingered upon—can permanently transform mortals to stone.

creatures.name: Megalania
creatures.description: Megalanias, like their smaller cousins the giant monitor lizards, strike fast and use their powerful bite to grip their prey. They prefer to swallow their prey whole rather than risk others getting a bite of a hard-won meal.

creatures.name: Mixed Martial Artist
creatures.description: These fighters combine the takedowns of a wrestler with aggressive stances and unorthodox blows, ensuring that contenders won't know what hit them. Martial artists strive to master the art of hand-to-hand fighting.

creatures.name: Mothman
creatures.description: Little is known of the strange creatures known as mothmen, save that when they make themselves known, calamity is sure to follow.

creatures.name: Naiad Queen
creatures.description: Naiad queens rule over pristine wildernesses centered around untouched lakes, massive rivers, or other collective bodies of fresh water.

creatures.name: Narrik
creatures.description: Far below the surface of Golarion, packs of narriks hunt in Darklands tunnels, looking for unfortunate beings to devour.

creatures.name: Naunet
creatures.description: Pugnacious and powerfully muscled, naunets serve as the scouts and rank-and-file troops of protean armies. Proteans are manifestations of chaos made flesh, natives of the Maelstrom that embody the primeval potency of entropy in their serpentine forms.

creatures.name: Ogre Boss
creatures.description: In ogre society, might makes more than right—it makes the rules.

creatures.name: Omen Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Fate is a fickle matter on Golarion. Even with prophecy broken on the world, there are ways to look to the immediate future or acquire a vague sense of long-term events. Omen dragons are bound to see the future—nebulous though it might be—at all times. Visions of the future hound them like a quiet …

creatures.name: Omen Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Fate is a fickle matter on Golarion. Even with prophecy broken on the world, there are ways to look to the immediate future or acquire a vague sense of long-term events. Omen dragons are bound to see the future—nebulous though it might be—at all times. Visions of the future hound them like a quiet …

creatures.name: Orc Skullcrushers
creatures.description: Orc warriors search for more effective weapons and tactics—most physical, but some magical.

creatures.name: Pairaka
creatures.description: Often charming, sometimes even seductive, pairakas worm their way into mortal relationships, subtly destroying all ties of friendship and love through emotional and physical manipulation and corruption.

creatures.name: Phade
creatures.description: On the Plane of Air, and throughout the Inner Sphere, phades serve as spies and assassins for their creators.

creatures.name: Praskith
creatures.description: These strange amalgams of plant and fungus with animal tendencies form where the natural world is corrupted by foul magic or frayed planar boundaries.

creatures.name: Pukwudgie
creatures.description: Pukwudgies go by many names in many regions, but those who know of them agree that defying their mischievous nature provokes their wrath.

creatures.name: Quatoid
creatures.description: Quatoids are peculiar elementals native to the Plane of Water that resemble four-tentacled octopuses with eerily humanoid faces on their mantles.

creatures.name: Quetzalcoatlus
creatures.description: Quetzalcoatlus are the largest flying members of the pterosaur family and are often mistaken for dragons due to their immense size and 40-foot wingspans.

creatures.name: Remorhaz
creatures.description: A remorhaz is an enormous arctic predator that resembles a multilegged insect with draconic features.

creatures.name: Salamander
creatures.description: Salamanders have serpentine lower torsos, but humanoid upper bodies with toothy reptilian snouts. Their affinity for cruelty and violence puts them in close alliance with the demons of the Abyss. Fire elementals are destructive manifestations of the scorching Plane of Fire.

creatures.name: Sceaduinar
creatures.description: Strange creatures born from jagged crystals in the heart of the Void, sceaduinar are fueled by its void energy and driven to extinguish all life.

creatures.name: Shaitan
creatures.description: Hailing from the Plane of Earth, shaitans are proud and brazen genies.

creatures.name: Shoggti
creatures.description: Shoggti travel to other planes in roving bands to capture and charm victims, bringing them back to the Abyss where they are enslaved for unknown-but no doubt insidiously evil-purposes.

creatures.name: Skeletal Hulk
creatures.description: Huge giants and other enormous creatures make powerful skeletons. Animated skeletons are among the most common types of undead.

creatures.name: Slithering Pit
creatures.description: A slithering pit is a strange, nearly invisible ooze with an extradimensional space for its digestive system, which mimics the appearance of an acid-filled stone pit.

creatures.name: Smaranava
creatures.description: Many view clouded nagas as jealous, malevolent creatures.

creatures.name: Soul Eater
creatures.description: Only the most desperate or foolhardy conjurers call upon the aid of these strange fiends from the inky swamps of Abaddon.

creatures.name: Specter
creatures.description: When an evil mortal creature dies, it sometimes returns to haunt the area of its death as a specter, a hateful remnant, always seeking to slay others-particularly humanoids-in an attempt to distribute its pain among as many souls as it can.

creatures.name: Sportlebore Swarm
creatures.description: A swarm of sportlebores is a much more dangerous foe than a single insect.

creatures.name: Spriggan Warlord
creatures.description: While the leaders of larger spriggan enclaves tend to be uniquely trained warriors with highly specialized skills, spriggan warlords are fond of the traditional spriggan weaponry of morningstar and crossbow, valuing this traditional fighting style for its simplicity and for using both weapons to th…

creatures.name: Stegosaurus
creatures.description: The stegosaurus is easily recognized by its twin rows of diamond-shaped dorsal plates that run down its spine and thick tail adorned with four large spikes.

creatures.name: Stygira
creatures.description: These scarred, eyeless creatures appear as withered hermits wrapped in tattered rags.

creatures.name: Succubus
creatures.description: Succubi are manifestations of the sin of destructive lust, and they are the most attractive of all demons—as befits their role in seducing mortals to fall to sin.
languages: three additional mortal languages, Telepathy 100 feet, Truespeech

creatures.name: Tendriculos
creatures.description: These strange amalgams of plant and fungus with animal tendencies form where the natural world is corrupted by foul magic or frayed planar boundaries.

creatures.name: Theletos
creatures.description: Theletoses maintain the balance between fate and free will.

creatures.name: Therapeutic Healer
creatures.description: Some healers feel great empathy for their charges and take great pains to help shoulder their allies' burdens both in and out of combat. The world is a dangerous place. Thankfully, there are those who devote their lives to easing the pain and suffering of others.

creatures.name: Tiddalik
creatures.description: Tiddaliks are giant, drought-causing monstrosities resembling grossly swollen frogs.

creatures.name: Totenmaske
creatures.description: Spawned by the same unnatural and self-destructive obsessions that drove them when they still lived, totenmaskes are the undead remnants of the most self-indulgent and sinful among mortals.

creatures.name: Tripkee Fiend-Keeper
creatures.description: Tripkees have a technique for dealing with unholy threats that has been passed down through the generations.

creatures.name: Tupilaq
creatures.description: A tupilaq is an artistically crafted construct carved from animal bones (typically whale or walrus) and imbued with the express purpose of eviscerating its creator's enemies.

creatures.name: Vanth
creatures.description: Protectors of the Boneyard, the stern and resolute guardians of the dead, vanths are psychopomps who resemble skeletons with raven-like wings and a mask resembling a vulture's skull.

creatures.name: Whisper Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Whisper dragons are keen collectors of rumors and secrets who spend centuries honing their information networks and relationships.

creatures.name: Whisper Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Whisper dragons are keen collectors of rumors and secrets who spend centuries honing their information networks and relationships.

creatures.name: Winter Hag
creatures.description: Winter hags are as icy in demeanor as their name implies.

creatures.name: Xorn
creatures.description: These squat, rotund elementals have three legs, three arms, three eyes, and one massive maw, which they fill with the gems and metals they find so delicious.

creatures.name: Zetogeki
creatures.description: Basalt comprises the earthen bodies of zetogekis, giant lizards with an innate ability to absorb and redirect kinetic energy.

creatures.name: Aigamuxa
creatures.description: Aigamuxas are towering humanoids that stalk arid deserts in search of prey.

creatures.name: Anancus
creatures.description: The anancus is an ancient species of elephant with unusually long tusks and a trunk shorter than its more modern cousins.

creatures.name: Angazhani
creatures.description: Angazhanis lurk in the depths of the world's jungles, where these sapient, four-armed apes prey on any creatures they deem lesser than themselves.

creatures.name: Animate Dream
creatures.description: An animate dream coalesces from centuries of stray fragments of reverie and dream left behind as slumbering minds drift through the Dimension of Dreams.

creatures.name: Ankhrav Hive Mother
creatures.description: Ankhrav hive mothers are fearsome predators that one can easily distinguish from the typical ankhrav not only by their greater size, but the presence of a large pair of razor-sharp, mantis-like arms.

creatures.name: Arboreal Regent
creatures.description: Arboreal regents are lumbering, solitary creatures responsible for guarding an entire forest.

creatures.name: Arrester Squadron
creatures.description: These guards have been extensively trained to perform complex maneuvers together.

creatures.name: Assassin
creatures.description: Assassins commit murder, either for pay or due to their belief in a cause, such as a religion or a political movement. Many are members of assassins' guilds: organizations that accept contracts to kill in return for money, favors, or both. In the underbelly of society, the lawless reign supreme.

creatures.name: Axiomite
creatures.description: According to axiomites, their kind rose from the raw mathematical underpinnings of the universe, manifesting as great builders who created the ancient, colossal cities of the Outer Planes.

creatures.name: Balisse
creatures.description: Balisses, or confessor angels, seek to assist mortals ensnared by moral dilemmas or crises of faith.

creatures.name: Behir
creatures.description: The behir is a massive, serpentine beast with never fewer than a dozen short legs, each ending in three hooked talons. A behir can live to 80 years, but most perish far younger to violence. Fully grown, a behir is 40 feet long and weighs 4,000 pounds.

creatures.name: Blood Hag
creatures.description: Blood hags, also known as soucouyants, infiltrate communities in the guise of young innocents.

creatures.name: Bodak
creatures.description: When a living, sentient humanoid is exposed to an extreme expression of supernatural evil, the experience can irrevocably damn the victim, crushing their mind and ripping out their soul in an appalling, unholy transformation that results in a creature that's anathema to life-the bodak.

creatures.name: Bone Prophet
creatures.description: The speakers for the dead known as bone prophets hold an esteemed place as voices for their decapitated god.

creatures.name: Bulette
creatures.description: Bulettes are fearsome predators that roam just beneath the surface of desolate wilderness areas.

creatures.name: Charnel Creation
creatures.description: Made of odd scraps of skin and muscle, a charnel creation is a grotesque parody of life.

creatures.name: Chimera
creatures.description: The chimera is the archetypal example of an unnatural monster made up of a monstrous mix of wildly different component creatures: in this case, a lion, a dragon, and a goat.

creatures.name: Conspirator Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Hidden among the shadows and upper echelons of society are the conspirator dragons.

creatures.name: Conspirator Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Hidden among the shadows and upper echelons of society are the conspirator dragons.

creatures.name: Copper Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Capricious and always eager to share a good laugh, copper dragons are among the wiliest of the metallic dragons, but this by no means interferes with their mission to spread freedom in oppressive lands.

creatures.name: Copper Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Capricious and always eager to share a good laugh, copper dragons are among the wiliest of the metallic dragons, but this by no means interferes with their mission to spread freedom in oppressive lands.

creatures.name: Coral Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Coral dragons are the primal guardians of the vast living coral reefs found in oceans across Golarion.

creatures.name: Coral Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Coral dragons are the primal guardians of the vast living coral reefs found in oceans across Golarion.

creatures.name: Denizen of Leng
creatures.description: Travelers and raiders from the cold, windswept dimension of Leng, these malevolent human-shaped creatures range across reality in ominous black ships capable of sailing beyond the borders of dimensions and planes.

creatures.name: Desert Drake
creatures.description: These distant cousins of creatures like adamantine dragons are a pale echo that lacks their relatives' power and intelligence.

creatures.name: Destrachan
creatures.description: This underground reptilian horror walks on two powerful hind legs.

creatures.name: Drill Sergeant
creatures.description: Maintaining discipline is of the utmost importance when conducting a military campaign.

creatures.name: Dwarf General
creatures.description: Dwarven generals embody pride in knowledge and tactical acumen, using their understanding of warfare and battlefield strategy to coordinate their subordinates into optimal locations.

creatures.name: Dynamo
creatures.description: The intricate clockwork prostheses known as sterling dynamos have become increasingly common in recent years, particularly in havens of technological progress like Dongun Hold and the city of Absalom, but a few specialized engineers have elevated their dedication to research and development of such…

creatures.name: Erinys
creatures.description: Erinyes exact vengeance and bloody justice for a creature's crimes, torturing and punishing their victims in ironic fashion before allowing them the escape of death.

creatures.name: Flesh Golem
creatures.description: Made of odd scraps of skin and muscle, a flesh golem is a grotesque parody of life.

creatures.name: Gendarme
creatures.description: Powerful governments retain gendarmes to guard important magistrates, enforce laws protecting national security, reinstate order amid unrest, and capture unusually dangerous criminals.

creatures.name: Ghostly Mob
creatures.description: When a horrific tragedy results in mass death, the restless spirits of the numerous dead might arise as a ghostly mob.

creatures.name: Giant Anaconda
creatures.description: The monstrous giant anaconda is a constrictor snake capable of swallowing whole creatures as big as horses—to say nothing of their riders.

creatures.name: Giant Hippocampus
creatures.description: Living in the deepest reaches of the ocean, giant hippocampi haven't been sighted near the shore and are often regarded as legends made up by sailors.

creatures.name: Giant Octopus
creatures.description: Giant octopuses are found in the heart of deep, dark oceans.

creatures.name: Giant Slug
creatures.description: These massive, primeval relatives of ordinary slugs squirm slowly through swamps and grasslands.

creatures.name: Girtablilu Sentry
creatures.description: Sentries patrol the outskirts of girtablilu communities, watching for external threats like desert drakes, stormcrown dragons, or adventuring parties set on recklessly exploring the girtablilus' sacred sites.

creatures.name: Glass Golem
creatures.description: Crafted of hardened glass and held together by magically treated lead, glass golems are both deadly guardians and works of exquisite art.

creatures.name: Golem
creatures.description: Long ago, the priests of ancient religions mastered a technique to bring them closer to their god: the animation of clay into a living being.

creatures.name: Gorgon
creatures.description: Though they may resemble constructs to the untrained eye due to their metallic, interlocking armor plates that look and feel like polished stone, gorgons are a creature of flesh and bone.

creatures.name: Granite Glyptodont
creatures.description: This squat and stony creature looks like an armadillo with a large raised back and a flail-like tail.

creatures.name: Greater Herexen
creatures.description: When a cleric rebels against their deity and dies in the grip of blasphemous rage, the heresies they committed in life may fuel their transformation into an undead herexen.

creatures.name: Green Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Green dragons are the most contemplative of the chromatic dragons as well as the most approachable.

creatures.name: Green Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Green dragons are the most contemplative of the chromatic dragons as well as the most approachable.

creatures.name: Guecubu
creatures.description: Sometimes an individual performs acts of horror so unspeakable that the earth they walk on becomes imprinted by their evil.

creatures.name: Guildmaster
creatures.description: In cities, artisans working in a common trade often form guilds to set standards of quality, establish common prices, engage in collective bargaining with business owners, and lobby local governments for favorable laws.

creatures.name: Hadrinnex
creatures.description: The bizarre hadrinnexes resemble defensive systems more than living creatures.

creatures.name: Hellknight Cavalry Brigade
creatures.description: A Hellknight cavalry brigade consists of several Hellknights and a single field-maralictor, all wearing the distinctive armor of their order and wielding lances.

creatures.name: Hellwasp Swarm
creatures.description: Hellwasps' infernal nature allows them to collectively inhabit and control humanoid corpses, and they use this ability to infest and horrify large swathes of territory unless destroyed.

creatures.name: Hive Mother
creatures.description: Ankhrav hive mothers are fearsome predators that one can easily distinguish from the typical ankhrav not only by their greater size, but the presence of a large pair of razor-sharp, mantis-like arms.

creatures.name: Hobgoblin Vanguard
creatures.description: Though there are times for precision and discipline, every hobgoblin general also understands the value of demoralizing the enemy with a show of overwhelming force.

creatures.name: Horned Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The magic that flows through primal dragons can manifest more animalistic or bestial features in a given type of dragon.

creatures.name: Horned Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: The magic that flows through primal dragons can manifest more animalistic or bestial features in a given type of dragon.

creatures.name: Intellect Devourer
creatures.description: Intellect devourers serve as advance scouts and infiltrators for the powerful force of alien beings called the Dominion of the Black.

creatures.name: Jah-Tohl
creatures.description: The grotesque jah-tohl arrive in living starships to harvest the brains of intelligent creatures.

creatures.name: Kishi
creatures.description: Kishis are duplicitous and murderous fey who dwell on the edges of Golarion's densest jungles.

creatures.name: Krooth
creatures.description: Krooths, sometimes called crocodile eaters, are fast and vicious hunters of bogs and wetlands.

creatures.name: Lamia Matriarch
creatures.description: The brilliant and powerful lamia matriarchs possess boundless ambition, always seeking to bring more people and territory into the clutches of their kind.

creatures.name: Lifeleecher Brawler
creatures.description: Lifeleechers are mortic orcs with gray flesh, sharp claws, and protruding tusks.

creatures.name: Lucky Courser
creatures.description: Masterful catfolk hunters travel the far corners of the world, stalking terrible the monsters who dwell there in hopes of someday putting an end to their predations.

creatures.name: Mage Killer
creatures.description: Whenever high command needs an enemy spellcaster taken off the board in the midst of battle, they send in a mage killer. Whether they're hired to wage war, protect a caravan, or infiltrate an impenetrable fortress, there's ample work for mercenaries all over Golarion.

creatures.name: Magma Scorpion
creatures.description: Whether skittering through Chthonian wastelands or basking in the searing sand of the deepest deserts, magma scorpions have charred carapaces, constantly emitting vision-warping waves of heat.

creatures.name: Marsh Giant
creatures.description: Dwellers of brackish coastal salt marshes and fetid bogs and swamps, marsh giants appear hideous to most airbreathers, with fishlike mouths, slimy graygreen skin, and dark, beady eyes.

creatures.name: Megaprimatus
creatures.description: The megaprimatus is among the mightiest of apes, quick to confront any perceived intrusions into its domain.

creatures.name: Mix Coatl
creatures.description: Mix coatls (pronounced "meesh") are guides to fledgling societies, providing fundamental information such as farming techniques, medicinal expertise, or more esoteric knowledge like the arcane arts.

creatures.name: Mohrg
creatures.description: The weight of murder wears heavy on the soul.

creatures.name: Moonflower
creatures.description: These enormous plants have thick, knotted trunks festooned with fanged blossoms.

creatures.name: Mountain Oni
creatures.description: Mountain oni are the most common oni and often considered the least sophisticated.

creatures.name: Nabasu
creatures.description: Nabasus are birthed directly into the Material Plane from the Abyss, an invasive infestation that releases the newly formed and ravenously hungry demons directly into the midst of their favored prey: mortal life.

creatures.name: Nosferatu Thrall
creatures.description: Nosferatu thralls are mortals bound to a nosferatu's will.

creatures.name: Onidoshi
creatures.description: Onidoshi, or ogre mages, are oni with the material form of an ogre.

creatures.name: Palace Skelm
creatures.description: Skelms crave power, and the palace skelms who stalk the halls where such power concentrates are among the most dangerous of their kind.

creatures.name: Phantom Beast
creatures.description: The River of Souls, alongside the souls of dead sapient humanoids, also carries the spirits of monsters, animals, and other creatures.

creatures.name: Procyal
creatures.description: As the most whimsical and playful of the agathions, procyals are the most likely to be encountered in the Universe, teaching deep philosophical lessons and delivering wisdom through pranks and impersonations.

creatures.name: Resurrection Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Resurrection dragons teeter between life and death.

creatures.name: Resurrection Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Resurrection dragons teeter between life and death.

creatures.name: Sandpoint Devil
creatures.description: While some creatures have been fused to create a new entity that's graceful and beautiful, the Sandpoint Devil is the opposite.

creatures.name: Sarglagon
creatures.description: Sarglagons dwell in Hell's myriad waterways, lakes, and oceans.

creatures.name: Shantak
creatures.description: The imposing shantaks are far more intelligent than their bestial appearance would suggest.

creatures.name: Skin Shifter
creatures.description: Skin shifters are defenders of nature, blessed by the spirits of the wild with the ability to shape shift into powerful animal forms.

creatures.name: Skittering Slayer
creatures.description: When a warrior's last thoughts are of bloodlust, their body can become so suffused with rage that it infects whatever eats their corpse.

creatures.name: Sphinx
creatures.description: Sphinxes are mystical beings with the body of a lion, the wings of a great bird, and the upper torso and head of a human.

creatures.name: Stone Giant
creatures.description: Stone giants are stoic, reclusive herders and artists with a rich history and collection of traditions.

creatures.name: Triceratops
creatures.description: Triceratopses are muscular quadrupeds with powerful short legs, thick necks, and heads crowned by a wide, bony frill.

creatures.name: Two-Headed Troll
creatures.description: After a particularly severe head injury, a troll might regrow two heads, with each taking on fragments of the old personality and its responsibilities over the body.

creatures.name: Tylosaurus
creatures.description: Though large in size-sometimes exceeding 40 feet-tylosauruses have sleek bodies and long, narrow snouts.

creatures.name: Wealthy Vigilante
creatures.description: By night, this member of the nobility dons a false identity to mete out violent, extralegal justice on petty criminals and the downtrodden.

creatures.name: Woodland Scouts
creatures.description: Elves of the forest or jungle take advantage of the terrain, operating in units that seemingly appear from the trees themselves. Elves' long lives give them centuries to delve into studies, artistry, or exploration.

creatures.name: Woolly Wrangler
creatures.description: When a giant, dangerous animal is found, there's always someone who tries to pet it.

creatures.name: Xoarian
creatures.description: The five-tentacled xoarians infiltrate many societies, although it's unclear how much of their activities are at the behest of the Dominion of the Black and how much is for their own perverse enjoyment.

creatures.name: Accuser Agent
creatures.description: Accuser agents might be high court advocates, official spymasters, or innocuous adjutants delivering important messages to magistrates, generals, officers, or mercenaries.

creatures.name: Adamantine Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The powerful adamantine dragons are one of several dragons known as skymetal dragons.

creatures.name: Adamantine Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: The powerful adamantine dragons are one of several dragons known as skymetal dragons.

creatures.name: Alchemical Golem
creatures.description: This golem is a walking alchemical nightmare capable of inflicting all manner of painful wounds.

creatures.name: Animated Furnace
creatures.description: This huge forge or oven is animated to protect a workspace or kitchen, travel alongside its owner, or both. Many animated objects have useful functions but become dangers when uncontrolled.

creatures.name: Aurumvorax
creatures.description: Aurumvoraxes are highly aggressive and territorial predators that sprint on eight legs, using their terrific strength and iron-hard claws to dig through solid stone.

creatures.name: Baykok
creatures.description: A baykok is the restless remnant of a warrior or hunter, cast out for evil acts and cursed to forever soar through the sky far from its home, unable to ever set foot on the ground again.

creatures.name: Black Dracolisk
creatures.description: Dracolisks are rare magical crossbreeds of chromatic dragons and basilisks.

creatures.name: Blood Painter
creatures.description: Simultaneously enamored with the natural world yet too alien to survive in it, blood painters are eight-limbed artists who stalk, kill, and dismember in search of pigment and sustenance alike.

creatures.name: Blue Dracolisk
creatures.description: Dracolisks are rare magical crossbreeds of chromatic dragons and basilisks.

creatures.name: Blue Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Blue dragons are the sleek and poised cosmopolitans of the chromatic dragons.

creatures.name: Blue Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Blue dragons are the sleek and poised cosmopolitans of the chromatic dragons.

creatures.name: Bronze Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Bronze dragons are among the most common of metallic dragons and the most likely to ally with mortals on worthy quests.

creatures.name: Bronze Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Bronze dragons are among the most common of metallic dragons and the most likely to ally with mortals on worthy quests.

creatures.name: Clockwork Mage
creatures.description: A clockwork mage is a lethal blend of magic and machinery.

creatures.name: Cuckoo Hag
creatures.description: Cuckoo hags seek out youths who are unhappy with their lives, disguising themselves as a trusted figure such as a friend or family member.

creatures.name: Deinosuchus
creatures.description: The deinosuchus is a primeval relative of the crocodile, and is an enormous predator capable of catching and eating dinosaurs that wander too close to its domain! Powerful and primeval in appearance, crocodiles are dangerous natural predators that dwell in marshes, riverbeds, swamps, and other wetl…

creatures.name: Desert Giant
creatures.description: Desert giants are nomadic humanoids who have dwelled in the world's most arid regions since time immemorial.

creatures.name: Despair Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Fear is one of the most powerful emotions, and despair dragons are masters of channeling those feelings of terror and hopelessness for their own benefit.

creatures.name: Despair Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Fear is one of the most powerful emotions, and despair dragons are masters of channeling those feelings of terror and hopelessness for their own benefit.

creatures.name: Doldrums Heap
creatures.description: Though most sargassum heaps divide once they grow too large, occasionally a heap will continue to grow indefinitely.

creatures.name: Dragon Turtle
creatures.description: These immense aquatic dragons have rocky shells similar to those of tortoises and flippers powerful enough to overturn hardy vessels.

creatures.name: Drakauthix
creatures.description: The drakauthix is a massive fungus that floats through the heights of the largest Darklands caverns, propelled by bladders that spray air and spores.

creatures.name: Efreeti
creatures.description: The efreet are hateful and merciless genies from the Plane of Fire, where they build metropolises and massive trade centers that draw extraplanar travelers from across the multiverse.

creatures.name: Expedition Leader
creatures.description: Full-scale expeditions require a central leader. Expedition leaders tend to have level heads in dangerous situations and can make decisions quickly when time is of the essence. Explorers are often well-equipped and well-trained for any type of hazard and are eager to lead others into the wild.

creatures.name: Faydhaan
creatures.description: The faydhaans of the Plane of Water are more powerful than the other genies dwelling on the elemental planes, but they prefer to forge alliances through diplomacy and flattery.

creatures.name: Firewyrm
creatures.description: Firewyrms live in tubes of molten lava found throughout the Plane of Fire.

creatures.name: Frost Giant
creatures.description: Frost giants are remorseless marauders who pillage and plunder from those who dare to live near them in desolate, frigid lands.

creatures.name: Galvo
creatures.description: A galvo is a bizarre humanoid creature made up of electric eels-dozens of them, transmogrified into one cooperative, electrified body.

creatures.name: Garuda
creatures.description: Garudas are winged humanoids created to serve holy gods and other powerful celestial beings.
languages: Plus two others, Speaker of the skies

creatures.name: Giant Snapping Turtle
creatures.description: If the regular-sized snapping turtle is foul-tempered, then the giant snapping turtle seems to be willfully violent toward anything that dares intrude upon its domain.

creatures.name: Giant Squid
creatures.description: Dwelling deep within the oceans, this huge cephalopod grows up to 45 feet in length and can weigh up to 600 pounds. Its arms and tentacles are lined with hundreds of hooks and suckers, allowing it to seize its prey with ease.

creatures.name: Gliminal
creatures.description: Beholding the blinding light of a gliminal is akin to staring into the sun, and consequently, only gliminals themselves fully understand what they are or how they truly look.

creatures.name: Greater Hell Hound
creatures.description: Believed to be bred by the Kings of Hell themselves within vast kennels, greater hell hounds are the favored guards and hunting hounds of powerful fiends and, rarely, mortals who worship them and have earned their favor.

creatures.name: Green Dracolisk
creatures.description: Dracolisks are rare magical crossbreeds of chromatic dragons and basilisks.

creatures.name: Hesperid
creatures.description: Hesperides are nymphs of sunset, guardians of the colorful golden hues of the setting sun.

creatures.name: High Priest of Pharasma
creatures.description: High priests are the leaders of larger churches and similar religious establishments, watching over the lower-ranking clergy and ensuring the surrounding community is taken care of.

creatures.name: Humanitarian Hermit
creatures.description: Some druids look after those forgotten by society and thrive at the borders of civilization. The world is a dangerous place. Thankfully, there are those who devote their lives to easing the pain and suffering of others.

creatures.name: Ifrit
creatures.description: The fierce and unforgiving ifrits hail from the Plane of Fire, where they build metropolises and trade centers that draw extraplanar travelers.

creatures.name: Irnakurse
creatures.description: Irnakurse are believed to be elves who have been subjected to particularly cruel and humiliating fleshwarping practices, though they are so corrupted by their transformation it is nearly impossible to tell.

creatures.name: Iruxi Masked Mummer
creatures.description: Iruxi masked mummers bring lizardfolk myths to life, donning elaborate costumes and bone masks to enact stories featuring their ancestors, the gods, legendary dragons and dinosaurs, and nature spirits of all kinds.

creatures.name: Jyoti
creatures.description: Jyotis are sometimes called "false phoenixes" (a term they find insulting) by the ignorant or willful.

creatures.name: Khravgodon
creatures.description: Few creatures can shrug off an ankhrav's acid and crunch its chitin like a khravgodon. Few creatures have survived as long and in as many environments as the opossum.

creatures.name: Kithangian
creatures.description: The kithangian—a horrifying amalgam of horse and scorpion also known as a "beast demon"—is a foul fend born of the souls of mortals who abused and tormented animals in life.

creatures.name: Leukodaemon
creatures.description: These skull-headed, vulture-winged daemons are harbingers of pestilence and servants of their patron Apocalypse Rider, Apollyon.

creatures.name: Marid
creatures.description: Marids are capricious but powerful genies from the Plane of Water; among geniekind, they are rivaled in power only by the fiery efreet.

creatures.name: Mastodon
creatures.description: Not to be confused with the larger mammoth, mastodons are primeval elephants who dwell predominantly in temperate forests. There, they travel in close-knit social groups and feed on the forest vegetation.

creatures.name: Megalodon
creatures.description: Prehistoric sharks of incredible size, strength, and ferocity, megalodons scour waters deep and shallow to sate their considerable hunger.

creatures.name: Mercenary Band
creatures.description: Some bands of experienced soldiers have storied reputations, their rolls filled with legendary warriors.

creatures.name: Mirage Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Mirage dragons are masters of illusion magic and use their powers to deceive others and further their own agendas.

creatures.name: Mirage Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Mirage dragons are masters of illusion magic and use their powers to deceive others and further their own agendas.

creatures.name: Mirror Seer
creatures.description: Seeking to be the most powerful and perfect creature in their domain, a mirror seer forges a deal with a nefarious entity for more power.

creatures.name: Mokele-Mbembe
creatures.description: Mokele-mbembes are large, reptilian predators found deep within Golarion's jungles.

creatures.name: Mummy Pharaoh
creatures.description: While mummy guardians are undead crafted from the corpses of sacrificed—usually unwilling victims—and retain only fragments of their memories, a mummy pharaoh is the result of a deliberate embrace of undeath by a sadistic and cruel ruler.
languages: plus any two languages they knew while alive

creatures.name: Night Hag
creatures.description: Night hags are thieves and merchants of mortal souls.

creatures.name: Noxious Needler
creatures.description: This construct is a walking alchemical nightmare capable of inflicting all manner of painful wounds.

creatures.name: Nuckelavee
creatures.description: When pollution despoils a natural waterway, it draws the dreaded nuckelavee to it from the First World.

creatures.name: Osyluth
creatures.description: An osyluth is an unparalleled inquisitor that revels in the sadism and torture for which Hell is so well known.

creatures.name: Pakalchi
creatures.description: Pakalchis strive to intensify their preys' inherent insecurity over personal and emotional bonds, playing on the threat of those relationships falling into ruin.

creatures.name: Phase Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: No place can contain a phase dragon or even hold their interest for long; their innate arcane connection ties them to teleportation and repositioning magic.

creatures.name: Phase Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: No place can contain a phase dragon or even hold their interest for long; their innate arcane connection ties them to teleportation and repositioning magic.

creatures.name: Red Dracolisk
creatures.description: Dracolisks are rare magical crossbreeds of chromatic dragons and basilisks.

creatures.name: Roc
creatures.description: These legendary, massive raptors, capable of carrying off elephants as prey, are typically about 30 feet long from beak to tail and have a wingspan of 80 feet or more.

creatures.name: Roiling Incant (Evocation)
creatures.description: Though many spellcasters can harness the forces of magic in a consistent manner, such power can't always be controlled, especially in the hands of reckless researchers, megalomaniacal villains, or untested novices.

creatures.name: Shoki
creatures.description: Shokis shepherd wayward souls to the Boneyard.

creatures.name: Spirit Naga
creatures.description: In naga culture, the foul-tempered, unkempt spirit naga holds a contemptible place similar to that of hags in humanoid cultures.

creatures.name: Stone Mauler
creatures.description: These towering heaps of earth can inflict tremendous damage up close and from afar. Earth elementals make excellent bodyguards for adventuresome spelunkers and are ideal protectors of important subterranean locations such as vaults and treasuries.

creatures.name: Storm Lord
creatures.description: Storm lords wage battles to claim important territory within the Plane of Air. Hailing from the Plane of Air, these beings appear in a variety of sizes and shapes.

creatures.name: Strix Aerialist
creatures.description: Strix aerialists are talented acrobats. Strix, called itarii in their own language, are avian humanoids with sprawling, dark-feathered wings and large talons. They possess angular features and piercing eyes that are fixed facing forward.

creatures.name: Tennin
creatures.description: Celestial artisans and elegant dancers called tennins serve as Nirvana's attendants, where they weave the gods' marvelous, seamless garments from clouds and frost and entertain the courts with skyward dances, their footsteps lighter than dew.

creatures.name: Tick Swarm
creatures.description: This swarm of thousands of fist-sized ticks forms a moving carpet of bloated insects-a nauseating and intimidating sight.

creatures.name: Tidal Master
creatures.description: Tidal masters use their power over waves and water to drown their enemies.

creatures.name: Tikbalang
creatures.description: Tikbalangs are forest creatures that delight in leading travelers astray.

creatures.name: Titan Centipede
creatures.description: These colossal, solitary centipedes claim vast hunting grounds.

creatures.name: Traveling Priest of Desna
creatures.description: Deities and their religions are only as strong as the belief of their faithful.

creatures.name: Tyrannosaurus Skeleton
creatures.description: A massive dinosaur predator makes for a fearsome skeleton. Almost any creature that had bones in life and leaves them behind in death can become a shambling, undead skeleton-humanoids, beasts, aberrations, fey, and more.

creatures.name: Vampire Mastermind
creatures.description: Vampire masterminds are evil wizards who control entire cabals of the undead and instill fear in the hearts of mortals far and wide. Vampires are undead creatures that feed on the blood of the living.

creatures.name: Vescavor Queen
creatures.description: A vescavor queen is a horrifying sight. Much like their swarms, they resemble an acrid green wasp, but their abdomens are abnormally bloated and covered in acid-leaking spikes; their mouths make up the majority of their face and are filled with large, gnarled teeth, each the size of a human finger.…

creatures.name: Vrock
creatures.description: When the gates to the Abyss swing wide, the first demons through are often vrocks, vulture-headed scions of rage who wheel through the air on black wings or dance with the exultation of their fiendish power.

creatures.name: War Wraith
creatures.description: These menacing spiritual remnants of wicked warlords or bloodthirsty generals are towering specters of shadow and death.

creatures.name: White Dracolisk
creatures.description: Dracolisks are rare magical crossbreeds of chromatic dragons and basilisks.

creatures.name: Witchfire
creatures.description: Manifesting as a sinuous form wreathed in sickly green flames, this incorporeal undead forms when a powerful hag or witch dies in agony or rage.

creatures.name: Yithian
creatures.description: Yithians are strange creatures, defined not by their physical bodies but by their ancient, disembodied minds.

creatures.name: Zelekhut
creatures.description: A zelekhut is a bounty hunter and executioner.

creatures.name: Zombie Dragon
creatures.description: The rotted husk of a once great dragon, this abomination has lost all its former splendor, but none of the ferocity.

creatures.name: Adlet
creatures.description: Adlets dwell in the farthest, harshest reaches of the Crown of the World, with a few journeying beyond to similarly frigid regions in other continents.

creatures.name: Beast Eidolon
creatures.description: This creature is intended to be used as the eidolon accompanying a Sarkorian god caller, but it can be used or adapted into any aggressive beast eidolon for a summoner.

creatures.name: Bebilith
creatures.description: While demons control much of the Abyss, even they are hunted in the wilder regions of this horrific plane.

creatures.name: Behemoth Hippopotamus
creatures.description: Behemoth hippos are omnivorous and many enjoy the taste of meat. Hippopotamuses, or hippos for short, are semiaquatic animals that spend most of their time in rivers and lakes, but can thrive on land.

creatures.name: Brontosaurus
creatures.description: Brontosauruses are truly gigantic behemoths, herbivores large enough to be unafraid of all but the most massive predators.

creatures.name: Champion Automaton
creatures.description: The artifcers of Jistka crafted warrior automatons to fght on the front lines of their armies, and in this goal they succeeded admirably.
languages: One other language the champion knew in life usually jistkan, Telepathy 60 feet

creatures.name: Chyzaedu
creatures.description: Chyzaedus' home world was consumed millennia ago by the Dominion of the Black via the manipulation of a black hole.

creatures.name: Cinder Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Among the largest and fiercest dragons, cinder dragons are typically volatile, demanding respect—even deference—from lesser creatures.

creatures.name: Cinder Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Among the largest and fiercest dragons, cinder dragons are typically volatile, demanding respect—even deference—from lesser creatures.

creatures.name: Clacking Sea Skull Swarm
creatures.description: Roiling about in seething waves of bleached death, clacking skull swarms arise from the remains of dozens of beheaded humanoids.

creatures.name: Clacking Skull Swarm
creatures.description: Roiling about in seething waves of bleached death, clacking skull swarms arise from the remains of dozens of beheaded humanoids.

creatures.name: Clay Effigy
creatures.description: Traditionally, clay effigies are crafted in the image of a deity and used as guardians of tombs or sacred crypts.

creatures.name: Clay Golem
creatures.description: Traditionally, clay golems are crafted in the image of a deity and used as guardians of tombs or sacred crypts.

creatures.name: Court Jester
creatures.description: Though court jesters are often put-upon as the targets of easy mockery and idle amusements, do not mistake their self-deprecation for weakness.

creatures.name: Dezullon
creatures.description: Dezullons are dangerous, carnivorous pitcher plants that dwell in forested regions with thick canopies or deep in marshes where few other plants can grow.

creatures.name: Einherji
creatures.description: Einherjar are mighty warriors chosen by valkyries from the ranks of those slain in terrible and legendary battles.

creatures.name: Empyreal Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The three major celestial planes—Heaven, Nirvana, and Elysium—each have their own respective dragons.

creatures.name: Empyreal Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: The three major celestial planes—Heaven, Nirvana, and Elysium—each have their own respective dragons.

creatures.name: Etioling Blightmage
creatures.description: When the primal spark of the First World nestled in a gnome's heart is engulfed in Radiant Fire , it fights back, pushing against death with riotous life and creating an etioling.

creatures.name: Exiled Revolutionary
creatures.description: Forces hire an exiled revolutionary because they were once part of the enemy.

creatures.name: Feathered Bear
creatures.description: Creature imported from Foundry PF2e data.

creatures.name: Fire Giant
creatures.description: The most militaristic of giants, fire giants focus obsessively on learning combat techniques, mastering the arts of forging weapons and armor, and finding new ways to dominate their enemies.

creatures.name: Fortune Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Fortune dragons have the innate ability to draw upon the raw magical energies that surround them.

creatures.name: Fortune Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Fortune dragons have the innate ability to draw upon the raw magical energies that surround them.

creatures.name: Ghonhatine
creatures.description: In transforming xulgaths, drow fleshwarpers sought not to create a new type of creature but to recapture a form of xulgath predecessor believed entirely vanished from Golarion.

creatures.name: Ghost Mage
creatures.description: A wizard who died with work incomplete might become a ghost mage. When some mortals die through tragic circumstances or without closure, their souls can linger and haunt a locale significant to them in life.

creatures.name: Giant Flytrap
creatures.description: Because they blend in so well with surrounding foliage, giant flytraps can use the element of surprise to make quick strikes against unsuspecting adventurers and forest travelers. Flytraps eagerly feed on humanoids, monstrous insects, and larger prey.

creatures.name: God Caller
creatures.description: The summoners called god callers have a magical link to eidolons, revered as gods by the people of Sarkoris.

creatures.name: Graveknight
creatures.description: Graveknights are undead warriors granted unlife by a cursed suit of armor.

creatures.name: Guardian Naga
creatures.description: The opposite of dark nagas in many ways, guardian nagas are benevolent and wise protectors of places of great natural or supernatural significance: ancient oak groves, forgotten holy temples, and vaults of sacred power are just a few examples of locations that these nagas protect.

creatures.name: Gug
creatures.description: A gug's most horrid feature is its barrel-shaped head, which splits vertically to reveal numerous rows of sharp, yellow teeth and an open throat.

creatures.name: Icewyrm
creatures.description: Resembling wingless, serpentine dragons formed of jagged ice and shot through with veins of nearly frozen water, these elementals dwell within icebergs and enjoy striking out at passing ships or creatures.

creatures.name: Imentesh
creatures.description: The loquacious proteans known as imenteshes serve as missionaries, spies, and heralds of chaos to further the protean goal of reality's dissolution. Proteans are manifestations of chaos made flesh, natives of the Maelstrom that embody the primeval potency of entropy in their serpentine forms.

creatures.name: Infernal Registrar
creatures.description: The infernal registrar is a mortal representative of Hell's bureaucracy.

creatures.name: Leng Ghoul
creatures.description: In the icy nightmare dimension of Leng, ghouls with hoofed feet gather in morbid groups to worship and research, forming societies that mockingly mimic (and in their opinion, surpass) those of the living.

creatures.name: Mage Knight
creatures.description: Though many spellcasters prefer to defend themselves with magic, some recognize that there's no substitute for a suit of steel.

creatures.name: Mammoth
creatures.description: Found mostly in colder climates, mammoths are accustomed to dealing with desperate and dangerous predators, trampling and crushing their enemies while using their larger tusks to greater effect.

creatures.name: Melody on the Wind
creatures.description: While the melody on the wind, known by some as a song elemental, might enjoy the beauty of music, it is by nature a destructive elemental force. Some elementals embody aspects of air, such as smoke, lightning, and fog.

creatures.name: Mobogo
creatures.description: Mobogos are massive, swamp-dwelling monstrosities that combine the worst aspects of giant toads and evil dragons.

creatures.name: Moon Hag
creatures.description: Moon hags are powerful soothsayers who use their foresight to sow chaos and misery.

creatures.name: Movanic Deva
creatures.description: Movanic devas are stewards of the flow of good creatures' souls through the cycle of life and death, from the point a creature is born to the day it dies.

creatures.name: Nereid
creatures.description: Nereids are aquatic fey with deep ties to water.

creatures.name: Nilith
creatures.description: The sleek, dark niliths resemble hairless, emaciated tree sloths.

creatures.name: Nosferatu Malefactor
creatures.description: Nosferatu malefactors spread plague in their wake and yearn for mortal blood. Nosferatus are ancient vampires born of mortals who died in great plagues of old. Perhaps because of their old age, nosferatus can't create more of their kind.

creatures.name: Nymolus
creatures.description: Alghollthus share a genetic memory and are theoretically capable of recalling every experience dating back to the existence of the frst alghollthu hundreds of millions of years ago.

creatures.name: Nyogoth
creatures.description: Little more than a coiled mass of intestines encircling a massive gaping maw, this qlippoth is an Abyssal scavenger, subsisting on the filth and leftovers of demons and qlippoth alike, although it relishes any opportunity to consume living prey.

creatures.name: Ofalth
creatures.description: Fully grown ofalths capture victims and slowly eat them alive. Found in castle dung heaps, city dumps, and sewers, ofalths are living amalgamations of wet detritus, sewage, and rubbish. They carry a disease called wretched weeps that causes the victim's blood to seep from its pores.

creatures.name: Orc Veteran Master
creatures.description: While the sword and shield are reliable and proven in battle, the veteran master is the weapon.

creatures.name: Peluda
creatures.description: Peludas are wild and savage dragons that favor reedy marshlands, sparsely forested bogs, and river valleys for lairs.

creatures.name: Phistophilus
creatures.description: Contract devils are clerks, scribes, and bureaucrats of Hell rarely found outside the infernal courts, and then almost always to pursue potential contracts, tempting mortals to sell their souls in exchange for achieving their worldly desires.

creatures.name: Piscodaemon
