creatures.description: Scions of death by poisoning, piscodaemons are cruel even by daemonic standards, delighting in slow and painful suffering.

creatures.name: Quetz Coatl
creatures.description: These sacred feathered serpents tirelessly serve the powers of knowledge and justice.

creatures.name: Quintessivore
creatures.description: Quintessivores are cunning creatures that wield arcane magic and scalpel-sharp spider limbs that can rend one's soul just as easily as flesh.

creatures.name: Raja Rakshasa
creatures.description: When not disguised as a humanoid, the raja rakshasa has the head of an animal.

creatures.name: Raja-Krodha
creatures.description: The most iconic rakshasas, raja-krodhas are tiger-headed hunters of mortalkind.

creatures.name: Red Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The largest and most powerful of the chromatic dragons, red dragons are a menace to civilizations everywhere, and their strength is rivaled only by their arrogance.

creatures.name: Red Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: The largest and most powerful of the chromatic dragons, red dragons are a menace to civilizations everywhere, and their strength is rivaled only by their arrogance.

creatures.name: Rekhep
creatures.description: Rekheps are the living shields that defend Heaven against fiendish incursions.

creatures.name: Roper
creatures.description: To all but the most sharp-eyed underground explorers, a roper appears to be nothing more than a large stalactite, stalagmite, or pillar of ice.

creatures.name: Rune Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Rune dragons have an innate insight on the power of runes.

creatures.name: Rune Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Rune dragons have an innate insight on the power of runes.

creatures.name: Sacristan
creatures.description: Sacristans are failures among the velstracs, creatures whose bodies and minds have been utterly broken by the velstracs' torments.

creatures.name: Shield Archon
creatures.description: Shield archons defend the fortresses of Heaven against fiendish incursions.

creatures.name: Silver Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Silver dragons are among the most chivalrous of all dragonkind; they wield frost and cold as weapons, can walk on clouds, and dwell high upon snowy mountain peaks or deep in steep, misty valleys.

creatures.name: Silver Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Silver dragons are among the most chivalrous of all dragonkind; they wield frost and cold as weapons, can walk on clouds, and dwell high upon snowy mountain peaks or deep in steep, misty valleys.

creatures.name: Soul Skelm
creatures.description: Soul skelms arise not from mortal men but from other skelms, as their long-held wrath slowly twists them and replaces lingering fragments of their mortality with even more fiendish aspects.

creatures.name: Swordkeeper
creatures.description: Collectors who want to guard their magical arsenals procure or build swordkeepers. These multi-armed constructs are equal parts display case and security system, each holding a single weapon within its body and projecting copies of the weapon it stores to deter would-be thieves.

creatures.name: Troll King
creatures.description: Some trolls have learned the skills necessary to survive well beyond the typical life expectancy for their kind and rally others to follow their lead.

creatures.name: Troll Warleader
creatures.description: Long-lived trolls sometimes force their way through the twin distractions of hunger and pain to learn more about the world around them.

creatures.name: Tyrannosaurus
creatures.description: Widely regarded as the king of the dinosaurs, the tyrannosaurus is a massive predator with a wide mouth filled with viciously sharp teeth.

creatures.name: Vicharamuni
creatures.description: Awakener nagas are benevolent and wise teachers of people and places.

creatures.name: Vrykolakas Master
creatures.description: Vrykolakas masters are sinister shapechangers. They walk undetected among the living and prey upon them like a wolf among sheep, often leaving the corpses to rise as vrykolakas spawn.

creatures.name: Warmonger
creatures.description: Warmongers believe the base state of life is violence. They stay in peak physical condition with constant training and keep their supplies ready for marching to war. Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: Watchmage Squadron
creatures.description: Governments often organize and deploy squadrons of watchmages in places where dangerous magic is expected.

creatures.name: Water Orm
creatures.description: These legendary creatures lurking in remote lakes always seem to find their way into the tavern tales of lakeside communities.

creatures.name: White Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The most feral and least intelligent of all the chromatic dragons, white dragons are brutish, predatory, and chiefly motivated by self-preservation.

creatures.name: White Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: The most feral and least intelligent of all the chromatic dragons, white dragons are brutish, predatory, and chiefly motivated by self-preservation.

creatures.name: Zuishin
creatures.description: Zuishin are kami warriors and archers tasked with watching over important shrines, ancient gates, or sacred doorways.

creatures.name: Argorth
creatures.description: An argorth is a huge, worm-like creature with three massive spiked mandibles surrounding its gnashing maw.

creatures.name: Ash Giant
creatures.description: Rugged and covered in pustules and sores from wandering harsh wastelands, ash giants dwell in the unwelcoming wilderness.

creatures.name: Atrixyl
creatures.description: Very rarely, when sinspawn (Monster Core 310) sacrifce sentient beings to runewells, instead of sinspawn, an altogether different sort of aberration is created.

creatures.name: Black Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Murderous tyrants of marshes, swamps, bogs, and fens, black dragons terrorize their domains with intense fervor.

creatures.name: Black Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Murderous tyrants of marshes, swamps, bogs, and fens, black dragons terrorize their domains with intense fervor.

creatures.name: Brainchild
creatures.description: A rumor can become so vivid and persistent that it comes to life, creating a brainchild—a living illusion that hatches from an intense belief in a remorseless and implacable killer.

creatures.name: Brass Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Brass dragons are whimsical tricksters who delight in humor and play.

creatures.name: Brass Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Brass dragons are whimsical tricksters who delight in humor and play.

creatures.name: Cloud Giant
creatures.description: The graceful and regal cloud giants are fractious, with roughly half their clans believing they should rule over humanoids regardless of their size and the rest shying away from any contact with outsiders.

creatures.name: Deadly Mantis
creatures.description: These gigantic mantids make their homes within prehistoric forests. These predators possess lightning-quick forelegs and a bone-breaking bite.

creatures.name: Devourer
creatures.description: When fiends and powerful evil spellcasters are lost beyond the farthest reaches of the multiverse, they sometimes return as horrific undead called devourers that consume the souls of the living to fuel their arcane machinations.

creatures.name: Diabolic Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Hell, according to some theologians, is a living entity in and of itself.

creatures.name: Diabolic Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Hell, according to some theologians, is a living entity in and of itself.

creatures.name: Elemental Avalanche
creatures.description: Stubborn and ponderous, elemental avalanches are massive beings of living rock and dirt.

creatures.name: Elemental Hurricane
creatures.description: Elemental hurricanes embody the ferocity of violent windstorms. Hailing from the Plane of Air, these beings appear in a variety of sizes and shapes. They're noted for being elusive, swift, and often difficult to detect due to being composed primarily of air.

creatures.name: Elemental Inferno
creatures.description: Walking conflagrations of unimaginably hot fire, elemental infernos are harbingers of destruction and heedless chaos.

creatures.name: Elemental Tsunami
creatures.description: Elemental tsunamis are huge and destructive, having none of the caring or nurturing aspects of water.

creatures.name: Garadasura
creatures.description: Garadasuras are massive ophidian spirits who hold to duties of guardianship and butchery.

creatures.name: Gold Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Gold dragons are the epitome of metallic dragonkind, unrivaled in their strength as well as their wisdom.

creatures.name: Gold Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Gold dragons are the epitome of metallic dragonkind, unrivaled in their strength as well as their wisdom.

creatures.name: Goliath Spider
creatures.description: Goliath spiders dwell in the deepest jungles, where they build webs as big as temples and feast on prey as large as hippopotami. Few everyday vermin inspire as much dread as the infamous spider.

creatures.name: Gongorinan
creatures.description: While most qlippoths focus on wiping mortals from existence, gongorinans follow the divergent plan of trapping mortals in forms incapable of understanding their actions and performing any sin meriting condemnation to the Outer Rifts.

creatures.name: Gosreg
creatures.description: Gosregs are agents of the Dominion of the Black who insinuate themselves into humanoid societies to implement the goals of their sinister masters.

creatures.name: Greater Nightmare
creatures.description: The enormous greater nightmare is valued for its ability to invade other realities with its rider. Nightmares are flaming equine harbingers of death.

creatures.name: Hamatula
creatures.description: Hamatulas are forged in Erebus to protect infernal vaults from those foolish enough to try to rob the archdevil Mammon.

creatures.name: Harmona
creatures.description: Catching a glimpse of a harmona is said to be lucky.

creatures.name: Hezrou
creatures.description: Hezrous dwell in the vast and endless Abyssal swamps, mires, and waterways and are equally at home on land and in the water.

creatures.name: High Roller
creatures.description: Those with a particular mastery over luck can make a living through games of chance as professional gamblers. These lone wolves have an aura of mystery, bravado, and swagger.

creatures.name: Hippopotamus Topiary
creatures.description: One of the rarest types of living topiaries is the hippopotamus.

creatures.name: Isqulug
creatures.description: The isqulug's appearance, though unsettling, might be its least awful aspect.

creatures.name: Kongamato
creatures.description: Though they resemble pterosaurs and hail from a similar era, kongamatos are actually a primeval branch of dragonkind.

creatures.name: Larabay
creatures.description: Larabays are bright-eyed, humanoid-looking fey with colorful wings and needlelike teeth who generally reside along warm coastal regions and islands.

creatures.name: Maestro
creatures.description: A maestro is a performer who has achieved true excellence.

creatures.name: Meladaemon
creatures.description: Meladaemons personify death by starvation and thirst, and revel in spreading the same despair that brought about their mortal demise.

creatures.name: Munagola
creatures.description: Munagolas are some of the most feared demonic warriors in Hell, combining the aerial strength and craftiness of erinyes with sheer brute force rivaling that of the strongest devils.

creatures.name: Omen Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Fate is a fickle matter on Golarion. Even with prophecy broken on the world, there are ways to look to the immediate future or acquire a vague sense of long-term events. Omen dragons are bound to see the future—nebulous though it might be—at all times. Visions of the future hound them like a quiet …

creatures.name: Omen Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Fate is a fickle matter on Golarion. Even with prophecy broken on the world, there are ways to look to the immediate future or acquire a vague sense of long-term events. Omen dragons are bound to see the future—nebulous though it might be—at all times. Visions of the future hound them like a quiet …

creatures.name: Quoppopak
creatures.description: Quoppopaks combine the most terrifying aspects of water striders, octopuses, and vampire bats into one evil, water-dwelling monster.

creatures.name: Raelis
creatures.description: Formed from the souls of storytellers, these boisterous azatas roam the planes, seeking out ever more impressive stories to collect.

creatures.name: Rancorous Priesthood
creatures.description: Followers of Rovagug must usually conceal their devotion to the Rough Beast, hiding in remote cave complexes or abandoned ruins, but they emerge when they sense weakness, gathering together into a destructive, homicidal mob to hunt down priests of rival deities or slaughter entire towns.

creatures.name: Requiem Dragon (Young, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Requiem dragons are stewards of the River of Souls and the process through which souls reach their final destination in the afterlife.

creatures.name: Requiem Dragon (Young)
creatures.description: Requiem dragons are stewards of the River of Souls and the process through which souls reach their final destination in the afterlife.

creatures.name: Seething Spirit
creatures.description: Hatred so powerful it lives beyond the grave-this sentiment animates the ephemeral crimson apparitions known as seething spirits.

creatures.name: Skeleton Infantry
creatures.description: This troop of skeletons was once a cohort of highly disciplined spear-and-shield infantry from an ancient empire. Almost any creature that had bones in life and leaves them behind in death can become a shambling, undead skeleton-humanoids, beasts, aberrations, fey, and more.

creatures.name: Spinosaurus
creatures.description: The spinosaurus is more than just one of the largest carnivorous dinosaurs—it's also one of the most unusual in appearance, with a large, sail-like fin running along its spine.

creatures.name: Spiral Centurion
creatures.description: These mechanical constructs were created to serve as guardians in an ancient and bygone era, although exactly who made them and the secrets of their construction have long since been lost to history.

creatures.name: Spirit Binder
creatures.description: Ghosts and other spirits swirl around the spirit binder, creating a constant aura of flickering faces and forms. Hidden secrets and occult powers have an irresistible lure for many. Since the majority of these NPCs are spellcasters, consider using alternative spell lists to adjust their themes.

creatures.name: Stone Bulwark
creatures.description: Stone bulwarks are slow and steady constructs typically carved from marble or granite.

creatures.name: Stone Golem
creatures.description: Stone golems are slow and steady constructs typically carved from marble or granite.

creatures.name: Thunderbird
creatures.description: Thunderbirds bring storms on their wings. In times of drought, they are welcomed. Other times, they are offered gifts in hopes they might leave before flooding begins. When angered, they call down hurricanes and lay waste to entire villages, so many settlements conduct extensive rites to appease an…

creatures.name: Unrisen
creatures.description: An unrisen is a mangled conglomeration of splintered bones, decaying organs, and rotting flesh, created when a ritual such as resurrect goes catastrophically wrong.

creatures.name: Vigilia
creatures.description: Axis's construct guardians are built in great numbers by axiomites and regularly patrol the streets of that rigidly structured metropolis.

creatures.name: Whisper Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Whisper dragons are keen collectors of rumors and secrets who spend centuries honing their information networks and relationships.

creatures.name: Whisper Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Whisper dragons are keen collectors of rumors and secrets who spend centuries honing their information networks and relationships.

creatures.name: Aeolaeka
creatures.description: Aeolaekas, also known as stone azatas, embody the joy of stone carved by artists' hands or natural forces.

creatures.name: Akhana
creatures.description: Akhanas are massive eyes formed of cosmic matter that monitor the balance of birth and death.

creatures.name: Arboreal Archive
creatures.description: Arboreal archives are solitary beings typically found in the remote wilderness.

creatures.name: Athach
creatures.description: The towering and brutish giants known as athaches are reviled for both their monstrosity and their cruelty.

creatures.name: Betobeto-San
creatures.description: A betobeto-san wanders the highways and byways of the Material Plane, searching for unwilling traveling companions to sustain the spirit's appetite for fear.

creatures.name: Black Belt
creatures.description: Martial artists strive to master the art of hand-to-hand fighting.

creatures.name: Calikang
creatures.description: Calikangs are giant, blue-skinned, six-armed guardians of ancient tombs and treasuries.

creatures.name: Catoblepas
creatures.description: The catoblepas is an aggressive beast at the best of times.

creatures.name: Cauthooj
creatures.description: These large, flightless birds are deceptively agile, considering their long bodies and awkward, hopping gait.

creatures.name: Chernobue
creatures.description: The chernobue infects all creatures it encounters with itself, spreading pain and calamity wherever it flops and writhes.

creatures.name: Conspirator Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Hidden among the shadows and upper echelons of society are the conspirator dragons.

creatures.name: Conspirator Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Hidden among the shadows and upper echelons of society are the conspirator dragons.

creatures.name: Copper Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Capricious and always eager to share a good laugh, copper dragons are among the wiliest of the metallic dragons, but this by no means interferes with their mission to spread freedom in oppressive lands.

creatures.name: Copper Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Capricious and always eager to share a good laugh, copper dragons are among the wiliest of the metallic dragons, but this by no means interferes with their mission to spread freedom in oppressive lands.

creatures.name: Coral Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Coral dragons are the primal guardians of the vast living coral reefs found in oceans across Golarion.

creatures.name: Coral Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Coral dragons are the primal guardians of the vast living coral reefs found in oceans across Golarion.

creatures.name: Crime Kingpin
creatures.description: Kingpins build empires from the shadows, ruling their territory ruthlessly and keeping their business private.

creatures.name: Deific Champion of Iomedae
creatures.description: The deific champion is the paragon of champions, representing the truest form of devotion and dedication to a deity. Religions inspire devout individuals to uphold their tenets.

creatures.name: Derghodaemon
creatures.description: Derghodaemons represent death through unfathomable violence and being eaten alive.

creatures.name: Feral Sea Skull Swarm
creatures.description: Some skull swarms are crafted from the heads of feral and dangerous beasts.

creatures.name: Feral Skull Swarm
creatures.description: Some skull swarms are crafted from the heads of feral and dangerous beasts.

creatures.name: Ferrugon
creatures.description: These ram-headed devils have wings and flesh made of rusted metal.

creatures.name: Fossil Golem
creatures.description: Fossil golems are typically made from the fossilized bones of dinosaurs and other ancient megafauna, though some crafters stretch the definition and instead use magically petrified bones of dragons or even giants.

creatures.name: Frost Worm
creatures.description: The frost worm's single round, red eye gleams prominently at the head of its monstrous mass, white as a winter snowfall.

creatures.name: Gimmerling
creatures.description: Gimmerlings are small, shapeshifting fey who stage ambushes to sate their endless hunger and childish greed.

creatures.name: Girtablilu Seer
creatures.description: The highest position of authority in a girtablilu community is the group's religious leader.

creatures.name: Gogiteth
creatures.description: A gogiteth is a slavering nightmare of teeth, eyes, and hairy spiderlike legs, and its appearance is invariably seared into the minds of any who witness it.

creatures.name: Great Cyclops
creatures.description: Gigantic loners, the great cyclopes are their lesser kin writ large.

creatures.name: Green Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Green dragons are the most contemplative of the chromatic dragons as well as the most approachable.

creatures.name: Green Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Green dragons are the most contemplative of the chromatic dragons as well as the most approachable.

creatures.name: Grisantian Lion
creatures.description: The appearance of the mighty grogrisant is a once-in-a-generation event, but the descendants of those legendary beasts are well known along the World's Edge Mountains and throughout Taldor.

creatures.name: Horned Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The magic that flows through primal dragons can manifest more animalistic or bestial features in a given type of dragon.

creatures.name: Horned Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: The magic that flows through primal dragons can manifest more animalistic or bestial features in a given type of dragon.

creatures.name: Interlocutor
creatures.description: Interlocutors are the most talented surgeon-sculptors of the velstracs, carving away flesh and replacing it with new body parts of muscle, sinew, and metal.

creatures.name: Japalisura
creatures.description: Japalisuras have a perverse propensity for extracting embarrassing truths from the universe, using a combination of artifice and prophecy to obscure the truth and corrupt traditional soothsaying.

creatures.name: Kokogiak
creatures.description: As deadly on land as in the water, kokogiaks are one of the most feared predators of the tundra.

creatures.name: Kolyarut
creatures.description: Kolyarut inevitables are enforcers of bargains and punishers of those who fail to uphold them.

creatures.name: Lich
creatures.description: A wizard whose insatiable desire for arcane power eclipsed their mortal life, the lich is a truly devious and versatile spellcaster.

creatures.name: Misery Siktempora
creatures.description: Creatures born of intense pain and suffering, the armless, six-legged misery siktemporas are some of the most numerous of their kind since misery is as ubiquitous an experience as living.

creatures.name: Monadic Deva
creatures.description: Monadic devas stand vigil along the River of Souls as it passes from the mortal realm into the Ethereal Plane.

creatures.name: Omox
creatures.description: Seemingly made from living, animated filth, omoxes have no true anatomy, although they generally spend most of their time in roughly humanoid shapes, resembling some grim caricatures of half-melted humanoids.

creatures.name: Paleohemoth
creatures.description: Paleohemoths are typically made from the fossilized bones of dinosaurs and other ancient megafauna, though some crafters stretch the definition and instead use magically petrified bones of dragons or even giants.

creatures.name: Peerless Duelist
creatures.description: Rumors circulate in the saloons that an undefeated master duelist roams the roads, waiting for their next contest. These lone wolves have an aura of mystery, bravado, and swagger.

creatures.name: Resurrection Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Resurrection dragons teeter between life and death.

creatures.name: Resurrection Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Resurrection dragons teeter between life and death.

creatures.name: Rusalka
creatures.description: These androgynous, river-dwelling fey delight in manipulating the emotions of those unfortunate enough to fall into their grasp, using humiliation to break victims' wills.

creatures.name: Sea Serpent
creatures.description: These fabled beasts resemble massive snakes with long rows of finned spines down their back.

creatures.name: Shining Child
creatures.description: Shining children are wicked, enigmatic monsters that roam remote planes and untraveled corners of the universe in search of esoteric lore.

creatures.name: Shuln
creatures.description: Scourges of the upper Darklands, these enormous, mole-like monstrosities slice and burrow through solid stone with massive forearms and adamantine-strong claws.

creatures.name: Taiga Giant
creatures.description: Taiga giants prefer a nomadic lifestyle, both to keep from depleting any one area's resources while satiating their massive appetites and to satisfy a constant wanderlust.

creatures.name: Tidehawk
creatures.description: Tidehawks, avian embodiments of the sea and rain, nest in deep underwater crevasses and glide among waves.

creatures.name: Tomb Giant
creatures.description: Masters of mausoleums and keepers of crypts, the dread creatures called tomb giants are anathema to all living beings, but especially so to other types of giants.

creatures.name: Valkyrie
creatures.description: The song of the valkyries plays wherever steel rings against steel.

creatures.name: Worm Prophet
creatures.description: Even though they believe they'll dwell with their deity in the afterlife, when nearing death, some priests desperately call out to any gods willing to listen.

creatures.name: Xiuh Coatl
creatures.description: Fiercer than other coatls, xiuh coatls (pronounced "shoo") are dedicated to seeking out malevolent creatures and offering a final opportunity at redemption.

creatures.name: Adachros
creatures.description: When a nascent demiplane fails to coalesce, its quintessence sometimes collapses into an adachros, a dangerous entity that combines overwhelming mental potential with no exposure to alternate viewpoints.

creatures.name: Adamantine Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The powerful adamantine dragons are one of several dragons known as skymetal dragons.

creatures.name: Adamantine Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: The powerful adamantine dragons are one of several dragons known as skymetal dragons.

creatures.name: Amalgamite
creatures.description: Teleportation magic can prove a difficult undertaking, even for experienced spellcasters.

creatures.name: Ambush Copse
creatures.description: Sawed and axe-hewn timber grind together as an ambush copse moves, smashing through the forest.

creatures.name: Anguished Flame
creatures.description: Ancient tales tell that the elemental lord Atreia created his six-winged children to steward mortal prayers into the realms of the divine.

creatures.name: Animated Trebuchet
creatures.description: Large armies sometimes pay exorbitant fees to animate their siege weapons. Many animated objects have useful functions but become dangers when uncontrolled.

creatures.name: Assault Alloy
creatures.description: Creature imported from Foundry PF2e data.

creatures.name: Ayngavhaul
creatures.description: Where a bright mind seeking knowledge becomes corrupted by eloquent lies and twisted truths, an ayngavhaul is sure to lurk.

creatures.name: Blue Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Blue dragons are the sleek and poised cosmopolitans of the chromatic dragons.

creatures.name: Blue Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Blue dragons are the sleek and poised cosmopolitans of the chromatic dragons.

creatures.name: Bronze Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Bronze dragons are among the most common of metallic dragons and the most likely to ally with mortals on worthy quests.

creatures.name: Bronze Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Bronze dragons are among the most common of metallic dragons and the most likely to ally with mortals on worthy quests.

creatures.name: Carnivorous Blob
creatures.description: Carnivorous blobs are the ravenous spawn of shattered worlds far beyond the stars, born across the galaxy in inert form until they fall like meteorites onto unsuspecting worlds.

creatures.name: Cave Worm
creatures.description: The most common and infamous of the cave worms gives its name to the entire family—a much-feared monster wandering the twisting tunnels of the Darklands that is capable of carving out entire cave systems.

creatures.name: Consonite Choir
creatures.description: Floods of crystalline blades called consonite choirs surge through the caverns and tunnels of the Plane of Earth.

creatures.name: Despair Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Fear is one of the most powerful emotions, and despair dragons are masters of channeling those feelings of terror and hopelessness for their own benefit.

creatures.name: Despair Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Fear is one of the most powerful emotions, and despair dragons are masters of channeling those feelings of terror and hopelessness for their own benefit.

creatures.name: Destiny Tempest
creatures.description: Before the Jaathoom Empire, the Plane of Air was ruled by destiny tempests from their Reverient Empire of Lost Nights.
languages: cant speak any language, Telepathy 100, Truespeech

creatures.name: Dibrasgorth
creatures.description: Dibrasgorths, sometimes called Mothers of Oblivion, are monstrous creatures of chaos who dwell in lightless spaces, often near the bottom of deep lakes and oceans both above and underground.

creatures.name: Dryad Queen
creatures.description: Also called a hamadryad, a dryad queen rules over an entire forest, or a portion of an incredibly large forest, leading and protecting all dryads within.

creatures.name: Elven Court Guard
creatures.description: In the tangled web of lineages, rivalries, and shifting alliances that is an elven noble court, aristocratic families employ bodyguards loyal to them alone. Elves' long lives give them centuries to delve into studies, artistry, or exploration.

creatures.name: Exscinder
creatures.description: Mortals require protection from forbidden knowledge and heretical texts.

creatures.name: Froghemoth
creatures.description: A terrifying abomination of teeth, tentacles, and unbridled rage, the froghemoth is thankfully rarely encountered even in the deepest swamplands or most remote Darklands caverns.

creatures.name: Gelugon
creatures.description: Insectile ice devils are strategists and masterminds in Hell's armies, using their superior intellect to strike against their enemies and spread Hell's influence throughout the planes.

creatures.name: Ghaele
creatures.description: Ghaeles are fiend-hunting knights of Elysium and champions of the freedom to take up arms against oppressors and other evils.

creatures.name: Glabrezu
creatures.description: Glabrezus are looming behemoths who revel in the act of twisting mortal desires into their ultimate ruin, betraying and manipulating as easily as they breathe.

creatures.name: Globetrotting Scholar
creatures.description: It takes a particular kind of personality to leave the creature comforts of academia in order to explore trap-ridden ancient tombs or observe man-eating monstrosities in their native habitats.

creatures.name: Hero Hunter
creatures.description: Some hunters grow bored of simple beasts and monsters. For them, a battle-tested warrior is the finest prey. Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: Iceberg Clam
creatures.description: Though technically neither an iceberg nor clam, the devastating elemental known as an iceberg clam has earned its appropriate name from its natural camouflage and feeding tendencies.

creatures.name: Irlgaunt
creatures.description: Irlgaunts resemble titanic spiders or crabs, but with cephalopod-like tendrils erupting from the tips of their chitin-armored legs.

creatures.name: Iron Golem
creatures.description: Traditionally crafted into the forms of giant suits of armor or powerful animals, iron golems are products of exquisite artistry and skill.

creatures.name: Iron Warden
creatures.description: Traditionally crafted into the forms of giant suits of armor or powerful animals, iron wardens are products of exquisite artistry and skill.

creatures.name: Jorogumo
creatures.description: Appearing as beautiful, well-dressed humans, jorogumo lurk in the high mountains and prey on travelers, often through words and charm.

creatures.name: Juggernaut
creatures.description: The heavy suit of mechanical metal armor a juggernaut wears is custom-built and highly complex and specialized to its wearer.

creatures.name: Leng Spider
creatures.description: The monstrous, bloated spiders from the windswept realm of Leng build eerie, dangerous lairs with the aid of magically compelled slaves. Leng spiders have anywhere from five to 13 legs, but never an even number.

creatures.name: Living Rune (Arcane)
creatures.description: The most famous living runes are those of the ancient Runelords of Thassilon, resurging as Thassilon rebuilds in the modern era.

creatures.name: Living Rune (Divine)
creatures.description: The most famous living runes are those of the ancient Runelords of Thassilon, resurging as Thassilon rebuilds in the modern era.

creatures.name: Living Rune (Occult)
creatures.description: The most famous living runes are those of the ancient Runelords of Thassilon, resurging as Thassilon rebuilds in the modern era.

creatures.name: Living Rune (Primal)
creatures.description: The most famous living runes are those of the ancient Runelords of Thassilon, resurging as Thassilon rebuilds in the modern era.

creatures.name: Millindemalion
creatures.description: Millindemalion are cruel fey tricksters capable of causing mayhem with their magical, mind-altering hats.

creatures.name: Mirage Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Mirage dragons are masters of illusion magic and use their powers to deceive others and further their own agendas.

creatures.name: Mirage Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Mirage dragons are masters of illusion magic and use their powers to deceive others and further their own agendas.

creatures.name: Owb Prophet
creatures.description: An owb who comes into contact and is chosen by one of the Forsaken gains a fragment of that demigod's power and forges a permanent connection with it.
languages: cant speak any languages, Telepathy 100 feet

creatures.name: Phase Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: No place can contain a phase dragon or even hold their interest for long; their innate arcane connection ties them to teleportation and repositioning magic.

creatures.name: Phase Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: No place can contain a phase dragon or even hold their interest for long; their innate arcane connection ties them to teleportation and repositioning magic.

creatures.name: Quai Dau To
creatures.description: This big-headed beast is covered in glittering scales; its eyes are like large copper pots, and its mouth is full of sharp teeth.

creatures.name: Shadow Giant
creatures.description: Shadow giants are natives of the Netherworld, where they have dwelled in perpetual twilight for millennia.

creatures.name: Skeletal Titan
creatures.description: Only the powerful and foolish would raise the bones of a mighty titan or similarly gargantuan creature as a skeleton.

creatures.name: Snow Oni
creatures.description: Snow oni maintain their seething temperaments even in their icy homes.

creatures.name: Storm Giant
creatures.description: Storm giants are looming but benevolent stewards of sea and sky, often serving as the natural guardians of tropical islands, coastlines, or rocky archipelagos.

creatures.name: Terra Carver
creatures.description: Even other earth elementals and creatures of stone fear the formidable terra carver.

creatures.name: Terra-Cotta Garrison
creatures.description: These constructed warriors can work together as a trained group to repel intruders.

creatures.name: Thanadaemon
creatures.description: Also known as the deacons of death, thanadaemons represent death via old age.

creatures.name: Tide Giant
creatures.description: On secluded beaches where the waves lap across pristine, untouched sands, tide giants dwell in solitude at the shore.

creatures.name: Tree Singer
creatures.description: Singers can speak the words of creation in song, a forgotten art from the First World.

creatures.name: Urglid
creatures.description: The hulking monstrosities known as urglids form from the souls of murderous undertakers, sadists who buried their victims alive, and intentionally neglectful grave keepers who gave up their watch over the dead.

creatures.name: Vanyver
creatures.description: Vanyvers are large, humanoid bats shaped from void and shadowstuff, their red eyes glowing like tiny stars in an otherwise lightless night.

creatures.name: Venator
creatures.description: Unlike most aeons, venators spend much of their time in the mortal Universe.

creatures.name: Viper Vine
creatures.description: A voracious, flesh-eating carnivore, the viper vine has a single enormous blossom arising from a thick, leafy tangle of snakelike vines.

creatures.name: Vrykolakas Ancient
creatures.description: Vrykolakas ancients are the sinister overlords of their kind, stealing blood, breath, and life to feed their immortal hunger.

creatures.name: Augnagar
creatures.description: The brutish and gluttonous augnagar live to feast—preferably on rotten flesh and, when possible, demon flesh.

creatures.name: Caldera Oni
creatures.description: As hot-blooded as the lava that floods their homes, caldera oni have an incredible appetite for the thrill of war.

creatures.name: Cinder Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Among the largest and fiercest dragons, cinder dragons are typically volatile, demanding respect—even deference—from lesser creatures.

creatures.name: Cinder Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Among the largest and fiercest dragons, cinder dragons are typically volatile, demanding respect—even deference—from lesser creatures.

creatures.name: Crag Linnorm
creatures.description: Despite being among the weakest linnorms, the crag linnorm is a devastating predator, capable of quickly cooking their foes with their magma breath.

creatures.name: Curse Monger
creatures.description: Oracles who have been consumed by their visions and the divine gifts bestowed upon them turn to more sinister paths, becoming curse mongers.

creatures.name: Deep One Elder
creatures.description: Given enough time, deep one elders can swell to dizzying heights. Lumbering, amphibious, and deathless humanoids known as deep ones inhabit coastal areas and deep ocean trenches all across Golarion. Though most simply wish to be left alone, others work tirelessly to grow their ranks.

creatures.name: Doprillu
creatures.description: The aberrations known as doprillus are hulks with banded muscles who wear ornate masks at all times that fill their wearers with magical strength and fighting spirit.

creatures.name: Dramofir
creatures.description: Beings of inscrutable intent and incredible power, dramofirs hail from the Dreamlands and shamble across the multiverse in search of dreams to incorporate into their voluminous robes.

creatures.name: Empyreal Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The three major celestial planes—Heaven, Nirvana, and Elysium—each have their own respective dragons.

creatures.name: Empyreal Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: The three major celestial planes—Heaven, Nirvana, and Elysium—each have their own respective dragons.

creatures.name: Fortune Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Fortune dragons have the innate ability to draw upon the raw magical energies that surround them.

creatures.name: Fortune Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Fortune dragons have the innate ability to draw upon the raw magical energies that surround them.

creatures.name: Graveknight Warmaster
creatures.description: Exemplars of undying violence, graveknight warmasters are devastating forces on the battlefield, able to spur allies to ever greater levels of violence. When a fearsome combatant falls in battle, the warrior's vengeful spirit can sometimes fuse with their armor, creating a graveknight.

creatures.name: Grikkitog
creatures.description: Grikkitogs, also known as the "hungry earth," are strange parasites from the Plane of Earth that infest earth, rock, and stone in order to feed their endless hunger.

creatures.name: Gylou
creatures.description: Though gylous are deeply entrenched in the expansive and complex machinations of Hell, they are highly skilled agents capable of nuanced diplomacy, masterful deception, physical finesse, and nearly any other tasks they set their minds to.

creatures.name: Kuchisake-Onna
creatures.description: A kuchisake-onna, or slit-mouthed woman, is a tall, masked figure who moves with an eerie grace.

creatures.name: Mezlan
creatures.description: The bizarre shapeshifters known as mezlans were created ages ago as spies and infiltrators by a long-forgotten ritual infusing a magically constructed ooze with the soul of an exceptional sapient being.

creatures.name: Nalfeshnee
creatures.description: Nalfeshnees are huge, corpulent, boar-headed demons that hoard treasures and knowledge alike and form from the souls of avaricious mortals.

creatures.name: Nightmarchers
creatures.description: While smooth roads remain a traveler's blessing, walking them after sunset risks nightmarcher attack.

creatures.name: Nikaramsa
creatures.description: Lies, knowledge, and deception are a nikaramsa's stock and trade.

creatures.name: Ozthoom
creatures.description: Ozthooms are shadowy killers who serve powerful fey creatures or even the Eldest—the demigods of the First World.
languages: Can't speak any language, Telepathy 100 feet

creatures.name: Ozthoom Shadow Double
creatures.description: Ozthooms are shadowy killers who serve powerful fey creatures or even the Eldest—the demigods of the First World.
languages: Can't speak any language, Telepathy 100 feet

creatures.name: Peri
creatures.description: Peris are contrary, artful celestials renowned for their beauty as much as their deceptive natures. Mercurial, though never malicious, peris strive to aid mortals, though they are more likely to dispense riddles and quests than to offer aid outright.

creatures.name: Plague Giant
creatures.description: Plague giants are rasping behemoths, whose weeping sores, fetid breath, and filthy fingernails all bear a terrible wasting disease.

creatures.name: Ravener Husk
creatures.description: Raveners require a steady diet of souls, and a ravener that's unable to feed for too long eventually cannibalizes their own soul.

creatures.name: Red Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The largest and most powerful of the chromatic dragons, red dragons are a menace to civilizations everywhere, and their strength is rivaled only by their arrogance.

creatures.name: Red Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: The largest and most powerful of the chromatic dragons, red dragons are a menace to civilizations everywhere, and their strength is rivaled only by their arrogance.

creatures.name: Rune Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Rune dragons have an innate insight on the power of runes.

creatures.name: Rune Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Rune dragons have an innate insight on the power of runes.

creatures.name: Sepid
creatures.description: Sepids are the brutal warlords of the divs, delighting in violence against mortals and their own particular flavor of vengeance.

creatures.name: Siegebreaker
creatures.description: When a well-constructed or magically warded fortification repels every assault, a siegebreaker is called in.

creatures.name: Silver Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Silver dragons are among the most chivalrous of all dragonkind; they wield frost and cold as weapons, can walk on clouds, and dwell high upon snowy mountain peaks or deep in steep, misty valleys.

creatures.name: Silver Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Silver dragons are among the most chivalrous of all dragonkind; they wield frost and cold as weapons, can walk on clouds, and dwell high upon snowy mountain peaks or deep in steep, misty valleys.

creatures.name: Sorcerous Sea Skull Swarm
creatures.description: The act of creating a demilich sometimes goes horribly awry, leaving behind a shattered remnant of a once-mighty spellcaster constantly leaking eldritch energies.

creatures.name: Sorcerous Skull Swarm
creatures.description: The act of creating a demilich sometimes goes horribly awry, leaving behind a shattered remnant of a once-mighty spellcaster constantly leaking eldritch energies.

creatures.name: Tabellia
creatures.description: Tabellias are the elite messengers of the celestial realms, serving deities and celestial armies by delivering messages, performing reconnaissance, and providing support for those in need of aid.

creatures.name: Triumph Siktempora
creatures.description: Triumph siktemporas embody the emotional satisfaction of overcoming a task that took up much of a creature's mind, be it taking an apple seed from between one's teeth or winning a war.

creatures.name: Trumpet Archon
creatures.description: Trumpet archons are messengers, emissaries, and storytellers-embodiments of the virtue of kindness.

creatures.name: Uthul
creatures.description: An uthul most often appears to be a dark, swirling cloud filled with flying debris and streaked with sudden flashes of lightning.

creatures.name: Vidileth
creatures.description: The powerful vidileths are the insidious veiled masters of the alghollthus.

creatures.name: Worm That Walks Cultist
creatures.description: This worm that walks was a cultist of the Great Old One Hastur in its prior life. It continues its conspiracies and strives to further its evil plots in its new life.

creatures.name: Animated Colossus
creatures.description: Animating a 100-foot-tall statue is worth the cost for spellcasters guarding immense dungeons. Many animated objects have useful functions but become dangers when uncontrolled.

creatures.name: Benthic Worm
creatures.description: The benthic worm is a deep-blue creature that is more at home in flooded tunnels than dry caves.

creatures.name: Berberoka
creatures.description: Berberokas are giant humanoids who live among forests and swamps, where they use their ability to swallow massive amounts of water to drain small ponds and lakes.

creatures.name: Black Scorpion
creatures.description: With a carapace the color of polished obsidian and a penchant for attacking villages, this humongous scorpion is one of the desert's most frightening predators. A black scorpion measures 60 feet from tip to the base of its stinger.

creatures.name: Deific Vessel of Urgathoa
creatures.description: Through direct intervention in the mortal world, a deity creates a deific vessel to do their will, whether from a willing servant or through possession, a contract, or a curse.

creatures.name: Demilich
creatures.description: Demiliches are formed when a lich, through carelessness or by accident, loses its phylactery.

creatures.name: Diabolic Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Hell, according to some theologians, is a living entity in and of itself.

creatures.name: Diabolic Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Hell, according to some theologians, is a living entity in and of itself.

creatures.name: Dybbuk
creatures.description: The discorporated spirits called dybbuks arise from souls denied respite in the afterlife, often because they committed some great transgression in life.

creatures.name: Flamboyant Thief
creatures.description: There's no honor among thieves, but if there were points for style, these thieves would have 10s across the board.

creatures.name: Gold Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Gold dragons are the epitome of metallic dragonkind, unrivaled in their strength as well as their wisdom.

creatures.name: Gold Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Gold dragons are the epitome of metallic dragonkind, unrivaled in their strength as well as their wisdom.

creatures.name: Grand Inquisitor
creatures.description: A grand inquisitor leads powerful governmental forces.

creatures.name: Graveknight Champion
creatures.description: Once the servant of a deity, the graveknight champion returned as an undead after a life cut short in service to their god. When a fearsome combatant falls in battle, the warrior's vengeful spirit can sometimes fuse with their armor, creating a graveknight.

creatures.name: Hyakume
creatures.description: Hundreds of bloodshot eyes peek out from under the fleshy layers of a hyakume's skin.

creatures.name: Jotund Troll
creatures.description: Jotund trolls are gigantic, nine-headed horrors who prowl frigid moors, marshes, and wastelands, often alone and always ravenous.

creatures.name: Lampad Queen
creatures.description: Lampad queens are capricious monarchs and protectors of vast underground domains, regarded in ballads and tales as allies and foes, monsters and muses.

creatures.name: Marrmora
creatures.description: On the First World, marrmoras dwell in ruined wildlands perpetually scourged by fire and rarely, if ever, travel elsewhere.

creatures.name: Marut
creatures.description: A marut is tasked with hunting mortals who cheat death by artificially extending their lifespans.

creatures.name: Morrigna
creatures.description: Bounty hunters and investigators, morrignas seek out creatures who thwart death or interfere with the natural flow of souls.

creatures.name: Mukradi
creatures.description: Fearsome centipede-like creatures, mukradis are three-headed predators with a devastating array of ways to kill, burn, and dismember.

creatures.name: Nemhaith
creatures.description: This undead guardian stands amid a whirling swarm of shackled spirits that rage against their fate.

creatures.name: Neothelid
creatures.description: This gigantic, mauve worm has a hooked mouth with multiple circular bands of teeth and four barbed tongues.

creatures.name: Nosferatu Overlord
creatures.description: Creature imported from Foundry PF2e data.

creatures.name: Okenevem
creatures.description: The most powerful archons reside in Iudica, the sixth tier on the holy mountain of Heaven.

creatures.name: Peerless Healer
creatures.description: Peerless healers are legendary, blending divine and natural medicine. Often pillars in their respective communities, they see to the health of the populace. The world is a dangerous place. Thankfully, there are those who devote their lives to easing the pain and suffering of others.

creatures.name: Phoenix
creatures.description: The phoenix is a primordial bird made of heat and flame that dwells in the most inhospitable regions of the desert.

creatures.name: Popobawa
creatures.description: Cruel shapechangers, popobawas feed on negative emotions, particularly fear, despair, and anguish.

creatures.name: Quelaunt
creatures.description: This three-armed, three-legged monster has no discernible eyes, nose, ears, or mouth, and no visible means of ingesting food.

creatures.name: Requiem Dragon (Adult, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Requiem dragons are stewards of the River of Souls and the process through which souls reach their final destination in the afterlife.

creatures.name: Requiem Dragon (Adult)
creatures.description: Requiem dragons are stewards of the River of Souls and the process through which souls reach their final destination in the afterlife.

creatures.name: Sakugami
creatures.description: Sakugami are especially powerful kami who protect seasonally blossoming trees, particularly ones in places where the primeval powers of nature remain strong.

creatures.name: Seraptis
creatures.description: Four-armed seraptis demons are radical nihilists at heart, despising other creatures out of bitter entitlement and laughing hysterically when others die or suffer.

creatures.name: Shoal Linnorm
creatures.description: Shoal linnorms occupy rocky shoreline shallows and slither along lonely coastlines.

creatures.name: Sordesdaemon
creatures.description: Sordesdaemons are hulks of sewage and daemonic flesh that embody death through pollution.

creatures.name: Sramana
creatures.description: Sramanas, or renouncer angels, exist to help liberate the penitent and ease their suffering.

creatures.name: Sykever
creatures.description: The most common darvakkas are sykevers, walkers in the night.

creatures.name: Tolokand
creatures.description: Streams run dry. The earth cracks. Green leaves brown and shatter. And the creature walks on. The faceless, unknowable tolokands wander the land, bringing desolation with every footfall-belching poisonous smoke, blighting plant life, cracking the earth asunder, and even staining the ground with the…

creatures.name: Toshigami
creatures.description: Toshigami are especially powerful kami who protect cherry trees, particularly ones in places where the primeval powers of nature remain strong.

creatures.name: Totum Font
creatures.description: Before mortals, fey, or even genies, totum fonts were the first creatures born of six elements undivided and harmonious.

creatures.name: Umonlee
creatures.description: Those who dwell in the coldest climates of Golarion tell of a brutal beast that swims through the ice and snow and can devastate entire villages in minutes.

creatures.name: Wemmuth
creatures.description: Fertilized by large quantities of spilled blood, such as that found on bloodstained battlefields or in the war-torn regions surrounding besieged cities, wemmuths are vile swaths of vines that draw sustenance from mortal suffering, lapping up blood like water.

creatures.name: White Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The most feral and least intelligent of all the chromatic dragons, white dragons are brutish, predatory, and chiefly motivated by self-preservation.

creatures.name: White Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: The most feral and least intelligent of all the chromatic dragons, white dragons are brutish, predatory, and chiefly motivated by self-preservation.

creatures.name: Zhuraita
creatures.description: Zhuraitas are dedicated to the freedom of academic and scientifc inquiry, protecting the creativity and inventiveness of scholarly felds.

creatures.name: Astradaemon
creatures.description: These unnerving daemons represent death by direct assault against a soul or life-force.

creatures.name: Black Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Murderous tyrants of marshes, swamps, bogs, and fens, black dragons terrorize their domains with intense fervor.

creatures.name: Black Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Murderous tyrants of marshes, swamps, bogs, and fens, black dragons terrorize their domains with intense fervor.

creatures.name: Brass Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Brass dragons are whimsical tricksters who delight in humor and play.

creatures.name: Brass Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Brass dragons are whimsical tricksters who delight in humor and play.

creatures.name: Bythos
creatures.description: The bythos is a guardian of space and time and ever seeks those who misuse planar and temporal magic.

creatures.name: Clockwork Dragon
creatures.description: Clockwork dragons are a marvel of clockwork design.

creatures.name: Cornugon
creatures.description: Hell's armies contain legions upon legions of different devils all suited precisely to their roles.

creatures.name: Elder Sphinx
creatures.description: Towering over their kin, elder sphinxes are the pharaonic masters of their kind.

creatures.name: Fjord Linnorm
creatures.description: Fjord linnorms make their homes in damp caves behind ice-cold waterfalls in the river-veined coastal reaches of the north.

creatures.name: Giylea
creatures.description: Giyleas are known as wheel archons, named for their appearance as a flying, armor-plated wheel of fire with eyes on each spoke.

creatures.name: Lesser Death
creatures.description: No one is quite sure what lesser deaths are, though some claim that they are avatars of the Grim Reaper.

creatures.name: Love Siktempora
creatures.description: Acts of great and intense love spawn love siktemporas, kindly creatures bearing intense strength that start and end wars in the name of the love that birthed them.

creatures.name: Mithral Golem
creatures.description: Mithral golems are gorgeously crafted, silvery humanoids made from colossal amounts of the purest dawnsilver.

creatures.name: Omen Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Fate is a fickle matter on Golarion. Even with prophecy broken on the world, there are ways to look to the immediate future or acquire a vague sense of long-term events. Omen dragons are bound to see the future—nebulous though it might be—at all times. Visions of the future hound them like a quiet …

creatures.name: Omen Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Fate is a fickle matter on Golarion. Even with prophecy broken on the world, there are ways to look to the immediate future or acquire a vague sense of long-term events. Omen dragons are bound to see the future—nebulous though it might be—at all times. Visions of the future hound them like a quiet …

creatures.name: Planetar
creatures.description: The beings of righteous fury known as planetars are known to be the least patient of angels.

creatures.name: Rune Giant
creatures.description: Rune giants are tyrants among their own kind, given power to command and magically control other giants.

creatures.name: Shemhazian
creatures.description: Shemhazians rise from the souls of torturers and those who reveled in mutilating the physical bodies of their victims.

creatures.name: Sumbreiva
creatures.description: Sumbreivas are the Void's unstoppable hunters, tracking down and destroying other creatures on their plane for sport and practice.

creatures.name: Thousand Thieves
creatures.description: Good help is hard to find. For a master thief unable to find competent accomplices, sometimes the only way to execute the perfect heist involves becoming an entire guild of tiny creatures. Those who purposefully transform in this way favor scuttling creatures such as centipedes or spiders. On occas…

creatures.name: Vilderavn
creatures.description: In their true form, a vilderavn is a great raven with a wingspan of 6–8 feet.

creatures.name: Warsworn
creatures.description: A warsworn is an animate mass of corpses composed of dozens, sometimes even hundreds, of victims of battle. They are formed by deities of undeath or war or, rarely, spontaneously manifest from the devastation of an especially horrendous battle.

creatures.name: Whisper Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Whisper dragons are keen collectors of rumors and secrets who spend centuries honing their information networks and relationships.

creatures.name: Whisper Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Whisper dragons are keen collectors of rumors and secrets who spend centuries honing their information networks and relationships.

creatures.name: World Ender
creatures.description: Unlike most villains, world enders are unburdened by nuance. Their ultimate goal is simple, if lofty: destroy the world and everyone in it. Villains pursue selfish and cruel goals, trampling over anyone in their way.

creatures.name: Zaramuun
creatures.description: Zaramuuns are beings of elemental sand that hide in deserts and wastelands.

creatures.name: Zomok
creatures.description: Mighty plant-dragons, zomoks prowl the woodlands, defending their homes from destructive forces while healing and restoring the woods following natural disasters and ravaging intruders.

creatures.name: Bandersnatch
creatures.description: Bandersnatches are great six-legged cats with wicked quills running down the length of their bodies down to the tips of their mighty tails.

creatures.name: Banshee
creatures.description: Banshees are the furious, tormented souls of those bound to the world by a betrayal that defined the final hours of their lives.

creatures.name: Conspirator Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Hidden among the shadows and upper echelons of society are the conspirator dragons.

creatures.name: Conspirator Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Hidden among the shadows and upper echelons of society are the conspirator dragons.

creatures.name: Copper Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Capricious and always eager to share a good laugh, copper dragons are among the wiliest of the metallic dragons, but this by no means interferes with their mission to spread freedom in oppressive lands.

creatures.name: Copper Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Capricious and always eager to share a good laugh, copper dragons are among the wiliest of the metallic dragons, but this by no means interferes with their mission to spread freedom in oppressive lands.

creatures.name: Coral Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Coral dragons are the primal guardians of the vast living coral reefs found in oceans across Golarion.

creatures.name: Coral Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Coral dragons are the primal guardians of the vast living coral reefs found in oceans across Golarion.

creatures.name: Deimavigga
creatures.description: As masters of cold, calculated logic and perfectly timed proselytization, the loathsome deimaviggas seek to set friend against friend and turn the faithful from their beliefs at every opportunity.

creatures.name: Eldritch Emeritus
creatures.description: To outsiders, the eldritch emeritus looks something like a joke—a befuddled old scholar, their mind so stuffed with obscure theorems and abstract metaphysics that concerns about mere daily reality fade away.

creatures.name: Eseneth
creatures.description: Eseneths, commonly known as soul-stitchers, are dispassionate surgeons who repair damaged souls.

creatures.name: Grandmaster
creatures.description: Martial artists strive to master the art of hand-to-hand fighting.

creatures.name: Green Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Green dragons are the most contemplative of the chromatic dragons as well as the most approachable.

creatures.name: Green Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Green dragons are the most contemplative of the chromatic dragons as well as the most approachable.

creatures.name: Horned Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The magic that flows through primal dragons can manifest more animalistic or bestial features in a given type of dragon.

creatures.name: Horned Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: The magic that flows through primal dragons can manifest more animalistic or bestial features in a given type of dragon.

creatures.name: Ice Linnorm
creatures.description: The ice linnorm dwells amid glacial crevasses, atop wind-blasted mountain peaks, or within glittering caverns carved from the hearts of the mightiest of icebergs.

creatures.name: Island Oni
creatures.description: Island oni are those powerful enough to claim small coastal islands, often creating makeshift armies of lesser oni who pillage surrounding lands and waters.

creatures.name: Kadamel
creatures.description: Fiends spread corruption to places across the planes—every place they can reach.

creatures.name: Keketar
creatures.description: The ruling caste of the proteans, keketars orchestrate attacks against the bastions of law and adjudicate protean disputes confidently and capriciously.

creatures.name: Leydroth
creatures.description: In ancient times, a cabal of primalists imbued an amalgam of beasts with both supernatural might and a hatred of magic itself, hoping to achieve a potent weapon for use against arcane spellcasters.

creatures.name: Marilith
creatures.description: Mariliths are the generals of the Abyss, formed from the souls of proud evil mortals, often warlords and despots.

creatures.name: Ninkonda
creatures.description: When once-honorable mortals fall to wickedness and perform terrible atrocities, ninkondas set out to track them down.

creatures.name: Phasmadaemon
creatures.description: The horrifying phasmadaemons make use of illusions and their own frightening appearances (that of serpents with bony tails, horned crocodilian skulls for heads, and several insectile pincers) to strike fear into the hearts of others.

creatures.name: Radiant Warden
creatures.description: The enigmatic and strange radiant wardens were constructed thousands of years ago to protect observatories and scholars against the incursion of alien aggressors from the Dominion of the Black.
languages: any one ancient language (such as Jistkan)

creatures.name: Resurrection Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Resurrection dragons teeter between life and death.

creatures.name: Resurrection Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Resurrection dragons teeter between life and death.

creatures.name: Thrasfyr
creatures.description: As bizarre in appearance as they are ferocious, thrasfyrs resemble six-limbed bears with bull-like horns, draconic scales, and a fire-breathing serpent for a tail. Every thrasfyr is wrapped in animated chains psychically linked to the creature's mind.

creatures.name: Vaspercham
creatures.description: Vasperchams are aquatic horrors who delight in violence, lurking in the shallows near shorelines.

creatures.name: Wendigo
creatures.description: Wendigos are incarnations of the fears of starvation, loneliness, and fatal exposure to cold weather. They haunt the frozen expanses of the world.

creatures.name: Ximtal
creatures.description: The struggles of loneliness and the anxiety that accompanies that feeling plague many creatures, and ximtals delight in exploiting this fear for their own perverse gain.

creatures.name: Adamant Sentinel
creatures.description: Crafted from a nigh-indestructible metal of great rarity, adamant sentinels can't be destroyed except by the most powerful foes.

creatures.name: Adamantine Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The powerful adamantine dragons are one of several dragons known as skymetal dragons.

creatures.name: Adamantine Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: The powerful adamantine dragons are one of several dragons known as skymetal dragons.

creatures.name: Adamantine Golem
creatures.description: Crafted from a nigh-indestructible metal of great rarity, adamantine golems can't be destroyed except by the most powerful foes.

creatures.name: Aolaz
creatures.description: Aolazes are great beasts carved from stone and metal and magically imbued with the essence of life.

creatures.name: Blue Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Blue dragons are the sleek and poised cosmopolitans of the chromatic dragons.

creatures.name: Blue Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Blue dragons are the sleek and poised cosmopolitans of the chromatic dragons.

creatures.name: Bone Ship
creatures.description: Rarely does anything living remain after a bone ship's passing-only death, destruction, and waves red as blood that clash and foam in its wake.

creatures.name: Bronze Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Bronze dragons are among the most common of metallic dragons and the most likely to ally with mortals on worthy quests.

creatures.name: Bronze Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Bronze dragons are among the most common of metallic dragons and the most likely to ally with mortals on worthy quests.

creatures.name: Cairn Linnorm
creatures.description: Cairn linnorms are disturbing, even by linnorm standards.

creatures.name: Despair Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Fear is one of the most powerful emotions, and despair dragons are masters of channeling those feelings of terror and hopelessness for their own benefit.

creatures.name: Despair Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Fear is one of the most powerful emotions, and despair dragons are masters of channeling those feelings of terror and hopelessness for their own benefit.

creatures.name: Duneshaker Solifugid
creatures.description: The largest solifugids, duneshakers, are so immense that their movements make the terrain around them tremble. They can prey on creatures the size of mammoths or even larger.

creatures.name: Enchanting Ritualist
creatures.description: Possessing great stores of knowledge on mental magic and rituals, enchanting ritualists can also be solitary and capricious, tricking and transforming their visitors.

creatures.name: Hatred Siktempora
creatures.description: Spawned by acts and legacies of cold-blooded spite, hatred siktemporas kill indiscriminately out of pure loathing, methodically hunting down and slaughtering anyone who catches their vile attention.

creatures.name: Katpaskir
creatures.description: Born of the souls of doomsayers and cult leaders who sought to undermine and unravel reality, katpaskirs are demons of nihilism and unmaking.

creatures.name: Kraken
creatures.description: A kraken is an enormous, squid-like leviathan with a cruel intelligence.

creatures.name: Magma Worm
creatures.description: Among the most dangerous of their kind are the fiery magma worms.

creatures.name: Mirage Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Mirage dragons are masters of illusion magic and use their powers to deceive others and further their own agendas.

creatures.name: Mirage Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Mirage dragons are masters of illusion magic and use their powers to deceive others and further their own agendas.

creatures.name: Phase Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: No place can contain a phase dragon or even hold their interest for long; their innate arcane connection ties them to teleportation and repositioning magic.

creatures.name: Phase Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: No place can contain a phase dragon or even hold their interest for long; their innate arcane connection ties them to teleportation and repositioning magic.

creatures.name: Purrodaemon
creatures.description: Few creatures embody war's sheer amount of bloodshed and loss of life as deeply as the purrodaemons, deacons of war.

creatures.name: Shoggoth
creatures.description: Although even raving fanatics and doom-saying prophets desperately claim the monstrous shoggoth is nothing more than a drug-induced vision or a thankfully unreal nightmare, the truth is altogether more dire.

creatures.name: Simurgh
creatures.description: Desert-dwelling people consider the sight of a simurgh a herald to a lifetime's worth of luck, and those who are unfamiliar with the majestic beast might mistake it for the avatar of a deity.

creatures.name: Skulltaker
creatures.description: Swirling down from misty peaks and through howling mountain passes like an evil wind, the vortex of bones known as a skulltaker is a terrible manifestation of the delirium and agony experienced by doomed climbers and lost trailblazers just before they met their end.

creatures.name: Thulgant
creatures.description: Thulgants are powerful and intelligent qlippoths created from the cannibalistic feeding frenzies of augnagars.

creatures.name: Urveth
creatures.description: An urveth is a massive, four-armed burrowing terror with a wormlike body and gaping maw that devours everything it can.

creatures.name: Cinder Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Among the largest and fiercest dragons, cinder dragons are typically volatile, demanding respect—even deference—from lesser creatures.

creatures.name: Cinder Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Among the largest and fiercest dragons, cinder dragons are typically volatile, demanding respect—even deference—from lesser creatures.

creatures.name: Empyreal Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The three major celestial planes—Heaven, Nirvana, and Elysium—each have their own respective dragons.

creatures.name: Empyreal Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: The three major celestial planes—Heaven, Nirvana, and Elysium—each have their own respective dragons.

creatures.name: Fortune Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Fortune dragons have the innate ability to draw upon the raw magical energies that surround them.

creatures.name: Fortune Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Fortune dragons have the innate ability to draw upon the raw magical energies that surround them.

creatures.name: Grendel
creatures.description: This reaver of the cold marsh is not just a monster; he is a force of nature.

creatures.name: Guthallath
creatures.description: A guthallath is an enormous construct created long ago, probably as a war machine, by a long-forgotten empire.

creatures.name: Hesperid Queen
creatures.description: Hesperid queens rule over isolated regions soaked in the light of sunset.

creatures.name: Obcisidaemon
creatures.description: While war can have many facets, obcisidaemons care only for the brutality and violence that conflict brings.

creatures.name: Red Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: The largest and most powerful of the chromatic dragons, red dragons are a menace to civilizations everywhere, and their strength is rivaled only by their arrogance.

creatures.name: Red Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: The largest and most powerful of the chromatic dragons, red dragons are a menace to civilizations everywhere, and their strength is rivaled only by their arrogance.

creatures.name: Rune Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Rune dragons have an innate insight on the power of runes.

creatures.name: Rune Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Rune dragons have an innate insight on the power of runes.

creatures.name: Sard
creatures.description: An immense, gnarled, tree awoken with raw, primal power by one of the fey Eldest of the First World, this monster-one of the legendary Tane-skitters on huge, spidery roots and thrashes its branches as fiery lightning courses within its blackened bark, a living manifestation of the violent clash bet…

creatures.name: Silver Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Silver dragons are among the most chivalrous of all dragonkind; they wield frost and cold as weapons, can walk on clouds, and dwell high upon snowy mountain peaks or deep in steep, misty valleys.

creatures.name: Silver Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Silver dragons are among the most chivalrous of all dragonkind; they wield frost and cold as weapons, can walk on clouds, and dwell high upon snowy mountain peaks or deep in steep, misty valleys.

creatures.name: Star Archon
creatures.description: Embodiments of the virtue of prudence, star archons serve as philosophers and administrators, and in times of war as the tacticians, strategists, and generals of archon armies.

creatures.name: Sturzstromer
creatures.description: Cobble mites that remain a cohesive swarm for many years become dangerous living landslides. Cobble mites resemble squarish stones a few inches across with mouths like split geodes. Though indolent and harmless alone, they're dangerous in large groups.

creatures.name: Taiga Linnorm
creatures.description: Taiga linnorms are covered in hundreds of quill-like black spines as sharp as spears.

creatures.name: Tempest Incarnate
creatures.description: When a sorcerer's blood calls to storm and sky, they can become a frightening force to behold.

creatures.name: Terotricus
creatures.description: The legendary terotricus is a massive slime mold that hails from the Outer Rifts.

creatures.name: Tzitzimitl
creatures.description: Due to their affinity for darkness and apocalyptic terror, tzitzimitls are widely feared as harbingers of death and destruction.

creatures.name: Balor
creatures.description: When folk whisper frightened tales of the demonic, what most envision is the balor-a towering figure of fire and flesh, a horned nightmare armed with a flaming whip and a sword flying through the night in search of its latest victim.

creatures.name: Baomal
creatures.description: Few sea monsters are as dreaded and feared as the two-headed baomal.

creatures.name: Bastion Archon
creatures.description: The mightiest of archon-kind form in the heat of battle from incredibly uncommon circumstances.

creatures.name: Bikkhasura
creatures.description: Asura who have been forcibly reincarnated across countless lifetimes, bikkhasuras have grown to nearly godlike levels of power.

creatures.name: Diabolic Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Hell, according to some theologians, is a living entity in and of itself.

creatures.name: Diabolic Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Hell, according to some theologians, is a living entity in and of itself.

creatures.name: Draconal
creatures.description: Dragon agathions, known as draconals, number among the most powerful of their kin and also the wisest, embodying the wisdom of a benevolent philosopher-king.

creatures.name: Eremite
creatures.description: Creature imported from Foundry PF2e data.

creatures.name: Gold Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Gold dragons are the epitome of metallic dragonkind, unrivaled in their strength as well as their wisdom.

creatures.name: Gold Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Gold dragons are the epitome of metallic dragonkind, unrivaled in their strength as well as their wisdom.

creatures.name: Izfiitar
creatures.description: Occupying the pinnacle of the loose protean caste system, izfiitars enact the ever-shifting plans of the vaunted protean lords and those of the divine Speakers of the Depths.

creatures.name: Kimenhul
creatures.description: Among the strongest of their kind aside from the sahkil tormentors, kimenhuls work their craft to foment despair in those who fear failure, forming cycles of self-loathing.

creatures.name: Maharaja
creatures.description: The most powerful of all rakshasas, maharajas form very rarely, never more than once or twice a century, and only from the reincarnated spirits of other powerful and wicked rakshasas.

creatures.name: Nasurgeth
creatures.description: Nasurgeths are hungry voids with glowing eyestalks and thousands of teeth.

creatures.name: Nessari
creatures.description: When an army of devils invades to bathe a region in bloodshed and hellfire, it is likely that one of the nessaris masterminded the incursion.

creatures.name: Norn
creatures.description: Ancient beyond imagining, norns are powerful fey women who hold in their hands the physical manifestation of fate and destiny in the form of golden thread.

creatures.name: Olethrodaemon
creatures.description: Even daemons fear the most massive and terrifying of their kind, olethrodaemons.

creatures.name: Pleroma
creatures.description: Among the most powerful of all the true aeons, pleromas are the ultimate manifestation of the duality of creation and destruction.

creatures.name: Requiem Dragon (Ancient, Spellcaster)
creatures.description: Requiem dragons are stewards of the River of Souls and the process through which souls reach their final destination in the afterlife.

creatures.name: Requiem Dragon (Ancient)
creatures.description: Requiem dragons are stewards of the River of Souls and the process through which souls reach their final destination in the afterlife.

creatures.name: Tarn Linnorm
creatures.description: Although more powerful linnorms exist, the multi-headed tarn linnorm can wreak an awe-inspiring amount of devastation.

creatures.name: Veranallia
creatures.description: Veranallias represent the freedom of life to grow, change, and adapt.
languages: speak with animals, speak with plants, tongues

creatures.name: Vrolikai
creatures.description: Powerful vrolikais command the untamed armies of demonkind, uniting them behind their one unifying desire for death and destruction.

creatures.name: Yamaraj
creatures.description: The greatest judges among the psychopomps are yamarajes, whose wisdom is legendary and whose edicts are unappealable except to ushers or Pharasma herself.

creatures.name: Elysian Titan
creatures.description: Those titans who remained true to their creators, refusing to participate in their peers' rebellion, earned their freedom while the others were imprisoned.

creatures.name: Grim Reaper
creatures.description: The Grim Reaper is the unflinching personification of death.

creatures.name: Krampus
creatures.description: Krampus is a tall, goatlike humanoid with a pointed nose, swept-back horns, and hooved feet.

creatures.name: Lerritan
creatures.description: Lerritans are unstoppable giants with skin of volcanic glass and blood of roiling lava.

creatures.name: Mu Spore
creatures.description: A mu spore is a thankfully rare fungoid monstrosity of vast power and strange intellect.

creatures.name: Ouroboros
creatures.description: Ouroboros are the embodiment of eternity. Though they're prevalent as a symbol, often scrawled across the margins of alchemical tomes, few know that the beasts actually exist in the Astral Plane, where they fly through the silvery-gray void like a serpentine wheel. The ouroboros is usually depicted…

creatures.name: Ravener
creatures.description: The ravener presented here was once an ancient cinder dragon.

creatures.name: Tor Linnorm
creatures.description: Tor linnorms dwell in the tallest volcanic mountains, either within naturally formed caverns or molten craters.

creatures.name: Thanatotic Titan
creatures.description: Thanatotic titans served as assassins in the war against the deities.

creatures.name: Volnagur
creatures.description: Of all Rovagug's spawn, the creature known as Volnagur is the one that has most distanced itself from the Dead Vault at Golarion's core, instead making its home among the skies.

creatures.name: Danava Titan
creatures.description: Danava titans once regulated the foundational forces that shaped reality.

creatures.name: Jabberwock
creatures.description: Few beasts have inspired as many legends, poems, songs, and myths in as many cultures, as this bizarre draconic creature.

creatures.name: Solar
creatures.description: Solars are among the greatest of all angels, ranking just below empyreal lords.

creatures.name: Green Man
creatures.description: Green men are ancient, enigmatic lesser deities of the primeval forests, living embodiments of nature and plantkind.

creatures.name: Hekatonkheires Titan
creatures.description: The first three hekatonkheires were meant to guard the gates to the Outer Rifts, but they proved too terrifying and rebellious—and so, in disgust, the gods cast them into the gulfs between the planes.

creatures.name: Sorvuth-Ka
creatures.description: Sorvuth-ka is the youngest of Rovagug's spawn.

creatures.name: Treerazer
creatures.description: Treerazer, the self-styled Lord of the Blasted Tarn, is a powerful demon on the cusp of ascending to the true power of one of the rulers of the Abyss itself-a demon lord.
